title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20191014081230
comment: 2nd documented trip through this canyon. Added several bolts to the canyon for the main drops. Clear water, unusual for the glacier water as it is usually milky. Very cold. Checked out the bonus mini-slot down below as well. Great day and gorgeous canyon, albeit short.

title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20200812053328
comment: We scouted out Adams gorge and it’s still flowing WAY too extreme to be safely runnable by my crew.

title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20201019204651
comment: This canyon is short, but burly and every bit of a5. We rappelled in from two spots along the rim to scout the crux from above and below. It is an amazing part of the slot where the creek narrows, dramatically curves, and drops quickly, forming a chain of three incredible and intimidating waterfalls. The creek was running higher than we originally expected and the first two potholes were pure whitewater. We spent time scouting and planning the best approach before dropping in. We entered higher than the R1 drop-in and found ourselves stranded above the R1 falls without an anchor. Oops. We escaped up a steep slope DCR and rappelled back into the creek at the next drop below ‘chockstone falls’. The creek was pushy but not too eventful until the crux rappel. There were advanced swiftwater challenges as well as rope management challenges from linking the three pools that we had to manage through. Anchors were in great condition. There is a nasty siphon about 20 yards downstream from the last pool of R3 where the entire creek pinches and dives underneath a boulder wedged in a narrow slot (see pic attached). Avoid the siphon by climbing out of the previous pool DCL and stemming over the slot. Beta has been updated. All in all, it was a full value day in a short slice of Adams Creek!

title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20210926014426
comment: Canyon was great. Flows were a bit high but manageable. Flows also rose throughout the day so an early start is a good idea. Sieve area is easier as a log has jammed into it and you can walk by it. Trust zero areas that seem like jumpers. Just below the “boiler room” is a pool that has been filled in with a recent rock fall. All bolts are great.

title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20211026173411
comment: Great day in Adams. Even at moderate flow it would be challenging for some folks. The first pothole swim can be overwhelming and it would be useful to have an emergency line ready for folks. Bolts and anchors are great. We pulled the rope for the boiler room while in the second pothole and used the large boulder to anchor off of for the last 10 foot rap. Do not jump these as they are very shallow and filled with jagged boulders. We did do the last slotty section further down stream. This could be ran multiple times and great for beginners to feel hydraulics in a safe manner.

title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20220925155410
comment: Ran Adams. R2 has lost a log and the chockstone moves with ease. We need to either create a traverse to the anchor station or come up with a new anchor station. All bolts were solid. Added a stray log to help with the strainer.

title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20230906195332
comment: The chockstone now has a fracture and could give way at any point. The rap from this feature needs serious attention. Aka DO NOT CASUALLY ROLL UP AND ATTEMPT. we placed a bolt DCR at the chockstone but one more needs set further down. We escaped after this feature as a team member was above for such an event.

title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20230918040211
comment: Flow the previous night was Very High. Flow in the morning was High. First we descended a fixed line to the bottom of R2 to assess. Rappeling on DCR was not possible - you would get swept off your feet and have a fast and deep descent into the boil. DCL was a better line, you could stay away from the firehose, stand on a ledge near the surface, jump halfway across the boil, and likely get propelled to the pourover for a safe exit. The boil was very deep. Ascended and went down to look at R3. There is a scramble down just before the canyon makes a 90 degree right hand turn. First tier looked reasonable on DCR; 2nd tier also DCR probably fine but not visible. After 3rd tier there was what appeared to be a new strainer. A fresh log. All water was funneled DCL into a 2 foot wide laminar blast into the log's root ball. Very dangerous, potentially fatal, strainer. You would be on rope to complicate matters. We opted to skip R3 and head back to do R2. R1 was not an issue. The drop in canyon was into a boil in shallow water. Very cold. The Ralstone at R2 was precariously wobbly, more than we expected. Due to the roiling boil, we decided ziplining packs and any rope bags was a must. After gingerly testing the Ralstone, we were spooked and decided to live another day. Did not want it to land on top of someone when it fails, or get pinched like Aaron Ralson in 127 hours. You would not get 127 hours here, you would have about 60 seconds in the cold flow. If the flow was moderate, there may be the possibility to rappel from DCR and avoid the chockstone hazard and also get off rappel in a favorable outwash direction, as the circulation is CCW. R3 would also much more reasonable to attempt at half the flow. Need cooler temps in October, even in this very low year.

title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20250914033734
comment: We re-equipped the middle section of this canyon that other users had identified as a problem. R1 is the same. R2 has now been split into 2 rappels. We utilized the bolt that had previously been placed DCR, to place a higher bolt, well out of the flow, that leads into a 10 ft in length. This avoids any contact with the “Ralstone” which is still wedged in the slot, very unstable. A brand new station has been placed DCL for the previous R2 (which is now R3). This station is in line with the previous bolts (DCL), with a solid stance for the anchor manager, and allows for line-of-sight for the entirety of the rappel. The original DCL, R2 bolts have been removed. Between this feature, and the original R3, there is nothing to report. We did not go into the boiler room due to increasing flows. The bolts are in good shape at this station. After a run through to re-equip, we ran the canyon until the final sequence. All is ready to go once flows are a touch lower. Future parties could consider placing a bolt for the R1 drop, and begin the canyon 200 feet higher to spend more time in this beautiful slot.

title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20250928045759
comment: We did not run the canyon. The creek was observed to be extremely low the day before the attempt. We saw it on the previous day at 10:30. By the time we showed up the following day at 8 AM the flow had more than quadrupled. Thinking that we could probably deal with that flow. We suited it up and hike down and watch the creek continue to rise. By the time we made it back to the car, we saw the flow more than doubled in three hours. If you are going to attempt this Canyon, it’s best to observe it from the bridge and when you know, temperatures are going to be very low. Probably best to make it your back up and check it before you run or whatever you were going to run initially.

title: Conditions:Adams Creek Gorge-20251010213835
comment: Sent Adams. Added a bolt DCR to break up the rap so that you can make it to the bottom with 50… need to scramble left to make that work. New bolts at R2 are nice. Log in pool was firm and used it to get to new station DCL. Log in the bottom of R2 wasn’t in play. Boiler room lived up to its name. The second pool was filled with breakdown 3 years ago but that all seemed to clear. Added bolt DCL for last pool entry. Last pool is not deep. The pour over is real. It was surging while we were in there. Sieve at the end is still there and we added a log above to assist. Fun day. Even these flows were pushy so if the level at the bridge is anymore than what you see in the picture then maybe take a pass. In canyon time was 2.25 hours. Only brought one pack and 33 meter ropes.

title: Conditions:Big Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20201005031154
comment: Took the "shortcut route" in. New logjam has appeared not too far inside the entrance. Might be able to downclimb underneath it somehow, but was easier to just rappel off a log and drop back in. Fun to float along in the current through a beautiful narrows. Nice chaser just after running the Middle Fork just upstream.

title: Conditions:Big Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20230623041736
comment: The log jam has shifted a bit and the rappel is now optional. There is a rock shelf DCR you can step down to and down climb into the creek, or a deep pool you can make an approx. 10ft jump into DCL. Water is cold and be mindful of being polite to the campsites you pass on the creek walk out. Also saw an otter on the logjam after swimming downstream a bit. He apparently thought my jump looked fun and followed suit before climbing back up and over. Good reminder we share the canyons and to be aware and respectful of wildlife.

title: Conditions:Big Huckleberry Creek-20210509030437
comment: Likely first descent of Big Huckleberry as no other anchors were found. Had somewhat low expectations dropping in, but turned out to be a quite lovely creek. Not destined to be one of the all-time classics, but enjoyable. Water levels were were great (but cold)... guessing we hit it in flood stage. Water levels likely drop off significantly heading into summer, but the creek was clear enough to suggest it runs all year. Might be worth checking out the east tributary as it had equivalent flow to the main fork.

title: Conditions:Big Huckleberry Creek-20211115212001
comment: Flow was strong and made creek walking a challenge. Below the top confluence we avoided some narrow spots due to the flow. The upper drop, at the confluence, on the north side was not runnable due to the flow however the south one was perfect. Rappels were great and anchors offer options to be in the water but out of the main flow.

title: Conditions:Big Huckleberry Creek-20240505175556
comment: Water was a little lower than expected with all the rain but it was at a nice level. All anchors are in good shape. There is a bit of a creek walk out but there are some decent little slides and pools along the way. Added a sling to a log across the creek DCL for the last little drop.

title: Conditions:Big Huckleberry Creek-20260118044311
comment: Lovely, cold day in Big Huck. We replaced the anchor for R2. R3 was a bit spicy as we were right in the flow and it was ice cream headache level cold! The road up to the top was bumpy but clear. Unfortunately, when we tried to return with the shuttle someone had a cut a large tree that fully blocked the road four miles from the top! We waited for two hours until help arrived with a saw. Our poor driver hiked the four miles up while the tree was cut. Beware of idiots with chain saws!

title: Conditions:Boetzke Canyon-20190831212313
comment: First known descent. Accessed by bike. Decent spot to lock up bikes near start of bushwhack. Water wasn't too cold, and no deep pools. Done with all natural anchors. Don't even think about bringing a bolt kit - it won't help you. Rock is absolute choss.

title: Conditions:Boetzke Canyon-20190913015758
comment: Great day. Did the canyon solo for work. Quite the schlep carrying 600 ft of rope/pull cord just to do the last rap (in addition to all the equipment I needed). Water isn't cold, but it was kind of windy in the canyon. Was comfortable in a 3mm shortie and a rain jacket while moving, but cold while standing around for long periods of time. Removed two things of cord/maelon - gotta love that fiddlestick! I think there's only 3 raps left with tat on them. Would be a fun challenge to try to ghost those drops - might be doable, but I didn't want to push it solo.

title: Conditions:Boetzke Canyon-20210627211358
comment: It was a warm weekday afternoon. We biked from Mt. Washington trailhead and locked the bikes to the bridge. Followed the existing approach coordinates for the most part. Just the approach took us 2h. It is steep bushwhacking, most use hands and feet often. We got lots of scratches. Once we got to the canyon, it was fun. All rappels are back to back. Replaced the existing anchors with new webbing. If it wasn't for the approach this canyon would be a whole a lot more fun.

title: Conditions:Bottleneck Canyon-20210719054643
comment: We totally missed the trail up. We started to the left of where we parked our cars, and I think the faint trail is even farther left. Brutal hike up, wearing our wetsuits after doing clark. We hiked up per GIA, but we ended up crawling back down the creek 200 ft lower in elevation before the first Rap. At this flow, we did not need the R4 traverse line to access the bolt.

title: Conditions:Bottleneck Canyon-20220725013923
comment: Enjoyable little outing - fun weekend combo with Clark Creek next door. Didn't bother looking for a route, but thrashed up wherever was easiest then followed the "shortcut route" into the creek, dropping in just above the first rappel. As bushwhacks go in the Olympics, it's pretty easy. Probably a 2 out of 5 (where 1 is open forest and 5 is "I can't see my feet"). Re-rigged goofy anchor DCL on R1. Traverse line was unnecessary at current flow. Could have used a little more water. :-)

title: Conditions:Bozoo Creek-20190204174949
comment: We ran Bozoo again as part of our Freeze Fest. Air temps were in the upper 40s when we got to the starting point around 9:30 AM. There was minimal packed snow on the ground so a passenger car could likely make it. We decided to hike directly up to about ~4000ft instead of taking the overgrown road and making the switch back since the road was covered in about 2 feet of snow. The post holing would have drained our energy/slowed us down so we proceeded directly up the mountain. The post holing when cutting across to our drop in around 4000 was terrible. Waist deep postholing, stick close to your team so you can help them out of the holes. The canyon packs in a lot of rappels if you're crazy enough to run canyons in these conditions. The cold will catch up to you by the last 3-4 drops and these are some rappels where you are completely in the water. Dry suit with rain jacket + hood highly recommended also we had several of our team where an insane amount of neoprene. Freezefest II complete.

title: Conditions:Budweiser Creek-20170627014132
comment: Meh.

title: Conditions:Cabin Creek (Olympics)-20250808153538
comment: Entry into the canyon required two rappels. (~100ft, ~190ft) Anchor retrieval required consideration on several of the rappels. False floors would present a risk at higher flow.

title: Conditions:Cabin Creek (Olympics)-20251016171609
comment: Great day to get a last run in on this canyon. Put in webbing on trees for the entrance rappels. Jumps are all in and no issues.

title: Conditions:Cabin Creek (Olympics)-20260607025529
comment: Flow lower than we anticapated. Neat bedrock in the upper section

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20160502061130
comment: The water was cold, but it was a warm, sunny day. We had a relaxing few hours in the creek and also cleaned up some of the anchors. This canyon could be best enjoyed by advanced canyoners just a little earlier in the season, or perhaps after a rain.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20160621214728
comment: We ran this canyon after it had rained continuously for the past 2 days. Flows were moderate high but most of the rappels are out of the water/lower flow section of the waterfalls. Great canyon to take beginners. Those more advanced may want to adding anchors that will set the rappel line directly in the water course for more fun. The last rappel (going through the chute) is fun!

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20170703230731
comment: The approach route is still flagged at the beginning and is well-flagged at the end, but the trail is hard to follow in some places in between, we definitely lost some time to route-finding. Flow level was good, most rappel anchors are currently set for dry or nearly-dry rappels; in retrospect it would've been more fun to re-set them to go down the watercourse. Very little debris/brush in the canyon, mostly easy (albeit slippery) walking between drops.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20170724055016
comment: We chose this creek for the purpose of playing around with rigging systems and practicing new skills, so we took our time. We also definitely lost 45 minutes to route finding in the beginning. The creek was running enough to be fun but not at all intimidating. Definitely a good beginner waterfall canyon, though the slippery blocky rappels were a bit heady for the climbers in our group who are used to dry rock and would be quite heady for someone new to rappelling and rope management. Still, everyone had a lot of fun, tried out some new things and got to gain experience in a pretty benign canyoning environment.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20200302175616
comment: The new Trail up is in great condition. Checked all webbing and did not need to replace any. Placing bolts in the future would improve safety and rappel routes. Anchors were easy to locate. We ran the canyon with 3 rope teams, constantly moving ahead to set the next rappel. Hike up with a pack will test your legs but the scenery and quick succession of rappels made it worth while. Rocks not that slippery compared to Hall creek next canyon over.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20200420021041
comment: The newer trail up is in great shape and dead ends at first rappel. Brushed some of the branches out on the way down, for easier pulls. This canyon could use the benefit of a couple bolts. Beta is good, water is cold. There is no swims, waste deep at best, and a couple of good shower style rappels, with options to stay dryer on more slick rocks. Third trip this year :)

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20201102043042
comment: I was lured by the "58 and sunny" forecast and envisioned myself rappelling in moderate-high flow with sunshine greeting me at the bottom of each drop... but if I had used my head I would have realized that of course a north facing creek doesn't get a single minute of sun on November 1. Definitely a dry suit day and two of us were in wetsuits. We stayed just above shivering but only by choosing the dry lines. The water was very cold. Still, it was nice to get out canyoning. I'm skeptical that the stream gauges mean much for flow indication in this creek, so I'll share the recent weather patterns: In the middle two weeks of October, North Bend got something like 7" of rain. The water tables were definitely saturated. This past week has been dry and sunny, with the exception of 1/4" rain early Friday morning. With a 1 mile catchment, the flows drop quickly - just a week of dry weather and it was a solid moderate flow. Change creek is really improving thanks to the efforts of a handful of folks in the community. Great to see well-placed bolts taking the place of some natural anchors that were causing quite a bit of erosion.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20201102230321
comment: First time down Change Creek for the group. Started hiking up at ~10am; fully geared-up at the top by 11am; and back at the car for 2pm. We spotted the reference point from the path (and even had cell signal to compare it to ropewiki). We had fun getting pounded in the (cold) flow, but it wasn't a problematic volume, nor was it unavoidable if desired. Probably at the limit of an average wet suit, I was glad to have a dry suit on. Despite a fun & fast day we still had some rope grief. The rope wouldn't pull on R2, so had to be ascended and re-rigged with less friction. Then on R3 we core-shot the rope on the sharp lip at the top, so we "creeped" the ropes from then on. An all round great (half) day out!

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20210505144931
comment: The trail is in good condition, well marked. It had rained hard the night before so the creek was full at the parking lot. Not a real issue because most rappels have the option to go to the side of the flow. Stuck a rope on the large rappel on the multi stage, but were able to release it because there was a rope going down the dry side and folks still up top to give it a yank. Really like this canyon for beginners

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20210625203100
comment: good, friendly flow. Note: R2 does not need webbing, and the bolts are best left unlinked. While it's out of the main flow, it's not out of the path of potential winter flood damage. If a tree limb gets caught on webbing left behind, it will destroy these bolts. Please leave them unlinked. The rope pulls just fine if you back away from the base of the rappel for a good pull angle.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20210816060319
comment: Good conditions. Even though water is low, it still made a fun outing. 3 of us had 3mm wetsuits on, while 2 people did not wear a wetsuit.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20220625051947
comment: Fun flow right now! There were huge floods in Change Creek this winter/spring and a lot of new logs and debris in the canyon. We spent time cleaning up and sawing away the most annoying stuff. R1 anchor was buried under 4” of gravel. R3 flow has been completely diverted to DCL with no flow at the rootball anchor, due to new tree fall that caused gravel to build up DCR. R7 bolts were buried by a landslide and fallen trees. We excavated them but we do NOT recommend using them as after we moved on we saw rockfall coming down right where the bolts were — use the DCR tree instead and link R7 and R8. We cleared the approach trail of overgrowth, checked and fixed up all of the anchors, and the canyon’s good to go.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20220811013424
comment: Water levels were on the low side, but we had a great time taking someone very new to canyoning through the creek. Anchors were in great condition (thanks, Becca). Compared to last year, there's been some upheaval with tree and rock falls in the lower part of the canyon. The approach is also a bit more of a slog than it was in the past, thanks to giant fallen trees. We were grateful for the flagging a couple of times where the trail was hard to spot. At R8 the bolt station is very exposed. We opted to rig a traverse line from a log instead of multi-staging from the R7 anchors.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20230517035812
comment: Great day in the canyon. High flow was very fun.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20230806173449
comment: Figured I’d check out flow on a rainy day. Was still low, but not very low as it would’ve been otherwise. Probably not worth doing aside from beginner trips until it rains more. Quick afternoon trip, all anchors in good condition. Very slippery

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20231012235206
comment: Fun day in low flow conditions introducing some new people to the sport! As other reports mention, there are a lot of downed trees on both the hike in and the creek walk out.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20240509033803
comment: Fun day with mostly clear skies and good flow. Hiked directly up canyon right (UCR). Avoided water on R5. Fun with the water flowing.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20240509033821
comment: Rap 1 rappeller’s left bolt spins a little. Placed webbing DCR prior to rap 3 bolts, otherwise all anchors in good shape.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20240528045625
comment: More water than we thought there would be. Had to bypass one rappel that woulda been dangerous due to flow levels. Great conditions to play in the water.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20240724044051
comment: Nice place on a hot day. All anchors seemed to be in good condition. Flow was low but still fun. Attempted to tighten the spinner on the last rappel to no avail.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20250313115305
comment: Flow was fun, things were slightly less slippery than usual. Anchors were in good/useable condition although the webbing for R1 should be replaced soon.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20250527232621
comment: Fun day with descent flow in nice weather. First time in this canyon, anchors in good shape but seems some are missing, maybe a local can update beta. Quite slippery but sounds like that's normal for this canyon. We used this as a training op for newer folks. R1: did not see boulder webbing, used the traverse line bolts and the deviation. R3: did not see Option 2 webbing, rigged a dry line off a tree DCL for one person but removed it after. R5: both anchors in good shape, pushed a log off that was in the rap line for the first 15 ft. R6: has really sharp rock on both anchors. If using DCR anchor recommend going off the rock ledge further DCR. Might be nice to find a better anchor location.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20250623214226
comment: Practice rigging day for a very inexperienced group trying to shake off some rust after an off-season. All anchors in great shape except: R1 webbing in good shape, quicklink may need replacement soon (surface rust with some small pit Several bolted anchors could use a little TLC with hammer/wrenching (a handfull of spinners on a single bolt of a pair. no obvious rust or concerns.)

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20260205050116
comment: Couldn’t find a flagged trail at all. The landscape forced us down into the creek at the bottom of the big drop. Bit of a bummer because it was RIPPING! R6 was a blast. At this flow definitely stay on rappel between R7 and R8. Definitely need to return with someone who knows the approach trail as the lines on CalTopo weren’t helpful after leaving the Mt Washington climbers trail.

title: Conditions:Change Creek-20260518044041
comment: 2 hrs on the approach, 4 hrs in canyon. There has to be a better way of approach. There’s no "trail", flagged or otherwise, where the kml lists it. Slopes are covered in deadfall, soil has been significantly weakened over the winter and cliff bands force one to constantly switch between hiking down towards the creek and scrambling up away from the drop in. People who care about this as a training venue need to do some serious work reestablishing the “alternate flagged trail”. I'm claiming "special challenges" based solely on the approach. Glad I came back to finish it in its entirety, most rappels are delightful albeit very slippery. The pool between R7 and R8 is now full of wood. All pre existing anchors are unharmed. We left two webbing anchors up creek from the established drop in as that’s where our “trail floundering” dropped us in and we needed to do some nuisance raps. Could we have ghosted them, sure, but I opted for guaranteed clean pulls. Great flow nonetheless.

title: Conditions:Chikamin Narrows-20250714193220
comment: No other anchors were found, so possible first descent. Dropped in upstream of the narrows to catch several rappels. R2 is excellent, dropping into a neat grotto. Would be fun to find a line to run the flow. The remainder are mostly boulder problems, requiring the odd nuisance rappel to get down. Suddenly, Chikamin narrows up with walls rising vertically 60-80ft on both sides. Gorgeous narrows and very photogenic, but not very technical. Needs more jumps and swimming! Easy thrash back up to the road.

title: Conditions:Clark Creek-20210706025038
comment: A great day with a great team running the 2nd descent of this canyon to complete the final few rappels in the canyon that we bypassed the first time around due to high water flow and a late day. Water was much more manageable and we were able to rappel multiple features that we bypassed the first time.

title: Conditions:Clark Creek-20210719053643
comment: Another great canyon. Hike up could be flagged but able to follow track from Jakes trip. Steep hike up, but quick to put in. R4 access and transfer over to the rappel is not easy. Very slipper and errors will hurt you. I sat on station for all 6 folks, making sure transfer went well. My hand saw fell off my belt near R2, if you find one at lower levels. We were able to combine this with Bottleneck canyon

title: Conditions:Clark Creek-20220718225221
comment: Great day! Conditions looked just slighlty lower than the moderate water reference picture, which is a great flow for beginners. R4 at the hanging belay is now unlinked - there was a Y-hang that used to be at R4, but we removed it since the webbing was rubbing pretty badly on the rock. Very important that next team in can manage the traverse and unlinked bolts properly. Next team in should definitely bring a wrench. The R4 nut and hanger on the right was loose (bolt still good) and only hand-tightened (we forgot a wrench), but the left bolt/hanger was solid. There are small, 5mm quicklinks from the Y-hang left on the hanger; it would be great if the next team could switch those out for larger 8mm quicklinks (sorry, we couldn't loosen it without a wrench). We could not find R7, assumed tree root may have slipped down due to erosion. But the webbing that was added on 7/17/2021 was fine. Please note, going that route is tiered, so be sure to continue down to the second tier as that second tier is not bolted.

title: Conditions:Clark Creek-20220725010528
comment: Followed the faint trail up to intersect the road and dropped in below R0. Some very enjoyable rappels and cool water features (ex: climbing down though the siphon and the Rooster Tail - a human-sized water spout). We took our time and had fun. We didn't see the anchor and accidentally downclimbed R3. Ooops. I wasn't fond of the anchor station at R4 -- it's awkward and management feels unnecessarily complicated. (Were I to run it again, I'd ponder finding a better anchor location higher up.) We were planning to replace the quick links on R4 with larger ones (i.e. turn it into an unlinked station), but the bolts are positioned for a Y-hang, so we ultimately decided to re-establish the webbing. The anchor is not subject to flood events and abrasion didn't seem to be an issue in its new position. Folks running the creek currently should *definitely* know how to rig retrievable safety lines and be comfortable managing a hanging rappel station.

title: Conditions:Clark Creek-20240903201903
comment: I don't recommend this one. Its a really long drive on a bad road. I didn't like the sloping rappels. The hanging anchor position is not good and makes it time consuming and overly complicated.

title: Conditions:Cora Creek-20230723232151
comment: No anchors were found, so possible first descent. Easy shortie creek; nothing too remarkable. R2 is the real reason to run this one where the creek plunges into a 80ft slot. Would have been more fun with some more water. Probably better earlier in the season.

title: Conditions:Cora Creek-20240206045045
comment: Visiting from Utah, I decided to do the old hike up to Cora Lake that I used to do as a kid. I brought my rope with me as even when I was little I thought it would be fun to descend through the creek. I figured the creek was pretty mild and would be a good transition from the desert canyons I am used to. I hiked up, swam around in the lake for a while and then headed down the creek. There are plenty of trees that can be slung for anchors so I didn't leave any gear. Though I did almost stick my rope on the log in Rap 2.

title: Conditions:Cortright Creek-20250907231320
comment: Cortright turned out to be a delightful descent with neat pools, cool geology, and much luscious green moss. It would probably be run all the time if there was a nice exit after the final rappel. (Would probably give it another star.) The exit down Cool Creek is a nice forested canyon, but is more of a creek walk / float and somewhat lacking in technical interest. Recommend swiftwater training as there's strong current in places. We saw a number of spawning salmon.

title: Conditions:Covell Creek (Main Fork)-20210503041547
comment: Possible first descent of the main fork on Covell Creek as no anchors or signs of previous passage were found. Access is excellent and this opens more options in the Covell complex. Anchors are a little thin in the upper main fork; the lack of significant vegetation along the sides indicates that this creek can really rip from time to time. Main fork joins the west fork below the confluence, so we enjoyed the narrows and rappelling Covell Creek Falls. If you have time, it's fun to continue down-canyon and hit some of the smaller drops too.

title: Conditions:Covell Creek (West Fork)-20180102032835
comment: The rappels in this canyon are incredibly fun! Keep in mind that the flow will double once you get to R3. R3 is pushy in high flow. After R4, we hiked out on the trail. The overall time of 5 hours includes route finding, finding and building anchors. I wore about 8 mm in the core and was decently warm. However, other members in our group wore 5 mm and no gloves(!) and were fine.

title: Conditions:Covell Creek (West Fork)-20210307032313
comment: Ran the creek. Dropped in higher than last time and it wasn’t worth it. Built new anchor DCL for R2 which makes it just shy of 200”

title: Conditions:Covell Creek (West Fork)-20210404150215
comment: Flow was great today, but very cold per coming right out of the snowpack. Anchor for the multi-pitch at R2 is in a somewhat awkward location. Next time need to look at breaking up R1 into two shorter rappels. This would get you down to a nice platform right at the top of the big falls which would probably be a better place to stage a group without tearing up the slope. The big falls was a joy to rappel. The lower narrows and exit rappel also were great. Found a sign just adjacent to Covell Falls requesting hikers to please stay on the trail, so next time we need to stay in the watercourse and continue down-canyon instead (will add +2 short rappels that looked fun). Lots of hikers about. We were informed that we would be going on someone's facebook page for sure. :-) A great little canyon!

title: Conditions:Covell Creek (West Fork)-20210516152622
comment: Dropped in high, has potential for slide rappels. Also has high potential for very public moss scraping on scenic touristy waterfalls. One and done.

title: Conditions:Covell Creek (West Fork)-20230226153041
comment: Big falls was completely frozen over. All anchors good. Lots of ice. Was plan C for the day. Old flagging on exit is now a full trail back to the parking area.

title: Conditions:Covell Creek (West Fork)-20230529165241
comment: Running at low now. Note there's a rigging for an R0 access rap DCR just after the suit up area. Don't confuse that for R1. We also dislodged a very large rock in the middle of Angel Falls. Luckily people and ropes were out of the way. BC-R2 (our final rap) was the highlight.

title: Conditions:Covell Creek (West Fork)-20240707191835
comment: Canyon terrain seems drastically different from the last reported trip. Landslide/blowdowns have clogged the canyon with debris. Handful of anchors needed to be rebuilt. Specifically, the R2 Option B (rootball) anchor has been washed away and the surrounding terrain didn’t provide for an alternative anchor placement, so we opted for Option A DCL. The fallen log anchor for BC-R3 has snapped and is no longer a viable anchor point, so we opted to bypass the final two rappels and exited the canyon directly after Covell Creek Falls. We were able to walk the rim of the canyon on the exit trail and peer into the remainder of the canyon (from BC-R3 through the creek walk exit) and the creek was filled with debris and log jams that would have easily added another hour to our exit if we had not exited at the bottom of Covell Creek Falls. Overall, a fun and interesting day of on-site problem solving but definitely exhausting having to navigate a canyon that felt log jammed start to finish.

title: Conditions:Dalles Creek-20150518054053
comment: Scouting trip; low-moderate flow; very low snowpack year, most of the snow in the drainage area has melted

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20170731051404
comment: Surprisingly awesome little canyon! Definitely could be dangerous in higher water per hydraulics. Flow was just about perfect today.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20190721130804
comment: Ran Dark and added a bolt on R4 to help with the lack of natural anchors. Fun creek and was at moderate flow. Need to go back with a bit more flor in the future.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20190730052557
comment: A very pleasant canyon with a short rapid-fire technical section. Added a backup bolt to the single bolted anchor. Moderate low flow. A very nice time.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20190815072817
comment: The approach is on an OHV trail, heads up for dirt bikes. Flow was on the low side but still fun. Great rock formations throughout.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20190826031459
comment: Returning to Dark Creek after a couple of years and it doesn't disappoint. A fabulous creek in the Dark Divide. Water colder than I remember; we're suspicious it must be spring-fed. Once out of the water, you'd warm up relatively quickly. Stuck a rope on the bolted anchor that we were able to free with a great deal of hauling and thrashing the rope from below. Might be good to extend this anchor so the master point hangs in space to keep this from happening in future. Great rappel.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20200817022251
comment: Always a good time on Dark Creek. Reasonable approach/exit, minimal bushwhacking, and some quality rappels. A must-do if you're in the Dark Divide.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20210802023650
comment: There's a new log perched precariously in the middle of the pitch on R8 -- just above the funnel chamber. Looks like a rappeller accidentally bumping into it could knock it down. We used caution and detoured around it... the rope will run clean underneath it. On the same pitch, we discovered a doubled 200ft rope will just reach the bottom. Extending the pull side may be recommended for a clean pull in a better location just downstream of the slot. All anchors in good shape.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20220718151456
comment: Dark was awesome! I wouldn't have wanted to run it at any higher flow. We couldn't find a lot of the anchors and the ones that we found were "baconed" so we built or rebuilt most of them. R7 hanging pothole was not a problem. The flow was going mostly over and beside the pothole. R8 rappelled out of the flow into the slot room. Very splashy and no hydraulic. Easy to swim into and out of the area. R8 wouldn't pull. We had to ascend back up to check it. It looked fine, block was on top and it wasn't caught on anything. I rerouted the pull over the log (above the slot room) and we pulled from far back. Ended up building a 3:1 to get it to pull. Extremely fun creek at this flow!

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20220731065019
comment: Another great day in Dark. Flow was perfect. A lot of fallen logs that were present last time I was here have been blown out, so the rappels are all clear and good to go. There have been endless reports of people sticking ropes on R8, so we re-rigged the bolts as a Y-hang anchor. Rope pulled like a dream. Be aware: the log on R8 is now about 10ft lower wedged between walls of the chasm. If you run the flow, there's a great line all the way down into the "Dark Room" at the bottom although you have to slide under the log. Played around doing some jumps on the very last drop in the canyon. Ran East Canyon Creek as a chaser.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20220802033345
comment: What a beautiful canyon. I was pleasantly suprised at the bang for your buck on this one. Approach is 3 miles but on all established trails. I want to know who did this approach in 1 hr (STUDS!) More flow would have been better but just on the edge of moderate flow and still super fun. Closing in on one of the PNW classics if you ask me!!!

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20220919144523
comment: We did a low-flow run of Dark and it was happily more than the trickle we expected (slightly more). We found it to be great for canyoneers who are nervous about water but want to gain experience rappelling in the flow. It’s a beautiful canyon. We removed the webbing from the rotten wobbly log at the top of R7. We had intended to bolt it (per requests in the beta) but found the rock quality to be insufficient for bolting. We built a cairn anchor and used that. Unless someone else is able to find suitable rock that we didn’t find, it may be that a cairn anchor is the long-term solution here, as there is ample mateial.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20230724190622
comment: Fun day on Dark. Performed anchor maintenance as required. The last anchor should be replaced. We should have done it but by the time I got there, most of the team was already down.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20230731173812
comment: First time actually seeing motorbikes at the trailhead; they took off in front of us and we never saw them again. Flow low to moderate-low, no issues, all anchors in good shape. Surprisingly nice cairn at R7. Current line takes you right down a rock rib between the main part of the waterfall and pothole to rappeller's right. (Going in the pothole is fun at this level.) The funnel rappel at R8 has a log spanning the canyon from one side to the other which is not visible from the rappel station. If following the watercourse, you'll need to squeak underneath it. Best to extend your rope on this anchor for the pull and toss the pull side over the log. A large number of mountain bikers showed up at the trailhead just after we got out and were picked up by a shuttle. Looked like a guide service.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20230814233325
comment: Dark Creek was good, but the flow is pretty wimpy. Anchors were in good shape.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20230907192646
comment: Surprisingly fun canyon with some really scenic rappels, although flow was very low.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20240715201046
comment: Fun day in Dark. Rebuilt anchors as required. There is a new log jammed under the old log on R7 which is stabilizing it or at least it doesn't bounce anymore. The carin anchor is gone so we added one to the log. We did not run the flow in R8. It looked way to powerful!

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20240722154109
comment: We ran Dark both last weekend and this weekend. The flow is really dropping. Get in there soon!

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20250803040556
comment: Great day in the creek! Splashy but not pushy. All were surprised at how cold the water was, and grateful for the frequent sunny areas to warm up in. All anchors had relatively new webbing. R7 is a "cairn" anchor of a small dishwasher size boulder with long webbing; bouncy, but good & solid.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20260526152815
comment: R1 looked just like the "high" reference photo from Jun 2023, which tracks with 2026 being a month ahead of "usual". The trail is in great condition. At R7 the cairn/threaded anchor has gone, but the log spanning the creek is stable and provides a good anchor. A bolt was damaged at R8, we took the opportunity to repurpose the remaining bolt as a traverse line, and commissioned a new rappel station on a ledge further DCL with good line of sight of the drop. The bottom of R8 was rowdy but was easy to get flushed out with a good jump across. Ran East afterwards.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20260620001018
comment: Fun day in Dark. All anchors in good shape except R8. New anchor station DCL for last pitch of Dark Creek Falls was used and ruins the rappel. I recommend not using it. If the next team has the capability recommend placing additional bolt at the original anchor or back up the single for all but the last person and rappel in the flow. You can rappel the dry (high flow line) from the original anchor without problem. I think the new station is a case of unnecessary over-bolting. It overcomplicates the drop with a traverse line, makes the rappel dry, and runs the rope over several sharp edges. The benefit it provides of better line of sight can be achieved in other ways. The new station was placed 3 weeks ago and already failed as one lose bolt wouldn't tighten and popped out with barely loading it with my personal pro. I put the bolt in my vice to loosen the nut and get the hanger off signaling the threads were damaged possibly from over-tightening.

title: Conditions:Dark Creek-20260702012944
comment: We ran the canyon replacing all mallion anchors with rings. We established the cairn anchor after getting freaked by the flex of the log. Feel free to rig the log but it gave us bad vibes. A future party could bring in more webbing to lengthen the cairn anchor. We then replaced the lower rebelay above the Dark Room which resolved the incident of the bolt pulling out in the previous trip report.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20141014063220
comment: Great canyon. It rained a lot the previous day but the water levels were pretty much the same.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20150828164205
comment: Water flow was moderate low and the water was chilly. The canyon is likely one of the most beautiful canyons in Washington and it's a must run! We went with a film crew so it took us a little longer than normal. Anchors are in good condition but webbing may need to be replaced in the next few months.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20150831135339
comment: Despite the very dry Summer, we went in during the first weekend rainstorm (50% chance of rain during the time we were actually in the canyon). The canyons in the PNW typically do not have a high chance of flashing since the ground is absorbent. The gauge USGS:14231900 was reading at 110% when we arrived (it had been raining in the entire night/ morning) so we decided to give it a go. We were lucky and it only rained briefly (5 minutes) on us throughout the canyon and we even got some blue sky patches! The increased flow is more noticeable and our group was cautious about proceeding to the slotty area. The first two-tiered rappel (R3) we were able to completely avoid by rappelling to the side/around. With this flow, rappelling in the water is not advised - there is a square-looking crack where canyoneers should be careful not to get stuck in! In low flow, rappelling into the crack will not be a problem. For R4, we went over the log instead due avoid the high water. For R5, care must be taken when rappelling into the pothole. You can rappel 1/2 way down and slide down the rest. The current will spit you out but have a line ready to throw / throw down the pull slide ahead of time to help others swim out. R7 is the rappel you see from the bridge. Is the by far the strongest hydraulic of all the other rappels. It is difficult to set rope length due to the amount of white water but still do your best. It is advisable to have people lock off about 1/2 way down (where you can stay to the side of the water course), pull up the rope to ensure there are no knots/rope is long enough, before entering in the lower 1/2 of the waterfall.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20160624183322
comment: This canyon is flowing at extreme levels and should only be run is very, very competent Class C canyoneers. At least two people need to be familiar with reading currents, escaping very powerful swirling potholes, setting up guide lines, and have throw ropes ready. Do not underestimate the power of the water in this flow. The canyon has changed significantly from the 2015 season - log anchors are gone, and other logs have accumulated in other places, some rappels are now jumps, footholds are buried 2 feet below the water. The 80' waterfall underneath the bridge is very dangerous due to the slippery rock and risk of slipping/falling and setting up a meat guided rappel is highly recommended.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20160705215258
comment: Canyon is flowing about the same flow about a week ago. We experimented with different hydraulic avoidance techniques now that we didn't have Thor with us. For swirling pothole #1, we used a floating anchor to zipline the packs down. The first person still went into the hydraulic unaided. For swirling pothole #2, we used a potshot to setup a guided rappel and then switched to a meat guided rappel afterwards. For the 80' waterfall, the first person down rappeled with the rope bag in order to deploy the guide line. First person still had to cross the waterfall, toss the rope bag, and then jump about 20 feet (away from hydraulic). Meat guided rappel worked perfect and should always be done in this type of flow. Many expert canyoneers have slipped on this waterfall - be prepared to add friction, lockoff, take small steps, etc...

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20170828213416
comment: We played quite a bit and took longer than we could have. It is a gorgeous canyon with beautiful lighting and several narrow gorgeous lined with ferns and moss.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20170829155046
comment: Moderate flow - you can enter all the pools without concern of hydraulics. A really fun day with a large group! Anchors are in good shape. There’s a little giant sequoia sapling that has been planted in memory of Luca Chiarabini. It is tucked away DCL between the 2nd swirling pothole and the rap underneath the bridge. Please water it if you are there and send pics to Tiffanie Lin (cinnybear[at]gmail[dot]com). Thanks!

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20170906035859
comment: Beautiful canyon, not to be missed. It holds a little piece of our hearts.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20170917025932
comment: Terrific canyon and a lot of fun. Can see how this creek would be extremely dangerous in high flow. Ran into an issue with the rope getting stuck on R4. Block was caught in a crack up near the top requiring one of our party to ascend back up to fix it. Otherwise smooth sailing. First pothole was a joy to rappel. White water & some current, but no serious hydraulic effects to be concerned about. Two thumbs up!

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20171016232204
comment: Chilly day with air temperatures at the upper 50s. It rained prior and during our time in the canyon. When we entered, flow was moderate and by the time we exited, it probably had gotten to about high flow. Thicker neoprene was appreciated in these conditions. Potholes were recirculating but nothing our group hadn't encountered/overcome before. We entered both potholes and had throw ropes ready. Great day despite the rain!

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20180702184006
comment: Flow was moderate high and lower than expected compared to last year conditions around the same time. There are new logs and gravel that have changed some of the rappels. The beta has been updated with the changes. Take note that the first swirling pothole has logs that obstruct the original rappel/jump. Rappel DCR instead.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20180806040041
comment: All the anchors are in great shape. Flow was relatively low so the hydraulics and swirling potholes were not very significant . Super fun Canyon, although a little more water would have been more fun.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20180806043217
comment: This was the first time any in our group ran this canyon, the beta made us very cautious about the potential water hazards. So some of the rappels were discussed heavily and contingencies were pre-rigged, ready for rescue. But it actually was a pretty calm canyon. We took our time, were safe, enjoyed the beauty of it all. We looked for Luca's tree but it appears to be gone. Found a ring of rocks where I believe the tree would have been, but no tree to be found :(

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20180912163853
comment: Great canyon. All anchors in good shape, although couple of bolts could use tightening. The narrows have a good bit more woody debris than last year. After exiting the canyon ran into a woman driving down from above who reported a cougar sighting along the road.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20190715174355
comment: No issues with hydraulics. Moderate high to moderate flow. Pothole #1 still has visible and hidden logs. The large white boulder has shifted down so there is no longer a rappel from the boulder.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20190805030942
comment: Another great day in Davis. There was much rappelling and jumping into pools. Approached a few of the falls with caution. Found a snake in Swirling Pothole #1, who, from body language, just wanted to get the hell out of there. Swirling pothole #2 has some good current; better to send packs down separately. Good to have someone at the exit to help people out. Water temps were perfect for a hot day. We took a "wrong turn" on the way in and accidentally found a steep dirty trail leading right to the top of R1.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20190816201004
comment: Anchors and webbing in good shape. You can see water swirling in swirly potholes, but they weren't difficult to swim through or escape. The rocks in the watercourse are very slippery and this is difficult to see (I'm not sure if this is normal as it was my first descent and I'm not from the area). All 5 of us were slipping throughout the day despite having shoes with sticky rubber and experience creek walking. I fell and broke my hand (see incident report). Note, it rained a lot the night after we ran this. We drove back into the bridge checkpoint the next day and the water was running a fair bit higher.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20200719185116
comment: My fat thumb accidentally hit save on a condition report before I was done, so this is an incomplete report. In the future I promise to take Daniel on more trips because his trip reports are the best. Also, his photos are the best. Really tho, all around, he is the best. Thank you for your time. Namaste

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20200726221647
comment: we bipassed R5 & R6 log jams rappels. Above R5 is a trail Rh that you follow 140 yds downstream. one slippery section. Two big tree at end of shelf, green webbing on last tree 60 ft rappel just past log jams 🤠. We climbed back on top of log jam and out green webbing with slings to pull u up in case you go the traditional route. Also note. you can exit out on this trial

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20200824191304
comment: At this flow, the log jam below R6 was very easy to climb over and no more difficult than any other part of the canyon. There is a small submerged rock to the left of the jam that you can use to climb over. There is a new optional jump off of the log jam as well (check water levels first).

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20200907014416
comment: Fun new rappel now that the logjam has blown out. Found the boulder submerged deep in the pool below the new falls. Climbed up over the logjam. You can either downclimb a sloping log or rappel. Super fun day and great group. Exciting new full-size freezer that someone dumped off the bridge.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20200913215601
comment: Great day! The new log jam at the bottom of swirling pothole #1 is easy to climb over. Davis received 0.15" rain the day before, ending around 10pm, but since the forest has been so dry for weeks, it didn't appear to impact the water levels at all. We removed a mailbox from under the bridge. No mail though.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20200921231258
comment: All anchors in good condition. R6 with the giant log jam is a really fun jump. Highly recommended.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20210314180132
comment: Scoped the flows last week after running Covell and decided to give it a go. Added redirects on the big drop to stay out of the powerful flow and on the lower drop below the bridge. Next group should bring some natural looking 5 mm dyneema cord to replace the white that was used. The two dangerous potholes were swam without issue but a strong swimmer should go first. The first pothole was jumped by half of the group after a depth check. There is still a long in there that spans the pothole. The slide that was once blocked by debris and the big boulder has become a horse tailing drop at extreme flow so that added some spice.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20210720035557
comment: Ran Davis Sunday July 18th in moderate high flow. Manageable pothole swirls and currents with some care and assists. Bypass not used on R4 Davis Falls, used the re-direct for R8 Lower Creek Falls to stay out of the whitewater flow. Great day out in this classic canyon. It's in a good shape, no changed conditions from previous couple of years.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20210727061242
comment: First time running it. Would estimate moderate/moderate-high flow. Didn't need the R4 redirect. R6 pothole was jumpable with an unproblematic hydraulic. R7 recirculating hydraulic was stronger and team assists helped. Added thicker black webbing to the R8 redirect, and now the white cord can be removed by a group descending in lower flow.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20210802024452
comment: As a result of a dry spring and summer, water levels are probably a month lower than where they'd be on a normal year. Some current in the swirling pools and on R8, but easy to swim out of. Spent some time playing in the horizontal recirculation on R7. Can still get pummeled on the big falls, so the redirect on R8 was appreciated.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20210813022911
comment: Ran Davis, paired nicely with Hager. All was good.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20210913181627
comment: Davis is a great creek. Dress warm the water is cold. The beta is accurate and beginner friendly this time of year. Our time was 5 hours due to having some training discussions at each anchor.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20220711022524
comment: Fun time through Davis. Added a bolt station for the second tier of Davis Creek Falls. You can either rappel in the flow or alongside of in higher water. This made for a great time. Just beyond the notable log jam, be mindful of getting through the logs upon exit as it is a tight fit. Check Swirpool 1 for sliding and it was great. We added a V thread anchor for Swirlpool 2 that will assist in getting down. The pool is SHALLOW. The sun was out and the day was great. Met the new landowner and he was cool with people continuing to run the can but did ask that we clean up any trash that we see. His name is Wes and he is very friendly. Please be kind and respectful of anyone you encounter. He does have children who swim in the lower section like the locals.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20220730235612
comment: Great trip and great conditions. Chilly swims but it was 100°F out. We added/replaced a hanger and rapide on R4+. Some raps jumpable. No worrisome hydraulics encountered, tho the chute in R5 was pushy. I reeeaally want R5 to be a slide... can we move that boulder? ;-)

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20220801041432
comment: The flow is down considerably from one week ago. No need for redirect on only get wet if you intentionally push right. New anchor at R4.5 is fantastic. Better rope pull and allows for rappelling in or next to the 2nd tier 40ft drop. The recirc in R5 is easily crossed by bending the knees and jumping onto one’s back while letting go of the rope to completely cross the pool. R8 is an easy walk down on rappel in the middle of the flow, boil at the bottom is fairly benign. Great fun level will not last long with the dry heat.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20220817024331
comment: Enjoyable, leisurely descent through Davis on a hot summer's day. No anchor was found at R2, so guessing folks are downclimbing through the logjam this year. Added an anchor on a tree far DCL to rappel in below the jam. Avoids any need to fool about climbing around on slippery logs. New bolt station (R4a) is terrific and a fun addition to the canyon. Water conditions were great, little pushy in places. Much lounging in pools.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20220830191545
comment: Glorious 5* day. Echoing others in saying the new R4 option is much more enjoyable. Two of the group swung at the end of R8 as they tried to stay out the pummel. Getting into the flow and dropping straight down through it is the better option. Other than that, I'd call it mod-low everywhere else.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20220926170920
comment: Such a beautiful canyon! Davis has moderate-low flow at this point in the season, which makes it a quick and easy descent. There were no points where we felt the flow was dangerous or the hydraulics concerning. There were plenty of swimmers though, so with lower night temperatures the water was chilly and the members of our party with thicker wetsuits were the happiest. For the 120ft rappel, we used the DCR bolt station for the full drop and the pull was fine from the bottom.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230617220635
comment: The pictures vary too much to land on what the levels were. That being said, it was definitely high and sporty. Notable items, the second tier on Davis Creek Falls has a large stump in it which makes it a bit easier to get to the new anchor. The big Boulder hasn’t moved. The logjam has progressed but very doable to get around. We jumped the first swirl pool DCL. The infinity pool and the V thread to help get out . Rap below bridge was straightforward and the log in the pocket is gone. The slidey log now has friends so as a doe see doe to get around it. All anchors are good to go.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230625040406
comment: All bolts good, like the new additions. Not sure when looking at water levels they consider the cowlitz (dam regulated) as an indicator. I would use the Cispus River, flows into cowlitz from the south, but similar drainage. My 2 cents. Water level was fun, but moderate. Never really pushy. Beta is good, recommend on the two swirling pools to jump them (into the sweet exiting spot) over rappelling into the SOB swim into the recirculation wall.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230625074105
comment: Edit on previous post, Cowlitz dam is downstream of the Davis creek, my bad. Cowlitz has much larger drainage basin up towards white pass. Cispus is a more similar drainage.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230627003949
comment: Davis was fantastic. Cold water (8mm wetsuits were appreciated). Swirling Potholes #1 and #2 were not as strong as anticipated and felt closer to moderate/moderate-high in terms of strength. The anchors were in good shape.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230705074455
comment: Great way to spend July 4th! Very fun creek with great drops. Swirling potholes were not as strong as expected. 2 team members had issues with SP2, but eventually were able to swim out. R4a is a great rap and worth the drive itself. Team enjoyed the raps, the pools, and the pushy but manageable flow.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230716004855
comment: Great day in the canyon. The log rappel in the begin has a hornet nest in or close to it. Be mindful! Also, a bolt was installed that wasn’t there a month ago when we opened it. Not sure what the point is. It was at the same location as the old redirect for high flow sequence for Lower Davis Falls under the bridge.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230717183255
comment: Flow was a2, moderate-low, easy to walk in the middle of the flow anywhere, very grippy. Everyone had a blast. This canyon never gets old. 5 noteworthy jumps, 5 downclimbs, 1 waterslide, and 3 longish rappels, all in a deep beautiful canyon. Upper Section: 1. DC left 10' on natural staircase, 2. DC left 10' through logjam easier than it looks, 3. DC right down natural ladder, 4. 70' rappel on side of waterfall do a J-tie at next botls, 5. 50' into plunge pool (can off rope on ledge and jump 15') multiple jumping perches from 5' to 20'. Lower Section: 1. Chute can be downclimbed almost all the way but rope needed at bottom, 2. slide on right 15' (or jump from left 20'), 3. downclimb on right 10' through logjam, 4. jump 12' into hanging pool (CW circulation) then DC 4' at pouroff, 5. rappel 50' at bridge, 6. jumping pool up to 10', 7. short downclimb/scooch logs, 8. small jumping pool, 9. finale is an overhang rappel 15' into a grotto and cave accessed through rabbit hole.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230725173311
comment: Beautiful trip down Davis in the late afternoon. We suited up at the car and left down the trail at 4:45pm, and were surprised to finish only 1.5hr later. So I updated the beta to reduce the time range to 2-5hr, from the previous 3-5hr. We had done Lewis earlier in the day. Davis was quite markedly a much better canyon than Lewis. We also camped that night in the field at the start of Davis.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230726155510
comment: Great day in Davis. Flow was low enough to not be too challenging but still fun.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230731173421
comment: Davis: classic fun as always. There is a new giant rootball jammed in the opening of the hanging pool on the big rappel (just below unlinked anchor at R4b). Didn't affect the rappel or pull, but could cause issues for anyone trying to climb out of the hanging pool in high water. Found part of a rusty metal cash box (?) at the final rappel and packed it out.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230814235757
comment: Very fun day in Davis! All the anchors looked to be in great condition and the jumps were great! We used the redirect for R4, but the flow was plenty tame to have gone straight down. The swirling pothole for R7 presented no challenges to any of the members.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230828213449
comment: Great day out. Wonderful weather and terrain. Water is relatively low, still great swimming. Skipped the redirect on R4. No special challenges.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230913222142
comment: R9 anchor log unstable. It is balanced on a pile of smaller logs right at the anchor webbing. Anchor Log shifted down 3 inches when 205lb rappeller went vertical. The rest of the team down climbed. Water is low but still fun. R5 pool rock exposed about 1ft above the water. Great day in Davis. View in attached photo is from midway down the down climb, looking back up-canyon at anchor. The anchor log is the horizontal log in the photo. Webbing is just visible at top of anchor log.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20231026064829
comment: Due to recent rainfall, water levels were pretty fun but not overwhelming. All anchors were in good shape, I avoided the bees by using the alt DCL tree. Might have been the last run for 2023, the air temps were quite cool and I def felt the chill towards the end of the canyon even in a dry suit. Amazing place, I can see why everyone goes here, I hope to get out there next season again!! I also did my best to look for trash under the bridge and didn't see anything laying around. I documented this canyon with current conditions and obstacles which I'm sure will change but I will add the vid to the media subpart of this canyon page.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20240701170451
comment: Great day in high flow. Cleared the R4 anchor station of hundreds of lbs of debris. Used the redirect to stay out the flow. Crux was the R5 slot/chute which needed careful footwork. The logjam exit from the R6 slide-pool has shifted downstream. What remains is much less stable than before, and has a siphon under the surface. Be cautious here. The down-climb on the other side is similar to the last few years. A bolt DCR up on the wall may help protect swimmers, up-climbers, and down-climbers from shifting debris. R7 recirc pool wasn’t problematic with a flat jump. We put a hanger & carabiner (no webbing) on the naked R8 redirect bolt - it wasn’t clear from its ambiguous state that it was meant to be decommissioned. Don’t mind if it stays or goes, but I suspect more confusion if it’s partially decommissioned again. Perhaps a little project for the WCC to handle the physical & community communications around (the R6 slide also has some bolts that also need decommissioning). Water temps weren’t nearly as cold as we expected.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20240716193901
comment: Great canyon. Beautiful and fun. Anchors were in good shape. Log jams are kind of sketchy.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20240729062425
comment: Returned to Davis after 5 years to introduce some cavers to canyoneering as was done for me.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20240729183055
comment: Of many trips through Davis this was the funnest. Finally jumped R4b from the pour off - it was plenty deep. Did the slide at R6 for the first time as well - usually do the jump, plenty deep in any case. With jumps at R4b, R6, R7, and after R8 and R9, that's a lot of jumps. With the 5 minute approach and 10 minute exit must be the lowest pay-to-play ratio in WA. I've changed my rating to 5-stars due to the higher jump and slide. We were in canyon 2.5 hrs. Another 1/2 hr for shuttle and gearing up.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20240806040330
comment: Great day as always. Did observe some spontaneous rockfall at the bottom of R4 coming down off the cliffs DCR onto the ledges above the pool. Signs of recent rockfall - use caution.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20240830170243
comment: Lovely day in Davis! All the normal jumps and slides are in. All anchors in good condition.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20240926165905
comment: What an amazing place! Went in loaded for bear, being new to PNW canyons. Overkill, but helped us proceed confidently Anchors were all great, and beta spot on.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20241006163327
comment: Some recent rains bumped the water level up to moderate or more likely moderate-low. Rain also left everything in the canyon wet and slippery. This was my third time through Davis, and I have noticed this canyon is pretty slippery to begin with, combine that with a light moister coating on everything and it's even worse. I've updated the beta for rappel lengths for R1 and R5. I brought my ultra-light 70foot rope for all the short rappels as listed previously on the beta and was short at R1 and R5 and had to extend my pulls, so the beta has been updated. Cleaned up several glass bottles and trash under the bridge. We did not have a safe way to carry out some broken glass. Future canyoners watch out for glass and if you have a spare empty keg to remove the glass that would be great. Found new anchor at the drop between R9 and R10 (Webbing DCL from root ball). This is the drop with the big knobby log that we all used to butt skootch down in years past. With new wood on this log and it being wet and slippery the new anchor was much appreciated and made for a safer drop rappelling.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20241009060850
comment: Overcast slightly rainy day but all were comfortable. The redirects have no caribeaners.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20250324185202
comment: Did not descend Davis today, although the flow was likely doable for a strong team. More notably there is now a large log in the recirculating pool. It may get washed away before the season, however if you are the first team down this summer, make sure to get a look of the log from the road before descending! More logs/debris may arrive and be deposited here from the up stream log jame

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20250612040738
comment: Great day in Davis, curiously lower flow than when we ran it early July last year, despite the Cowlitz gauge reading nearly double. We replace the webbing and quicklinks above the roundy pool with stainless links, swapped the webbing for the log slide rappel, and the redirect had the nut spun nearly completely off so we removed the hanger and link, the bare bolt is still there. Even at this flow jumping into the pool at the bottom of the bridge simply flushes you straight out, and the redirect seems like it creates more of a hazard than anything. The log in the roundy pool is wedged up well above the water. It does impede the jump, but isn’t a hazard in the pool.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20250617063010
comment: We spent 3 hours hauling and cutting the log out of the whirlypool. Would’ve been nice to still have a redirect, even if decommissioned, on R8. Great trip, great team, great canyon!

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20250713031338
comment: Davis was fun. Anchors are all good.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20250729015135
comment: All anchors are in great condition. Kudos to the team who placed the new bolts and removed the previous ones.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20250807090658
comment: Canyon is in great shape. The maintenance and newer bolts have improved it a lot and made the canyon easier to run than I remember from a couple years ago. The water felt and looked like a strong moderate. It may have picked up a little from the rain. Was a good day!

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20250809161208
comment: Flow looked moderate according to reference photos but felt more on the moderate-low side. Incredibly slippery creek. 5/4mm was comfortable. Approximately 2.5h in canyon and 30 min to gear down and walk the road and retrieve our vehicle.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20250822164223
comment: Agree with Nclegg's condition report with regard to flow. From the photo comparison, could make a case for moderate flow, but conditions in canyon were evaluated as moderate-low.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20250908033356
comment: The new bolts are indeed lovely! The water level is definelty low at this point. The boulder at the bottom of the V slot is above water. Make sure to scout the jumo at the bottom of Upper Davis Falls as there is new(ish) rock just below the surface DCL.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20251012173616
comment: Another late season run. I replaced the webbing on R2 (root ball above Upper Davis Falls) as the current webbing was pretty damaged.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20260411024748
comment: -The logs at R2 have shifted to over the pool, now goes as an L sit shallow jump, in lower water consider bolting wet line dcr. -R3 log anchor gone, still a down climb. The convenient log we sit while rigging R4 has shifted away from the wall. -To make accessible at higher flows consider bolting a traverse anchor to R5, a meat traverse anchor was required here. -The log jam between R6 and R7 is gone. Apparently it’s what it was like 10 yrs ago. At this flow it looked like white death. So we scrambled up the side of the canyon dcl and bolted an anchor that dropped us into the pool beneath where the log jam used to be. -At higher flow consider using a bag anchor at R7 instead of sending a swimmer down to set up a guided off the dyneema hand loop as we did. Even with flotation (or because of it) one can become temporarily stuck in this recirc. -R8 isn’t the crux everyone makes it out to be, but we meet anchor guide rapped it due to a drysuit malfunction. -The log jam at “R9” is gone, it’s now a very very shallow slide and could use a bolt station. -R10 is cleaned out a bit, in lower flow a wet line dcr might be nice. -All pre existing bolts are intact, more will be needed because so much wood has been cleared out. -We left a rim rope as an escape line just below R4 which we didn’t end up using. After running the canyon we were pooped. I’ll come back for it.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20260510220935
comment: NEXT TEAMS PLEASE BRING: * A good ring hanger and variety of different nuts for R3b. We tried the nut from a 3/8" Simpson Strong Bolt 2 - it almost fit, but it binded up after one rotation. Maybe it's an M10, or 9/16" with a different thread. * 5mm sheathed Dyneema cord for the exit of pothole #2. It's still functional but the sheath is heavily core-shot. Davis is still running high, and swift-water skills are necessary. We bypassed pothole #1 using the high DCL anchor the team earlier this year added. However we did confirm the exit of the pool is a fun slide! If the log blocks the entrance slide is TBD. Excellent day with just the right level of challenges that let us take our time to practice skills & decision making. Only real negative is that the creek is frigging slippery.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20260526221734
comment: Conditions remarkably similar to ~3 weeks ago. Completed previously reported anchor maintenance, everything should be in great shape. Also added anchors to the exit of the first pothole to facilitate staging people & packs. It's still unclear if the log in this pool blocks the entrance slide or not.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20260617021144
comment: Great day in Davis flow was still playful but not bad. Anchors were in good shape…new single hanger by the old log jam spins but usable. Checked this pool with goggles and only one log is in the pool so slide goes and a precision jump it also possible, just don’t hit the log! The slide is probably safer option. Didn’t see a bolt at the R9 sketchy down climb, would be great for a single here unless we are blind and missed it.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20260705000358
comment: One team member sprained their thumb after slipping while creek walking at the bottom of upper davis falls. He was able to rappel left handed and we made our way slowly down the rest of the canyon. The R5 traverse line bolt, R6 bypass anchor and R7 v thread loop were all essential to finishing strong. Thankfully non catastrophic albeit still a wake up call to take creek walking very seriously.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20260711024735
comment: So two things. The pothole slide dcr with a log probably goes? But I wasn't brave enough to check it. It also makes jumping really awkward. The famous Davis boulder has once again moved! It's now about 6-7' underwater and has been pushed about 6 feet back in the pool. This rappel might finally go as a slide but we didn't test it. I would be keen to go back and try it through. Breaking a tailbone or leg is def possible though if it doesn't go.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20260713042719
comment: Beautiful canyon! High walls, high quality raps, gorgeous slot with a big grandiose feel! Was definitely floored by this one! 5/4 wetsuit was comfy. We helped a beginner through and took our time. Loved the scenery! Plenty of unique and memorable features in this one! We suited up at parking and desuited at cars, hardly any dry hiking no bush. Wish it lasted longer!

title: Conditions:Denny Creek Canyon-20150609204533
comment: The water level was low-moderate. We started just above Snowshoe Falls and did the full canyon. Due to time considerations, we bypassed many of the shorter rappels. We built a deadman anchor with several large rocks in the main watercourse at Keekwulee Falls. From where we rigged, it took about 130' of rope to reach the bottom.

title: Conditions:Denny Creek Canyon-20210706230909
comment: Despite the recent heatwave (105F+), a surprisingly large snowfield sits just upstream of R3 blocking the width of the canyon. It doesn't look safe to cross or skirt-around. We didn't check any higher, and started our decent from R3 (boulder midstream). The water emerging from under the snow was chilly (no shit). R4 (Keekwulee Falls) was kicking out a significant rooster tail. We went from a single bolt + tree combo on the DCR flow, all the way to the pool below the falls. It kept us out of the main rooster tail flow, but still in the fun flow that pours less aggressively off the lip.
