On the lower angle slopes below, passing under the main flow gave a powerful but fun hammering (entirely avoidable). From there we made the most of all the potential raps downstream. The raps are all quite short and technically tame, but the flow was still high enough to keep it fun. In lower flow they might be more tedious and warrant bypassing. There wasn't much sign of existing webbing, so we burnt through a lot re-rigging the raps. [starting from above R3 in beta] * R1 - Short, but steep with steps. Pool is deep on DCL side. * R2 - Keekwulee Falls, as described above. * R3 - Short, from obvious boulder DCL. * R4 - Short from DCR boulder set back a little. Awkward step halfway down. * R5 - Double slides from DCR boulder set far back. * R6 - Low angle series of cascades, from threaded boulders midstream. The Garcia gauge (12143400) was reporting 170cfs.

title: Conditions:Denny Creek Canyon-20210825021333
comment: Upper 2 Rappels are not an option. Snow /avalanche flow is still 30 ft deep and impassable. Start at R3, off side trail to top of the falls. We had to hunt and built almost every anchor. Natural anchors are being swept away during hight water floods or webbing picked up by visitors, even from the trip I did 1 1/2 month ago. Water level is VERY low. Most pools at the bottom of the Rappels are only 4 ft deep and not jumpable.

title: Conditions:Denny Creek Canyon-20240715173929
comment: Nice sunny day to enjoy an easy canyon. Flow was very low. Lots of hikers around the upper falls and crowds near the bottom. Parking lot fills up quickly.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20180827000836
comment: We set up a bunch of slung anchors and fiddlesticked a bit (all natural anchors). Beautiful day. The flow in the middle fork Snoqualmie was around 350 (accidentally said 650 before - honest mistake, sorry!) cfs, and flow in the canyon created some dangerous hydraulics in places. Flow down some of the falls was sufficient to kick you around hard. Rock near the creek was slippery from algae. Jumps noted in beta (edited 8/26/18) were all doable. Some had shallow water but no hard landings. We exited the canyon at the boulder garden above R14 and finished the canyon on 8/26/18 at significantly lower flow.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20180827001537
comment: Did the canyon from R14 down to finish it off from our descent on 7/22/18. Flow was low enough that getting under the falls and moving through the flow was much more doable than on 7/22. Flow in the Middle Fork Snoqualmie was at 150 cfs.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20180902162959
comment: Ran Dingford Creek on a rainy day with 11 people. We dropped in from the start and ran the full descent and found some existing cord/cordelettes at several drops. It took about 7 hours for us to complete. This is a wonderful canyon full of jumps and slides and beautiful water. It will surely become a classic!

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20180902163239
comment: Ran this again and established a couple more anchors. There were no hydraulics.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20190801182604
comment: First descent of 2019. MF Snoqualmie gauge was at 306. Flow was slightly sporty. Nothing was too pushy; it was possible to stand at almost all of the pour overs, but a couple of the beginners needed frequent spotting. We did 8 rappels, a handful of small jumps and short slides. Some time was spent replacing anchor webbing. Half were natural anchors, half bolted. The route could be improved with a couple more bolt stations. One stuck rope was time consuming. 9 hours car-to-car.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20200730035019
comment: Great group of aquatic swimmer/river rafting guides. Ran everything except the last 4 rappels due to higher flows significantly impact the access to the anchors station on the 4 bottom rappel;s. No new log jams or rock movements. All jumps and slide from last year are all viable. All 1/2 bolt/chains are in good shape and well placed. YOU must know where to go, because the beta provided is useless. This is my 16th trip down in the last 2 years and knowledge of rocks and downclimbs are critical. Trail up is great, and road in is slow and pothole and requires a SUV type vehicle clearance.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20200804212802
comment: The Beta has been updated to reflect where/when is a slide, downclimb, rappel or jump etc. The Water wast 340 CFS and again we only did upper 3/4, but the lower is almost there. We went down the middle of dragon chute and Chunder dome with no problems except a lot of fun water hitting you.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20200908180703
comment: Descended with the gauge reading about 150cfs. Still decent flow for "relatively low" conditions. Maybe not "sporty" but still plenty of flow to make it interesting. Just FYI for anyone on the newer side considering dropping in at 300 or 400+cfs. Although it sounds like flows may not be representative of the gauge? More importantly, this canyon has had a reputation for and continues to be talked about as a fun "waterpark." This is not only largely inaccurate but disingenuous and dangerous. I can attest from personal experience in 2019 going into it with an "everything is play time" attitude and broke my leg nearly at the top of the canyon on a minor drop. Just from what I've heard myself in 2020, there have been at least four minor injuries in this canyon. THIS IS NOT A WATERPARK. Many of the pools are NOT deep and wide. Boulders and ledges in many pools are numerous and frequent. There are slippery spots. Jumping or sliding in the wrong place or using poor form could easily lead to injury. (And all the more difficult to read at higher flows with more whitewater.) In addition, this canyon has downclimbing that may be difficult and intimidating for newer canyoneers. A slip could be disastrous, especially at higher flows. Please don't be lulled into a cavalier attitude in this canyon by those not warning of the risks or going in to only maximize fun.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20200909044622
comment: First time down the canyon for the group. Hard to exactly follow the beta in places, but navigated without too much trouble in the end. I concur with other comments about "water park" being a misleading description - it seemed to have few straight-forward jumps but plenty of risky sections. One member of the group sprained their ankle jumping from the J2 high-option (over-shot the narrow landing by a few feet). After continuing down the canyon for a further 20 minutes it was apparent they needed to escape. We protected a few extra sections to help their descent, then used the halfway escape-point to escort them back to the trail, where they were able to return to the trailhead unassisted. It will be very interesting to see what this is like in different water levels.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20201012174405
comment: Gauge was about 275 about a week after heavy rains. Seemed comparable to photos/videos from a higher gauge reading in early season. Significantly more interesting and challenging than mid summer conditions. I'd personally recommend wearing a hat/brim to go through the middle of the flow on the chunderdome, at least at these levels.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20210721000422
comment: First decent. Both of us are Class V whitewater guides and love pushy water, which it was. Very slipper rock, PLEASE use felt booties, my 5-10 were slippery. Did the upper 3/4, and bailed on the last 4 raps, because there water flow was just waves leading into the next rappel station. All bolts are in great shape, no new debris in pools or new rock falls filling the pools. Beta is still good. I would not recommend doing this at any higher level that 400 CFS on the main fork. 2 comments below are accurate, this is not a Disney land water park, but more a short slipper walk/crawl to the next challenge water park. This is granite rock park, that is not known for forming deep pools due to the hardness of the rock, compared to sandstone. Assume your depth are shallow and alway lower non-jumpers to check depth. This is my 20th trip down this and knowing your routes greatly increases your enjoyment. If you are interested in doing this canyon, please contact me and I most likely will be free to join you.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20210730152439
comment: Ran the full canyon. Did the wet routes on all options, no issues. Rock is still slippery. Nice canyon with afternoon sunshine

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20210806164039
comment: Ran whole route. Great water level for the wet lines (Tanner @ ~275cfs).

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20210808050020
comment: Ran it in the rain and chilly 60 degrees. Water level rose slightly during the day, made it funner. We lost a yellow back pack, with 300 ft rope in the final rappel, pool. Tossed it and it just went down. If found let me know.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20210815041017
comment: Fast trip - 10.15am departure from trailhead, messing around in the final pool by 3.15pm. Great moderate fun flows & great low hassle day, despite the heat & smoke.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20210815174656
comment: Still a little smoky, Verified a couple rappels can be slides also. Will update Beta. Ran the wet and dry routes with this big group based on each persons preferences. All pulls good. Recovered non floating back pack out of final pool. Great day. Going back in on the 17th

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20210823181324
comment: Back again. Cool & cloudy weather meant we opted to keep moving all day. Ended up completing the whole technical section in 3.5hrs; a total of 4.5hr overall. Gauge at 180cfs. Flows & pool depths definitely lower than earlier in the month, but no impact on the jumps & slides we usually do.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20210927045040
comment: Fantastic day an quiet possibly the last of the season for Dingford. Middle Fork gauge was at 233. The water was on the high side of moderate, but did not cause any challenges, however, we did utilize hand lines using present bolts for two rappels. All the usual jumps and slides were good.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20220722183152
comment: We only ran about 75% of Dingford. Middle fork snoqualmie gage at 574 cfs. This is still extremely high flow and you should only go with those who know the canyon intimately and leaders who are expers in white water canyoning environments (different than just white water rafting or any other sport involving turbulent water). Lots of avoidance, sketchy pool crossings, high risk of getting swept over if obstacles are not properly protected and lots of planning ahead. We avoided the Chunderdome by using the bolts DCR. Make sure to protect any pool crossing!

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20220916014813
comment: 120cfs on the gauge, the lowest I've run it. Many pools felt shallow, but constrictions still had force to be weary of. Expected Chunderdome to be much milder, but it still had chunder to it. (Note, despite the gap in trip reports, many 10s of people have run the canyon since July).

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20230709151538
comment: All bolts fixed and good. All pools look clean, but always check. updated beta for couple new bolts put in last year. Ran the heart of dragon chute and chunderdome. Fun, fun. Great group of canyoneers.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20230716052212
comment: Lost a 1/3 of the flow in one week. Less pushy, everything still good, but less surfing on some of the rappels. Was planning on doing mineral creek, but the road was closed, so did the turn around to here. Fun fun canyon. saw other groups in but they stayed just ahead.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20230806005913
comment: Good run through a great canyon as always. Beta for R13 at the Toaster says not to jump but if you down climb through the tight sections in the flow (highly recommended in manageable conditions) our entire party was easily able to do a short canyon butt flop into the pool below R13 without issue and entirely bypass the rappel. Also recommend not underestimating the Chunderdome if utilizing the “in the flow” rap. It’s impossible to see your foot placements and the volume of water in the rappel line is not to be trifled with. There’s also several foot traps that 3 out of 4 of us found by stepping into them during the rappel. Could easily break an ankle/entrap someone in higher flow. Otherwise all bolts are good and the canyon is still a classic.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20230907194554
comment: Great water park of a canyon. Super fun day, even if flow was a bit low. Chunderdome was quite mild.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20240715025015
comment: Big water dropping quickly. This is not a waterpark, there are very specific routes and there should alway be someone in your party who has done the canyon before, because the beta can not be so exact. I am alway open if you need a person. Today, flow 550, all bolts good, but skipped the bottom 4 rappels, due to time and Thrill Vs risk. The waterslides were very pushy, trying to take you over the next lip. Two logs in Chunderdome, One completely sideways at the top, blocking wet route. Whoever removes them need to make sure they do not drop in and block the wet route channel. I reset the webbing up on DCL for dryish route.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20240804170803
comment: The flow was somewhat spicy, especially in the chunderdome and the last rappel. No hydraulic dangers to worry about. The flow was about 270cfs at middle fork Tanner.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20240921223926
comment: Great day in Dingford! Flow was still relatively high for this late in the season. Overall water conditions were moderate but brutally cold. Time includes lapping the big slide 5 times but bailing after the Chunderdome (at which we set up a guided rappel). By the time we finished the Chunderdome rappel we had a party member who was pre-hypothermic so we exited by scrambling up a chute DCR.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20250721203530
comment: Finally to hit the famous Dingford! Nothing like a 20ft jump to start a canyon. Great canyon and a lot of fun, with a great group. Anchors were all solid. This canyon is well taken care of. I'll be back!

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20250815044152
comment: Amazing day in Dingford! All anchors are in good shape and there are no special concerns right now. Enjoy!

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20250819005329
comment: Great day down dingford. Tons of fun knowing all the slides and jumps in the canyon. Water flow was surprisingly not a lot for having rained a lot. Water was warmer than expected too (still quite cold)

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20251004065850
comment: All anchors were good, slides and jumps were more shallow than normal. Shallow technique is required even for jumps and slides that normally go. More moss than peak season, watch for slippery edges and climbs.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20260428043440
comment: Ran at 160 cfs per comparison gauge, felt lower moderate, maybe mod low? Good splashy fun taking friends down. Some of the jumps and slides were noticeably more shallow than when I first ran it at 220 cfs a month prior.

title: Conditions:Dingford Creek-20260713040624
comment: Great day. All anchors are good. Normal jumps and slides are good too.

title: Conditions:Discovery Creek-20250714193532
comment: No anchors were found, so possible first descent. Ascended steeps slopes west of the creek to reach a flat-ish area around 2900ft, then dropped into the creek. Nice-looking 45ft falls just above... next time we'll drop in a little higher up. Relatively low flow, although things still can get interesting when it's concentrated all in one area. R2 is the big pitch and is excellent: running right down a V-shaped groove. You have to drop into one pocket and push yourself through a veil of falling water to get out. The entire falls gradually turns DCR. Below, the canyon tightens up significantly with numerous back-to-back obstacles. Very reminiscent of nearby Clark Creek.

title: Conditions:Downclimbs R Us Creek-20220525152659
comment: Parked about 1.5 miles away from the TH due to snow patches covering the road so likely any group that goes after will be much faster if they park at the TH. Dropped in at 3800' but would recommend dropping in around 3400-3500' as there is a lot of creeking and nuisance rappels prior. Water is still snow melt, there are a few small waist deep pools.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20190721131306
comment: Ran this canyon after a group ran Dark and another ran Davis. So it is possible. We took and abbreviated rout to avoid the 40 minute creek walk and dropped us in almost to the top drop. We jump the first drop from both sides. The second rap is NOT deep enough to jump and set up and anchor around a very visible boulder. R3 was jumped from a ledge about halfway down by the majority of the group.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20190815070025
comment: Checked out East after running Dark. We parked further up the road and bushwhacked directly east to the first drop. The exit at the Cispus river was running very high and very cold- silty glacial melt with rapids. It was easy to stick to the bank but use caution.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20200817022506
comment: First visit after several recommendations to check this one out. Took the NorHex Route in and descended a narrow draw into the creek. Wow, what a canyon! Great bang for the buck. The last rappel into the narrows is spectacular. We found the exit route without much difficulty.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20220731065503
comment: Exiting Dark Creek by a good hour, we decided to run East Canyon Creek just down the road. Rebuilt anchor at R2 (looks like it's underwater in high flow). Pools are amazing and lovely float out through the narrows. Less awesome: the bushwhack back out. Will update the beta on the best route. Ideally, the routes in & out should be flagged to standardize route development.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20220919152341
comment: First time here and what a lovely creek! Good fun to play around in the pools, and it’s very pretty! The NorHex approach was great minus the final descent to the creek… though far better than a bunch of creek walking. We found R2 to be jumpable, though with a technical launch DCL. The pool below was at least 8’ deep… possibly 10’. It gets shallow quickly further away from the rock, so positioning someone where it’s shallow helps the jumper hit a safe target.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20230808004222
comment: We should call this "The Big East". Of all the canyons in the area it had the most flow by a wide margin during the rendezvous. The big flow, big jumps, huge grotto, deep aquamarine pools, swimming channel, make this one of the most underrated canyons. The approach does suck. The exit is actually very easy now due to a pretty well defined use trail up and then open forest walking to the road.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20230814234359
comment: Ayoooo this canyon was a BANGER! Sure the approach and exit aren't fantastic, but this one needs way more love than it gets. The canyon itself was spectacular and the flow was awesome, really glad we ran it. Each rappel was jumpable, but we touched bottom on the second pool. If I wasn't the first person down on the third rappel, to depth check, I would've jumped! That pool was so deep and I shorted the rope so the other members had fun plopping into the water.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20230907193903
comment: Short but a lot of fun. Did the overland entrance which wasn't great, but I've done worse for less. First rappel went as a jump, second was a little too shallow, and the last was jumpable as well after rappelling down to the ledge.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20231003213819
comment: Fantastic canyon with a lot of beauty and big wow-factor on that last 35-ft jump! Air temperature was around 50 degrees, not sure of the water temperature but it was cold. I had 11mm of neoprene in the chest area and 3-4 on the arms/legs. I was chilly in the water but okay.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20240808234220
comment: Nice day out in a beautiful creek. Did 140' rappel on alternate entrance from tributary into East.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20241007030721
comment: Splendid canyon. The 40ft jump from the ledge below R3 makes it a 5 star canyon for me. All anchors were in good condition

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20250804052831
comment: Great day in a new creek for me! We bush whacked in from NF-2322 and rappelled the tributary drop into the main creek, whihc was a fun warm up. There is a rebelay/second anchor on the ledge just below the start. Using this makes for a better line down to the main creek. The jump at R1 was great. We stayed out of the flow on the last two rappels. The walk down the Cispus was straightforward and the hike up the hill was steep.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20250809034149
comment: Gorgeous canyon. This one rivals Davis in my opinion. Loved it. The hike out sucks, but it’s well worth it. I echo everything Trey said. Tributary rap all the way. Anchors were in good condition.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20250928050255
comment: We did his Canyon as a back up to Adam’s. Adam’s was being Adams, which was highly unpredictable. We cut out some locals only rope that had webbing connected to it as if it was going to be an anchor. Did I just read the previous report comparing Davis to East Canyon? Wow. Thats wild.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20260429040608
comment: If rappelling R2 the line you take will determine how clean your pull is, considering the quick link for the pinch anchor is both underneath the boulder providing the pinch and situated well far back from the edge. The beta says rappel to the left, but I was surprised how far to the left that had to be. Should’ve just jumped it but I was running solo. It was nice knowing from a previous party that the R3 jump was clear and deep. This jump is the only thing makes the hike out worth it.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20260526181611
comment: Great sunny day in the canyon. We used the bridge approach and is was a scenic and floaty creek walk. All the anchors were good and the jumps went, although flow was too high for the in flow rappel on R2 so we used the bolt. The large punch bowl on R3 does form a recirc at this flow rotating back toward the falls along the DCL wall. A strong swim across toward DCR will put you in the outflow.

title: Conditions:East Canyon Creek-20260620003304
comment: Great run after Dark Creek. All of us jumped the first rap, most of us jumped the second, and the final rappel was great! Floated down the Cispus to the exit.

title: Conditions:Eightmile Creek (South Cascades)-20191014192251
comment: Some interesting rock formations and waterfalls along with a stellar jump point into a pool were high points. Very low water levels despite recent rains. Lot of creek walking through debris. Not worth it in lower flow.

title: Conditions:Eightmile Creek (South Cascades)-20200515214147
comment: First descent of Eightmile. Some nice falls, but the exit is kinda long. Fairly high pay-to-play. Would be good to go earlier for better flow. Maybe April-May timeframe. This one may be a good early season creek.

title: Conditions:Eightmile Creek (South Cascades)-20231112070251
comment: Nice flow today, but we should have skipped the lower three falls. The creek walk to the lower sequence followed by the long creek walk out to Panther Creek is not worth it. It would be best to leave the creek via the exit after rap 2.

title: Conditions:Falls Creek-20250501032743
comment: The scramble to the top tier is quite fun…looks like there was a rope assist but has since been cut down. There are no anchors set so I ghosted everything. R1 In flow dcr, r2 in massive flow dcr, r3 is pretty much a dry rap cause you’d have to be psycho to drop in That flow. Upper falls end in pool that’s deeper than you think. Middle section lets you fall behind the pour over and rap behind the falls which is amazing, def the highlight of the series of 3 drops.

title: Conditions:Falls Creek-20251116014902
comment: Great day at FCF. Big water. I don’t think the reference photos are close to accurate. Probably reversed in flow descriptions. Did the traditional way up so we could look at the flow. Water was a little brown. R1 could be better. Maybe a bolt DCR? R2 we used a traverse to the tree in the middle and went middle DCR. Went down 30 feet to a shelf and tossed a bolt in that is behind the veil (takes a little effort to get to the shelf) and out of site to the general public. Rapped to the bottom from there. We stayed out of the boiler hole as it looked wild and there’s a log in there. Put a bolt in the back of a boulder that’s in the middle of the creek and added webbing to keep things tidy. Fun day.

title: Conditions:Fir Creek-20220810215155
comment: A fun little canyon! The zigzag chute sequence is terrific. Parked at the gate (being careful not to block it). Flow was relatively low, but still fun. To exit, we thrashed up the nose of the ridge right where Fir drops into Vance Creek. It worked, but was a dirty mess. Will look for a better route next time. On the way back, we realized that our parking location had the unintended consequence of luring multiple groups of hikers down the road past the gate. They were looking for the Vance Creek Bridge trailhead and, on seeing our parked cars, assumed we were parked at the trailhead. Ooops.

title: Conditions:Fir Creek-20220815010458
comment: Another great little canyon in the Skykomish River Valley. Hangers on R2 are loose, but the bolts seem sound. We hiked out via the Vance Creek route. Vance is low this time of year and hiking along the gravel bars (crisscrossing from side to side occasionally) and taking the road out is far more pleasant than the bushwhack route we did last time.

title: Conditions:Fir Creek-20230907195134
comment: Fun canyon with some really scenic rappels. A landslide has occurred at the base of the last rappel, which may provide a new exit, did not explore too far as there are also ground wasps making their home in the dirt. Definitely bring a change of clothes for the hike out if taking the long route.

title: Conditions:Fir Creek-20240707214452
comment: Fir Creek just as pretty as always. All anchors in good condition. Flow was high enough that some of the hanging potholes were obscured from view, increasing the hazard of stepping/falling into them by mistake. The water was quite pushy at the top of the final rappel. Took the longer approach up Vance Creek for the first time and preferred it over the "shortcut" exit with the thorny bushwhack.

title: Conditions:Fir Creek-20240830190924
comment: Great day in Fir! The cave sequence with the hanging pothole is beautiful. No notable challenges. All anchors in good condition. We bushwhacked and made it from the last rappel to our cars in 49 mins.

title: Conditions:Fir Creek-20240903204247
comment: Fir was super fun and probably the best creek from the weekend. Too bad its so short. We paired it with Rock and had a fun day! I updated the shortcut exit. It only took 20 mins to climb out to the road.

title: Conditions:Fir Creek-20250708172920
comment: Scouted the canyon ahead of a training day. Took the "2024 shortcut" exit. Bushy and strenuous, but creek-to-road was only 25mins at a slow pace. Returned the next day and took the Vance Creek exit. Gorgeous hike out along the river bed, and a good consistent gradient up the road. Hard to tell which exit to prefer - think it depends on time, weather & team.

title: Conditions:Fir Creek-20250724200641
comment: The water in Fir is noticeably colder than Rock and Whitehorse.

title: Conditions:Fisher Creek-20201025051347
comment: Likely a first descent of Fisher Creek as we found no other anchors or signs of passage. Had low expectations on water levels, but it turned out to be surprisingly good after the area received an inch of rain the night before. Creek was a little overgrown suggesting that Fisher runs low during the summer months. Spring and fall are likely the best. Lost count of the number of rappels, but the creek remains pretty action packed until after the final rappel sequence.

title: Conditions:Fisher Creek-20260714032012
comment: We had a great low-key adventure in Fisher Creek. When in low flow a3 conditions, this canyon is suitable for beginners led by competent leaders, but easily would push a4/a5 in moderate or higher flows. Given the low-flow conditions, we only needed to rap about 4 of the drops. Our group decided not to leapfrog, and we ended up rebuilding each of the 4 anchors where we rapped - we were not rushing. Based on the state of the webbing I don't think anyone had been in-canyon for at least 3 years. I hope our bush-whacking benefits someone. The shuttle makes for a good pay-to-play ratio, especially for dusting off the cobwebs at the beginning of a season. Things to note: -LAMAR should scout the rope pulls thoroughly- we were fine, but this could be a challenge for some given the vegetation. -Log jams were unstable in a few locations and rock was the typical amount of slick of the PNW. -The difficulty and risk of this canyon increases exponentially at moderate and higher flows. There is ample evidence that this canyon carries high flood flow in winter/spring. I also witnessed a potentially nasty sieve on DCR that could form at high flows. (I believe after the technical section?) -Entry and exit could use re-taping. Forgot my survey tape. Note that the loggers are using pink tape, so maybe bring a different color! -The canyon is mostly shaded and can be a little windy, so if going with a 4/3mm bring a splash jacket etc. -Lots of downclimbing!

title: Conditions:Fools Canyon (Olympic Peninsula)-20200922032229
comment: Easy approach with option to shuttle, easy exit. A fun nontechnical float day. Water was noticeably colder than other canyons in the area the same weekend. Bring extra flotation. Very slick creek walking with ankle biters. Excellent narrows and carved out walls. Some big jump potential. Brought a rope just in case but didn’t need it. Ran this the day after Rock Creek, made a good combo.

title: Conditions:Fools Canyon (Olympic Peninsula)-20210628004926
comment: Solo trip to escape the forecasted 110F heat. Despite the baking sun, the water was still cold. Not sure this route should ever be done without one! As previously reported the creek-bed rocks are as slippery as hell, and just getting to the start of the canyon proper made me wish for some hiking poles! Given the lack of recent reports, I carried a short rope & harness, which remained in the bag all day. Once in the canyon there were no problematic obstacles to report. Some of the constrictions are pushy (but if I wasn't solo I'd probably call them "fun" instead). All of the rapids can be bypassed. I don't think any of them were jump-able (I lowered myself into some expecting to find a deep pool, but found thigh-deep water instead). However where the water runs slower & deeper lots of the canyon walls looked like great jumping spots. The large land-slide damn is still in place and can be passed easily DCL. I couldn't find the drainage marking the start of the suggested exit route, so bushwhacked directly uphill from the exit of the final pool. A little steep in places, but its not that long until the slope dramatically levels-off to a plateau, with only a short mellower climb up to the road from there. Taking the canyon cautiously, plus exit faff, and hiking the road, was 3.5 hrs total.

title: Conditions:Fools Canyon (Olympic Peninsula)-20220926225130
comment: Approach was easy and short, water was flowing strong and cold. A mellow creek stomp up until the two rivers meet and it drops into the first narrows. A lot of slick scrambles around sets of rapids, but very doable. Water was very cold and I was grateful for my 6/5mm wetsuit. The narrows in this canyon are deep and spectacular and full of deep, crystals clear water. the crux of the trip was the rock and deadfall damn at the end. With the flow as high as it was the DCL down climb did not look like a wise option and I opted for a down climb through and over the deadfall which was its own adventure. Once over that it was an easy walk/float to the end with a great spot to do some jumps from the side right before the canyon totally widens out again. Any sign of an exit trail is totally gone since this canyon is rarely travelled and I opted for a straight up hill bushwhack to the road.

title: Conditions:Fools Canyon (Olympic Peninsula)-20220926234424
comment: Flow was moderate, but water still cold. Rapids were easier to navigate around, but the down climb around the dam at the end was still sketchy, but more doable than in high flow. Unfortunately lost the rope and rope bag I'd brought along just in case under a rock in the first pool on the down climb, and decided it wasn't worth risking entering the hydraulic to retrieve it, and decided to go back later when the flow was lower and see it if it had reappeared. Let my mistake serve as a warning that although this canyon is nontechnical, the dam at the end creates some dangerous hydraulics that require caution.

title: Conditions:Fulton Creek-20200720165839
comment: Excellent half day canyon approximately equal distance from Portland and Seattle. Be aware of dirt bikes in the area. Easy entry, no shuttle required, rough exit up a steep hillside through overgrown slash cut debris. Very slick creek snot walking with many knee deep holes so watch your ankles. Lots of floating, small jump spots along the canyon. Handline recommended for final rap.

title: Conditions:Fulton Creek-20220925223643
comment: Ran Upper Fulton prior to an exploration of the lower part of the creek. New shortcut route is nice and drops you in just above the technical section. A new tree has fallen across the creek atop R3 which makes for a much better anchor in a non-exposed position. The rappel itself is slightly longer. Beta has been updated. Water was low, but still fun. We continued on down the creek to run Lower Fulton.

title: Conditions:Fulton Creek-20221030195744
comment: Halloween canyon! One week of rain and back to fun level (moderate to mod-low), but cold. Team member in drysuit was the happiest. Fun shortie outing in the Olympics. Replaced anchor at R2 as bacon is always better on the breakfast table. Used nearby Interrorem Cabin as basecamp.

title: Conditions:Fulton Creek-20230712052424
comment: Beautiful canyon, especially the amphitheater at R1.

title: Conditions:Fulton Creek-20240707005237
comment: A lovely canyon. The bolt on R1 is in good condition. We replaced the webbing on R2 and R3, and moved the R2 anchor to a different spot, to the root ball of a tree, to make it a cleaner pull. There are spots for some optional short jumps at the bottom of R1 and R2, check the depth as it is not very deep. The bottom of R2 could has an undercut at the far end of the pool, which could be a problem in higher flow. Great day overall.

title: Conditions:Fulton Creek-20240823185200
comment: Pretty solid canyon, light flow

title: Conditions:Fulton Creek-20240903192435
comment: Full Fulton was fun and totally worth running. We went a day early to scout the lower exit and work on the trail. We found a game trail and did a lot of cutting and cleaning up. The exit is steep and way less bushwacking than the original beta. I'll update the kml map. I wore a shorty wetsuit with a rain jacket and was fine with temp. Our time was 1.5 hours for upper Fulton and 4.5 hours middle, lower, and exit. Total was around 6 hours.

title: Conditions:Fulton Creek-20260701044402
comment: Great lil section, ran full Fulton, upper section was missing anchors, even found one log with webbing on it In The middle of the creek. I would not run just upper, best to make full day and do entire canyon! Rocks are quite slippery and dangerous. Rope pulls seem tricky by had no issues with rope retrieval.

title: Conditions:Galena Creek-20250103014911
comment: An amazing winter canyon! Roadside, low elevation, easy approach/exit and with two big, fun waterfalls with great flow and rebelays giving fantastic lines through the flow. We were joking that this is 3 stars in summer and 5 stars in winter, and it's really true. Approach took us 25 minutes with minor routefinding and exit took us 15 minutes. A very enjoyable day!

title: Conditions:Galena Creek-20250127154854
comment: Fun creek! Rocks and trees covered with thick layer of ice made approaches to anchors and descents challenging.

title: Conditions:Galena Creek-20250209190008
comment: Having such a short approach and exit makes this a wonderful winter canyon. We couldn't find anchors at R1 or R4/P2 (lots of snow/ice) so we rigged retrievable. The ice and snow made the approach, exit, and climb up to R4 more difficult but made for a fun and character building experience. Would highly recommend!

title: Conditions:Galena Creek-20250417051023
comment: Much different canyon when not frozen! Great flow right now…R2 has a rooster tail at the top in current flows. Exciting Raps all the way down. All anchors were is good working order…there is a fallen tree across R3 just before the water goes over the edge which is kind of a nuisance. If your in need of a spare tire there are about 50 at the bottom of the last rap. Pretty terrible. Beautiful canyon though!

title: Conditions:Galena Creek-20250522084238
comment: Afternoon start during a rainy day made for some fun flow. All anchors good. The hike up from the bottom is steep up loose terrain for the firet bit, so be careful.

title: Conditions:Galena Creek-20250729015949
comment: We only ran the lower section and spent much time at the bottom of R3 assisting with something unrelated. The retaining wall anchor was very loose and had to be tightened; all others were good. Unfortunately, the trash is starting to smell and is quite noticeable without higher waterflow flushing it out. The Rock Creek of the Gifford. :(

title: Conditions:Galena Creek-20260328085659
comment: R3b (coming out of the culvert) is very spicy at this flow, honestly safer to downclimb behind the branchy cluster dcl before getting on rappel (take the rope with you, possibly even route around the cluster as a redirect) than trying to skirt in between the cluster and the torrent. R4b (bolts dcr) be mindful of the log halfway down the pitch in the middle the watercourse, stay dcl of it but don’t step between it and the rock, some chance of entrapment Skip the last drop, don’t even look, the sight of all those tires robs some of the magic of such a banger canyon. We took track of approach and exit, a map will be submitted. Otherwise the pitch lengths given in the beta are all longer than they are in actuality, but considering all the two pitch drops this is for the best. Get in there while it’s still flowing with spice, snow in the upper catchment won’t last long. Also, although I’m newer at canyoning and respect the work put in by the first descent team, in no world is this a class B slash A2 canyon. It’s best run in the off and early season. Off/early season flows can be expected to be A3 at a minimum. French V4 seems appropriate, whether it deserves an ACA technical rating of 4 is iffy, since reaching the rebelay stations (2b/3b/4b) from their respective 1st pitch anchors (2a/3a/4a) isn’t complicated, even in high flow. I’m open to a counter take and being corrected on it.

title: Conditions:Galena Creek-20260427015210
comment: High flow day in Galena. We ran R2, which was fine, and R3, which was fairly punishing even after we used the redirect. We took a dirty dry line far DCR to avoid R4 because the flow and the log in the water course appeared dangerous. We left after that, skipping R5. The routes to the anchors are showing some wear and there might be some concern about erosion soon if this creek keeps getting run frequently.

title: Conditions:Goforth Creek-20160815231821
comment: Encountered one less rappel than canyoneering northwest suggested, lots of water and not too technical. was able to swim most of the .9 mile exit from the East Fork Humptulips River. Don't forget your National Forest pass.

title: Conditions:Goforth Creek-20201005023302
comment: Parked at the drop-in and descended Go-Forth. Only encountered three rappels. Final rappel into the Humptulips River is very pretty. Water then quadruples if not more. Flow prediction for this creek should really be the river as it's the crux. Was able to float / swim downstream 0.8mi. Had to hop out to circumnavigate some narrow bits. Shuttle would be nice to skip the 1.6mi hike back up the road, but it's not that bad. National Forest pass not required.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210508232313
comment: The canyon was not run in this flow. This report was created for tracking water levels.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210711223513
comment: The canyon was fully run on July 26. The team took 2 other days to establish sections of the canyon. This is likely the most technical canyon in Washington currently as there are multiple traverse lines (with intermediate points) to avoid flow and hydraulics and requires careful footwork. In this flow, your team should be comfortable with these techniques and high water features like hydraulics. The canyon has very fun features but also very technical/serious sections that require respect. Other than those warnings, this canyon is very, very fun. It has a multitude of slides -- a feature not too common in the PNW. 5-star canyon.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210712182415
comment: Ran the entire canyon from where the road crosses. The big drop halfway from the road to where the classic R1 (Jump/ Slide) is was neat but I was fine doing it once as the rest of the canyon is much, much better. Be mindful of the rope as we did coreshot a rope on this rap. Stay in the flow to avoid this from happening. Half way from the big drop to the classic R1 is where the fun begins. Plenty of fun slides that could be added to the eventual run. That being said, the classic route is great. It’s like mineral meets Lost meets Davis with a side of Big. I know, sounds wild. The run is not for the faint of heart as the hydraulics are real and people need to be mindful of their teammates at all times. Also, check depths routinely. It’s easy to forget when you’re having so much fun.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210715193903
comment: Flow was slightly sporty, but we were able to walk into the problematic hydraulic (where the traverse line keeps you away) and stand in it - was chest deep and slightly pushy. If not prepared and you come off your feet it could churn you up a bit, esp. in higher flow, and be hard to stand back up. I like slides so thought it was great, even though it's not a committing canyon. There are two legit vertical slides plus another 25-foot low-angle slide that's noteworthy. Everything is fully bolted up now and ready.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210719043923
comment: Ran Hager for the 4th time. Water levels are lower. Jumps and slides are still In but noticeable touching bottom more but isn’t an issue yet. Great day.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210728023206
comment: NOTE: Google lies. You need to enter on NF48, not NR21, (the crossover road is gated from 21) We did Big, davis and Hager over 3 days. This canyon is amazing, best I have done in this state. S2 we repeated, jumped, and repeated, beautiful. The lower section is very technical and need solid people setting the ropes and traverse lines. The water flow maybe lower, but I thought it was perfect. We were able to get into the flow almost the entire time, and it was pushy. Several slide have solid reversals, and I did a couple unintentional back rolls when trying to get out. Baby throw bags are nice. The approach and exit are vague, so we flagged and tried to make a path in. Our flagged approach ended upstream 200yrd of S1, but it has a great changing area and their were several nice warm up Slides. We flagged the exit route, straight up the bank and right back to the car, huffing and puffing in our wetsuits for 20 min..

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210802191435
comment: Still riding an amazing high from Hager. The most fun canyon I've done in the PNW, hands down. All fun and giggles until oh shit it's time to pay attention. The beta and trip reports do not overstate the serious technical challenges of this canyon. You need to be in there with strong leadership and a solid team. Hager was flowing a4+ and was noticeably higher than last Sunday. Hydraulics are not quite keepers, although some might hug you for a turn or two before they let you go. 😈 We did not enter the one below R10 which looked serious and like one to avoid. There is a decent social trail developing on the approach. It is easy to navigate yourself to the beginning of the canyon (5 mins). At the exit, follow a game trail between the creek and the clearcut until you see your cars parked at the hairpin, then scramble 10 yards up to your cars (15 mins). Someone didn't find these trails and flagged a different and less pleasant route on the approach and exit. We removed as much orange flagging as we could find. Major shoutout to the first descent team for finding and opening such an incredible canyon! Hager deserves 5 stars and is destined to become a PNW classic.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210813022406
comment: Ran Hager and Davis today. Quick updates. Log in R1 pool has been removed. Could be useful to check depth or find a new anchor to go in flow. Removed logs after S2 and pool just before the triple slides. Fun day. WATCH OUT FOR THE YELLOW JACKETS ON THE EXIT. There is a nest and they were active. We were fortunate.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210815202406
comment: Nice day on Hager. Oddly the flow was higher than it was a few weeks back at the end of July but not by a lot. Partner got very cold (3/4mm during a morning run) so we decided to exit after the three slides. There’s a pretty definitive trail now from that section back to the parking.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210816041745
comment: Hat's off to the first descent team who opened the canyon this year. Where is all that water coming from? The current map looks like one should drop in directly off the road, but that will put you at the top of R3. Looks like one should head up hill for a few hundred feet, then drop into the creek for the official start at S1. Realized our error and backtracked. We set up a short shuttle and pioneered a new exit route over to the lower switchback on FR 48. Lower exit added +1 rappel into a short dark slot (lots of fallen trees overhead) and then exiting out DCL up over the ridge. There appears to be some more interesting water features below our exit point. Easy hike out - mostly flat/downhill. At the lower switchback, there appears to be a flagged trail heading east that we didn't have time to explore... Does it go all the way to the creek? That might make a pretty marvelous exit.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210819011837
comment: A great day with a group of 7. Amazingly consistent flow since over a month prior. We ran R10 as a slide, with a teammate stationed at the bottom to help anyone who might have become stuck in the recirculating hydraulic. I would not recommend doing this slide in any higher flow than moderate. Doubled up several single bolts in the lower technical section. A great day overall. Done in time for an early dinner and drive home.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210822000819
comment: Amazing canyon with lots of great slides and fun rappels. Water level was a fun moderate flow but these desert Canyoneers would not want to run it any higher…lol. Props to the team that opened the canyon up and made that flowing hallway safe to run!! 😍💦👍

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210907073823
comment: Another great day in Hager. Water levels pretty comparable to several weeks ago -- not much change. Hydraulics would still grab your feet, but pools were relatively easy to escape. Continued downcanyon for +1 rappel (50ft from log anchor), then exited at the cairn for short 5-10min walk to the lower switchback for a shuttle. If the canyon was extended a bit (couple small rappels, maybe another slide; might need a bolt or two?), there's a really nice walk-out DCL (complete with switchbacks!) just downstream of the tributary that comes in DCR. Easy walking through open forest over to the road.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20220626034048
comment: Great day in Hager. Added the seasonal QuickDraw to the final sequence. Please leave it and we will get it when the season ends. Flows were very high. We avoided on potential strainer by exiting DCL. Very easy. Also avoided the 2nd traverse line sequence as getting to the station proved problematic due to flow + very slippery conditions. Again DCL for a few feet. All other jumps besides S6 were good to go.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20220801021528
comment: Excellent conditions. Pushy slides but all hydraulics were easily escapable. We exited after the Upper Section so most of the group could catch a plane home.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20220808171449
comment: Another fun day at Hager. All anchors are in great condition, all slides are in, but hydraulics while not dangerous were strong at times. We tried the alternate shuttle exit option after R15, and probably would just prefer to exit after R14 next time as the route finding was proved to be difficult and more time consuming than hiking back to the drop-in parking spot.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20220821022031
comment: Fun day at Hager. Water was colder than Big or Davis, and definitely more challenging than them. So minor hydraulics in the canyon, nothing that caused worry. All anchors in good condition.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20220906055902
comment: Hager is always fascinating for the sheer volume of water pumping through it. If anything, water levels seemed slightly higher in the last technical section. We set up a shuttle for the quick exit. Will look at flagging or improving the lower exit route next time, but it's a 5min hike out requiring minimal navigation. We also added a new anchor to R9. Sidebar note: a well-meaning member of our team inadvertently cleaned the redirect on R12. It is now taped to a tree with a sign just up the trail on the hike in. If you run the canyon, please return it to it's proper location. Thanks! (Do check conditions before you go as a new forest fire has broken out east of Packwood.)

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20230620010533
comment: Ran most of the canyon besides the last traverse and narrows. We were welcomed by 4 new logs which we promptly removed. Al we replaced two smaller stainless links with larger ones. We jumped or slid everything minus S6. Conditions were high and there wasn’t much to worry about with folks who have done the canyon more than 10x total. If you go after us please be ready to either tighten bolts or replace with a drill. We’ve never had to yank 4+ logs out on the season opener so who knows what happened downstream this past winter.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20230627004414
comment: We descended Hager in its entirety. Thanks to the crew before us for removing the logs. The anchors in the final traverse and narrows were all in good shape, we tightened what we could. The redirect on R12 is still not there. The water in the final section was sporty and engaging.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20230716010323
comment: Fun day. Nothing special to note. Fun was had.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20230723000337
comment: Another great day in Hager. Took our time and enjoyed the slides and myriad clear pools. Found a dead deer floating a pool at the bottom of lower technical section. Exited via well-marked shuttle route. One mystery solved: the trail used for the exit (both directions) was built (likely illegally) by dirt bike riders.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20230804163744
comment: Managed to get a run in behind the Rondy groups without seeing anyone. Just as fun as always but a bit on the cold side with a 3/4 wetsuit. There appears to now be two redirects about 10 feet apart on R12 DCL since the last time I ran this canyon, maybe not totally necessary but makes the rappel very easy with both. We dropped in above the beta drop in point and it has a few fun small slides. All bolts are good though and everything goes currently.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20230814223939
comment: Low-Moderate flow. I removed a handline that was left between S2 and R1. It is behind a tree right at the entrance trail of the canyon (very easy to find). It is unacceptable to leave fixed equipment like this, one can go back after a run with ease and remove. Also - R12 has 2 redirects now. This adds minimal protection and should be removed, IMO.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20230831002151
comment: Fun trip to see the troll. Anchors in good shape. Only issue was the smell and guts of a rotting deer carcass at the bottom of R14 - with guts scattered all the way to R15.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20230831144159
comment: Fun canyon, fun team. The troll lives up to his reputation.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20240518233644
comment: Flow was high but still a lot of fun in the upper section. It was a little warmer than expected in 5/4 wetsuits. A large cedar fell from above the anchor onto the R1 ledge and into the pothole. Unfortunately that tree is not going anywhere anytime soon. Added a note to beta. Quite a bit of debris in the outlet and above R2 as well. Next group in should bring a saw and continue the cleanup we started around the anchor. Exited at S8 due not having a big enough team to set safety. Walked down canyon right to R10 anchor. Getting to R10 anchor could need protection from being swept into keeper hydraulic. Looking down stream into the dancefloor it was rowdy but we would have continued with a bigger team.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20240528233040
comment: A great day in the upper+middle section. High flow added frequent spice, but in a strong & experienced group it was very much enjoyed. I've improved the description of the R1 slide, and dialed-down the recent warning about it. Sliding R1 has been rarely done over rapping from the tree. The last report caused a bit of confusion as to which slide was blocked (people were worried it was the iconic entrance slide). Hopefully it makes sense to everyone now. All other slides and jumps go, albeit with extra precautions regarding whitewater hazards. The group were dressed for cold conditions, but found it to be surprisingly mild compared to Tule the day before. Exited after S10 as planned, to avoid the slower & colder lower section.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20240701194125
comment: Full run from a little above S1 through R14. Flow was great. Anchors in good shape, both redirects present on R12, jumps and slides currently clean.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20240714221750
comment: Super fun day in Hager. First time for all 3 of us. Did a couple slides an extra time, and took our time examining each problem. Nothing too difficult, but the hydraulics sent us spinning a few times. Not as cold as expected. We exited up the original non-shuttle route.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20240806150417
comment: Replaced webbing/link on R1. Tightened bolt R7. Removed R8 single bolt, this bolt has been 1/2 way in/leveraging for the last 2 season. Webbing/link added DCL for more logical rap.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20240807041544
comment: Beautiful canyon with a fun team, the first time through for several of us. We made improvements to a few anchors, including: - R2, moved anchor higher to make it easier to get on rappel - R8, ''

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20240909064653
comment: Brought some new friends to meet the troll. Pleasantly surprised there was decent flow today. Water felt particularly cold today. Hydraulic in S6/R7 pool was a touch grabby. Boil/keeper at R10 was still gnarly. The other major hydraulics in the canyon were benign. Appreciate the new webbing on R2 & R8 but kinda miss that janky old bolt.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20240926170845
comment: Loved this narrow, intense little canyon! One team member was concerned about the look of the hydraulics and escaped to the road about halfway down. But 2 of us completed it, and the hydraulics we encountered were only mildly pushy. Close study of great beta on this site helped us handle everything just fine. AMAZING place!

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250212053504
comment: A cold spell plus the driest January in 40 years has Hager running with surprisingly (and delightfully!) reasonable flow. Beautiful, fluffy snow blanketed the trees, logs and banks but we found only a little ice in the canyon. It was VERY cold! This would not be a good choice for anyone newer to winter canyoning. All the usual slides and jumps are clean and all anchors are in good condition with both deviations in place. Car-to-car time of 3.5 hours for the upper and lower canyon.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250324184640
comment: Water gauged Hager today. It was running fast but likely doable for an advanced team who had done it before. We did not descend the canyon but we did walk the rim and pull a sizable log out that would have likely become a strainer later in the season.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250502200126
comment: There is some new (relocated) wood in play in Hager. Scout ALL jumps and slides before committing, especially when on your first seasonal descent. Flow was high but manageable when not wearing ￼packs and having personal flotation. Our group had wetsuits and dry suits with PFDs and vests. Having a swiftwater mindset of rope management, flotation and downstream safety made the day much less stressful. 😎

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250505025018
comment: Fun day! We took out after the triple pools and skipped the lower section - mostly out of laziness (conditions would have been fine) No new wood (or other hazards) to speak of compared to last year

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250505163111
comment: Slides and jumps are all clean, anchors in good shape. High flow some slides will “washing machine” you before spitting you out! Removed several logs from the upper section that could have been problematic, large one moved into pool with others above R2. Exited after 3 slides due to time but checked redirects which are present.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250616181536
comment: Surprisingly moderate conditions in most of the canyon for this early in the season. Lower section running closer to mod-high.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250617184630
comment: Great day to have swift water training! Thanks to Sam and the crew! The only thing I have to report is the water is really really cold even on a 95 deg day!

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250729013058
comment: This was the 2nd half of a Davis-Hager run. All slides/jumps in the beta are good. We did temporarily stick our rope on the log at R2 but recovered it. If not pulled quickly as the rope exits from the quicklink, the rope will drop into the log and get stuck. We recovered the cut rope of another teams at that same spot so take care on the pull. At R12, the 2nd deviation accessorry cord is starting to show wear. We didn't have any accessorry cord on us so the next team to come through should examine and consider replacing this. All other anchors are in good condition.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250808150432
comment: Time reflects upper, middle, and lower. First time through the canyon - spent a fair amount of time taking photos, depth checking pools, etc...beautiful canyon! Very cold water. We all had 5+ mm setups and were still a bit chilled at times (we also descended the canyon in the evening and air temps today were lower than average for this time of year).

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250820003008
comment: Amazing day in Hager after some rain charged the creek up a little bit. Nothing scary, probably just more fun! All the slides and jumps are in good shape. Several of us slid the whole Dance Floor! Hopefully the beta for that will get updated soon.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250822151534
comment: We had a great time playing in the Hager water park. All the slides/jumps were in. The dance floor was so fun we climbed back up it to do it again since it wasn't slippery at all.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250822171936
comment: Great day running Hager for the first time. We were in 3/4mm suits and were feeling the cold despite it being a fairly warm day.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20250927230127
comment: Water levels are still pretty low, but all the slides and jumps are still super fun. The hydraulics are all gentle and playful at this time, but that'll probably change pretty quickly when the rain comes in this week. Overall a great day if a little chilly as always in Hager. Don't forget that extra neoprene or rain jacket for this one.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20251021210819
comment: Hager today was cold!! Bring neoprene caps and gloves. Hydraulics were in play.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20260526033922
comment: Great day! Note: the flow was high but still a4. The upper section and exit have changed quite a bit. No log at the big opener slide, but it’s actually easier and more fun without the log. Debris has cleared from the pool and pothole below the opening slide, and R1 now goes as a slide! A very forceful slide, and only a few tested it, some of whom touched bottom lightly (its bedrock) so slide at your own risk. The low-angle bumpy slide at the end of the upper section is now a log downclimb. All slides were checked and clear for sliding/jumping except the narrow recirculating pool above the dance floor - the team used the traverse line to bypass that pool, so that has not been checked A large landslide at the exit makes the original exit impassable. Recommendation is to continue one more rappel and a few short drops, and take an exit canyon left that sustainably takes you to the established trail.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20260608072456
comment: Baller full run of hager as last day of swift water training. Pools are huggy and spinny but easily escapable with adequate neoprene and decent technique. Half of us wore drysuits, the other half wore medium/thick wetsuits and splash tops. No one got *too* cold but all were grateful to the guides for increasing the pace from instruction/scenario mode to team/gtfo mode when we hit lower hager as chills were beginning to creep in. Amazing, in a sad way, to see the massive landslide now complicating the exit from lower. Sending confidant scramblers ahead to set a hand line is helpful.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20260617145739
comment: Amazing as always! Did a quick run in the heat if the day which made the cold water feel great. Was interesting to see some of the changes with more slides and some filled in.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20260620004523
comment: Amazing day in Hager! Drop slide instead of R1 is great but land flat.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20260711022931
comment: Great day in hager. The big slide goes. 2nd to last drop also goes. Water was chilly but not bad. Ran into another team of 3 and merged forces.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20260713041821
comment: Decent canyon with quick approach/exit. Canyon is very short with shallow carved walls. Doesnt have the epic grandiose vibe of some of the others nearby. Very much a “waterpark” style canyon with a few jumps and slides if you are into that kind of thing. Raps are all just okay, not very memorable.. 5/4 wetsuit was plenty warm. We took the early non shuttle exit as we were told it is much quicker than the longer creek walk/shuttle option. Trails are all nicely cut in, no bushwack

title: Conditions:Hall Creek-20170810215011
comment: Fun, short, easy! We decided to ghost this, since alders on the channel boundaries are abundant and make for great fiddlestick anchors. The waterfalls are all low angle and easily rappelled in the water at lower flows. This canyon would make a very nice challenge with potential escapes throughout most of the canyon (if you're willing to bushwhack up a steep slope) during the fall or spring. Air temperature was decently warm, but the water is chilly. I started out in a shortie and changed into a full wetsuit midway through after getting a bit cold. The approach trail is very nicely built and makes this a very fast trip. Thanks to whoever did all the work to build up the Hall Creek and Zig Zag Trails! The first time I explored this area, they were just tiny boot paths.

title: Conditions:Hall Creek-20180721025036
comment: What this creek lacks in technical interest, it makes up for in convenience and esthetics (at moderate-low levels anyhow - I think much lower and it would be pretty boring and quite slimy). This is a fantastic beginner canyon - only 3 rappels, one in the water course adds some fun. Also, the approach is a trail the entire way and you have service the whole time. A number of fun little slides too. That said, its still a technical canyon so beginners should not take that lightly.

title: Conditions:Hall Creek-20200716164325
comment: Approach is pretty steep for the first half, without stopping to check the flow it took us 25 minutes to get to the top of Hall Creek falls. We walked for a few minutes up the creek to check it out. All anchors were in good condition. There is another rappel between #2 and #3 in the description, I'd call this #2.5. It may be downclimbable on the right, but given how slipper this canyon is, I would not recommend it. I think this is a 5 rappel creek. #4 anchor was difficult to find, all black against black rocks in the water, just FYI. We put a new ring on it. An anchor for rappel #4 was set up from a a tree on DCR rather than on the tree stump in mid-channel. The water is low, and you only get exposed up to your waist and hardly spend anytime in the water. This could be done with no or very thin wetsuit when the sun is right above the canyon. We used a 3/2 regular and 2mm shorty, started our descent at approximately 7pm. The water is very cold, but air temp was around 74 degrees.

title: Conditions:Hall Creek-20220717063736
comment: Upper part of the creek is slippery as usual. Lower part has a couple rappels with nice flow, and is beautiful as always. All anchors in great condition, including a couple with nice new black webbing (thanks, Becca!)

title: Conditions:Hall Creek-20230806184621
comment: Low flow. Very slick rock

title: Conditions:Hall Creek-20241107010637
comment: Slippery as to be expected and flowing steadily. Replaced the webbing and quick link on the dry rappel for R5.

title: Conditions:Hinkle Tinkle Creek-20230604222206
comment: It looks like locals may visit some of the trail-side falls occasionally, but, otherwise, no signs of other visitors were encountered, so this may be a FD. We had low expectations, so were pleasantly surprised at what a nice little creek it turned out to be. Pretty much an ideal creek for beginners and almost worth a visit alone just to see the bedrock. Would have been more fun with some more water -- need to visit earlier in the year next time. A worthy day out.

title: Conditions:Hinkle Tinkle Creek-20260427030350
comment: No other anchors were found, so this was probably a first descent of Lower Hinkle. Found a nice trail leading from the road pretty much right to the top of R8. Quite a bit of woody debris in the lower canyon. Bushwhack out was relatively easy.

title: Conditions:Hinkle Tinkle Creek-20260524235217
comment: Returned to lower HT with a friend new to canyoning. All the anchors were in good shape, since we just paced them all last month. We exited above R13. A short bushwhack uphill DCL brings to you to a road grade and an easy trail to the top of the ridge. After that swack out via the same directions in the canyon description.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20180617140311
comment: A fun day! The first 1/4 mile of the approach trail was quite overgrown (and thorny!). Because of that, the trail was not at all obvious, but still discernible. After that it cleared up and was easy to follow. It was very helpful to have the map downloaded on gaia for finding it though. On R5, we used a 300' and a pull cord for the first pitch, and found when we pulled the rope that both ends of the 300' would have made it to the anchor for the 2nd pitch (with only about 5' to spare). So, we think that first pitch may be more like 145'. We also thought it might be fun to do the full waterfall in one rappel, and not pitch it out - it would keep you in the flow more. Might make for a terrible pull though. Plus it would necessitate a lot of rope. Overall it was a fun, esthetic creek.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20180806181911
comment: Took a slightly lower line on the bushwhack to drop into the creek. Would have been fun with more water, but a nice mellow day. Creek is pretty action packed per not walking more than 5min between rappels.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20200726041804
comment: Prospect in the area didn't pan out, so we fell back on Hopkins as a backup plan. Someone's done trail maintenance since the last time I was there. Approach trail far less brushy. Approach hike is pretty mellow, then you get a great payoff with rappel after rappel in the creek... including the big 250ft+ one that will keep you on your toes. Cowlitz ranger station appears to have posted some nasty-grams regarding all the pipes and other bricka-brac near the bottom... tho' all that junk has probably been there for years at this point.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20210516201430
comment: Flow was high and very forceful. More like pushy a4. Creek crossing even got our attention in certain parts. I uploaded a screen shot of the current snowpack with this trip report for reference. It has been a dry week with warm temps, so the snow was melting quickly - highs in the mid-70's on the day of our trip.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20210608200133
comment: Great conditions on Hopkins. Water was high but just low enough not to be too challenging. It was only pushy where the creek constricts. Felt like a totally different creek vs the dry summer conditions.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20210620195047
comment: First time down it, in an experienced group who'd done it before. As last report, only pushy where the creek constricts. Elsewhere the flow eases off very quickly. First rap is probably the crux flow-wise, giving you little in the way of warm-up! Replaced webbing and quick links in various places, including on the multi-pitch (did it in 3 pitches). There's definitely fun to be had here in the next month or two before it dries out. (Set difficulty to "special challenges just due to the multipitch rigging). Watch out for ticks!

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20220403055546
comment: We did R5 in two pitches with a 200’ rope from the tree at the edge of the drop. Creek was very slippery which became tiresome with all the low-angle rappels. South-facing, open canyon gave us sun almost all canyon which was delightful on a cold day. All anchors in good condition.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20220705151251
comment: Flow was not pushy and perfect for our group. Creek walking and rappels are super slippery!! We did R5 in 2 stages, 60 feet to the hanging anchor then 190 to the bottom. Hopkins is great, I love that all the rappels are close together without much creek walking. Run it before it runs out of water!

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20230130185047
comment: Fun day. Plan B. Noticed plenty of random and extra webbing. I guess this is a practice beginner canyon which is fine but let’s clean it up a bit. Noticed bright nylon cord holding up some black webbing.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20230529170543
comment: Just about the right level for Hopkins. Plenty of wet stuff to choose your own adventure in, but nothing which had to be avoided. Zipped packs on R1 so people could enjoy the biggest stuff. Did R5 in two pitches, which provided a lovely ~200' rap line in the water. Still damn slippery in places.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20230605020038
comment: First rap was the funnest and only one you could get an overhead dousing on the way down. R6 could be done down the gut in the flow from a small tree about 20 feet back from the other anchor. The other rappels were mostly out of the flow. An impressive old growth tree DCL just below R5.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20230608034117
comment: Beware: R9's anchor was a wrap 2 pull 1 (to my memory) set high (eye height or higher) on a thigh-sized tree DCL. The first rappeler (avg weight) in low flow with proper rappel technique shook the tree back and forth a little bit. After he disconnected, I moved the webbing down to reduce leverage. I was the next one down so I did not get to watch the tree for movement but it supposedly was fine afterward. The lowered anchor made pulling difficult. If I returned to the canyon, I'd likely want to replace the anchor, maybe making it a traverse to a better natural anchor (both stronger and positioned easier to pull the rope down). I wouldn't be surprised if someone tries to move the webbing up for a better line, so please be aware (or build a better anchor), especially if you anticipate a larger load (or higher flow). R9 is not that steep but it is slippery, so it is easy to fall and increase forces on the anchor.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20230618051934
comment: Super fun day in Hopkins despite tons of mosquitos. Team of 7 with 6 new to this creek. Great run that would have been even better with a bit higher flow. Some raps were very grippy while most were very slick.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20240506022711
comment: Very fun but very wet and cold day out. We bypassed a few rappels. There was a bit more blowdown than last year in the canyon including a lot at the base of the final rappel. The big sequence is good and actually has 4 anchors going all the way down. Could stay out of the flow for the multipitch.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20240506202950
comment: Steady rain was coming down all day, and we bypassed several rappels, mostly the ones where staying in the flow was mandatory and looked hazardous. The sequence down the main falls was quite spectacular and was the highlight of the trip. Anchors were in good shape.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20240513054158
comment: Outstanding fun day in the high flow. Bypassed R1, did all other rappels.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20240520023058
comment: Flow seems to have decreased a lot in the past week R1 was fun but pretty tame. All rappels were in, all anchors good.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20240707200023
comment: There are ticks on the approach, a member of our group was bit twice. Terrain used to access R7 is somewhat exposed and eroded, suggest using traverse line in order to access the rappel anchor. There was also a fresh log jam at bottom of R9. Beta has been updated. Overall fun day. :)

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20250408154244
comment: Lower flow than expected, fun water level, good flavor but no spice. Replaced hanging rebelay anchor at R5. Repelled from the new traverse anchor at R7, better line to stay in flow and avoids the awkward start at the original anchor. R9 cleaned some downed wood out of the chute at the top of the drop but a handsaw would be helpful to finish the job. Spent some time clearing loose debris from the logjam and the bottom was f the rappel. We got of rope and pulled from the top of the logjam to avoid rope tangle in the logjam. Easy enough to down climb from the top of the jam.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20250408184045
comment: Hopkins has great flow and is super slippery! I'll update the beta with items of note.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20250626194936
comment: Great day out. Low flow and sunny. All anchors in good condition.

title: Conditions:Hopkins Creek-20260116043148
comment: Flowing high, anchors all in great condition. Skipped R3 due to debris in the lower section of falls and in the pool making rope entrapment probability high. Lots of erosion and small tree branches in the creek. A few rock and tree strainers are present mid canyon. Easily avoidable however pull rope quickly to not get a rope stuck after being pulled down creek. Only encountered some rockfall on the big rappel at the 2nd station. Fun canyon in current water lvls.

title: Conditions:Hughes Gorge-20230908171017
comment: Quick evening scouting of this drainage. Probably some of tallest canyon walls I have seen on the peninsula. Only encountered small drops/ logjams that were easy scrambles

title: Conditions:Hughes Gorge-20250803184203
comment: Explore the most of the rest of the Hughes Creek. Lower section is fairly neat and worth doing, took about 1.25 hours from the car to get to. We explored to where marked on the map, took about 5hrs to get to that point (probably not worth repeating). Will probably bring going back to do the one rappels and maybe scout that other drainage that drops in

title: Conditions:Koda Creek-20200822021545
comment: Likely first descent of Koda. Weren't entirely sure what we were getting into, but turned out to have some quality falls. Would fun to do it again with more water. We dropped a shuttle car down by the campground, but ended out thrashing the 600ft back out and groveling up the headwall. Would have been safer to send the best climber out and rig a rope for others to climb. Headwall exit was the hardest part of the day. Nice half-day adventure.

title: Conditions:Koda Creek-20250610191628
comment: Downclimbed/slid R1, removed old torn up webbing from R2 tree and ghosted it. R4 was a fun rappel. The downclimb groove is a cool little narrows. Followed the creek down to the confluence with Paradise then walked up creek and combined with a descent of Paradise Creek and the creek walk exit to Wind River Rd. About a 10 hour day car to car with lots of sitting in the creek to cool down.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20200720004547
comment: Ran the full LeBar, tho' we might have dropped in early. Followed the old overgrown road grade to the first stream / slide, then thrashed down a steep slope through classic Olympic forest to the creek. Reasonably nice platform just above the creek which allows you to jumpstart the day by literally jumping in. Great creek, great water conditions. Exited to the bridge, startling a number of campers in the area.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20200823030935
comment: A fantastic 4th of July descent. Lurking on the old Canyoneering Northwest website, this canyon looked like it had some nice scenery. Did not disappoint. We explored both the upper and lower sections, which were not documented on the old website. While certainly low on the technical side, the canyon was quite beautiful and was well worth the trip. A great beginner canyon by midsummer.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20210627043710
comment: Dropped in a the upper entrance this time. A trail has been cleared all the way there. On arrival, don't go down the steep dirt slope, but instead take trail to your left down through the trees. Flow was great today, but spicy in the narrow sections. Replaced missing anchor at R1.
