One short drop somewhere below was kicking off a horizontal recirculation which took some effort to pass through. Hangers on R5 are both loose and spin, but the bolts seem sound. Lovely to drift in the long cool corridors of LeBar when it's 100 degrees F back in town. A great day out!

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20210705233536
comment: Great day! 80 degrees outside made floating leisurely and wonderful. Flow is slightly pushy in a few places but generally friendly. We dropped in a bit lower than the main drop-in - on the hiking trail, we dropped down the 2nd tributary creek that the trail crosses. It was a very easy, non-brushy creek, and allows you to skip what I think are an annoying 30 minutes of slippery creek walking. Props to Jake & Logan for finding this new drop-in! (added to the beta)

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20210719055848
comment: We parked at the berm and followed the trail to the 2nd creek crossing. Lots of floating for then next mile. I almost think you can walk in from the car parking spot and save 40 minutes of creek walking and floating. This canyon has some of the best floating sections, but after 6 hrs we were ready to be done. Very, very pretty, but pack your lunch ( we didn't and got hungry). Massive log jams with some slippery downclimbs. Good beta. All pools should be checked. Most are only 6 ft deep, and have hidden rocks.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20220705165632
comment: Dropped in at second drop-in point on the morning of July 4. Moderate flow and clear of debris until R1. R1 has a significant logjam (image attached, as seen from above R1). The original tat off the down log is no longer visible and the log seems to have shifted from the historical picture (Water guage image in beta is below R1). We felt that a rappel was possible off a natural anchor. The rappel would keep one out of flow in the upper section but the we didn't think it was possible to avoid the flow in the lower section. Be prepared to build your own anchor(s). We opted to not go further due the moderate flow, uncertainty of the rest of the water course and the skill/size of our team. Navigable for a intermediate team. We navigated back up the creek ~100 yard and escaped back up the hill slope to the parking lot start.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20230705152858
comment: A well spent holiday afternoon. Water was moderate, cold and canyon was a stunning gem. Found someone had placed a bolt on the rock knob DCL right above the first rappel. Also the unlinked bolts for the final rappel seem to have been removed or torn out and only a single bolt is there (you can see the holes left from previous bolts) Had to cut a few feet off a rope on the first rappel when the flow dragged the end under rocks at the bottom while pulling the rope, but had brought plenty of rope to finish the canyon thankfully.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20230706040441
comment: A great day out in this always beautiful creek. Our friends ran the canyon the day prior (7/2/23) and found that the log anchor on R1 had been washed away. They were prepared with a bolting kit and installed a single bolt DCL. Added a new R2 rap to the beta from a log jam after the Grotto rappel (it can be downclimbed but is inconvenient). Floods have taken out one of the bolts on the final rappel, leaving just a single. This bolt should be backed up again at some point, or potentially replaced with a V-thread as this section seems to see major flood events and anchors will continue to be damaged long term. If placing a bolt, please consider placing a removable bolt, such as a FIXE Triplex, so the holes can be re-used later if the bolts are damaged.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20230712054021
comment: This canyon has some unique features that make it special. If you're looking for a canyon on the Olympic Peninsula, this is a good one to consider. Waterflow was a good level for beginners. All in all a great day out. Jake posted some pertinent details on anchors on his report.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20230718101203
comment: Fun canyon. Water flow was forgiving and easy Little slide/shoot below deck project was fun

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20240628040516
comment: A cruiser river walk/float with annoying log jams which ends with 2 short but spectacular rappels in the flow we has today. Leader must do a flat jump to clear those boils and eddies. My partner got stuck in DCL eddy on last rap. Rescued with throw rope. Good times were had.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20240707190341
comment: A great day in Lebar, we were happy to be in there on this very hot day. The canyon saw some major flooding this season with some new logjam debris to navigate. The grotto below R1 has a new gravel bar that was not there prior. The last rappel still has a single 12mm Triplex bolt, which is sufficient for now. One of the empty holes around that bolt will fit a 55mm Triplex 12mm sleeve bolt if anyone has one (now discontinued unfortunately). The eddy beneath the final pool required strong swimming ability. We had a throw rope ready in case someone got stuck (see attached photo)

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20240715013159
comment: Beautiful creek. Water didn't feel particularly cold on a hot day. Monster log jams. Cool jumps. The pool below the last rap requires a couple of strokes of strong swimming, or jumping out to clear the eddy. Overall, a great day, and nothing further to add from previous reports this year.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20240913053849
comment: We had a great day in LeBar! I wanted to leave a trip report since I didn’t see any from later in the season — LeBar appears to run well into September and is quite lovely this time of year. Flow was friendly and the creek still had current to shepherd us through the long pools. We really enjoyed floating through the pools and taking in the scenery. By taking our time, we saw a lot of wildlife, including frogs, fish, a newt and CRAWDADS! It was so cool! We also had a curious dipper follow us down the canyon. All anchors are in good condition and the trap pool is a non-issue at this flow. 7.5 hours car-to-car using the standard approach.

title: Conditions:LeBar Creek-20250721204433
comment: We bushwhacked down on the steep slope about 20-30min to a decent tree by the cliff, and dry rappelled down to the creek about 30' (landing into a pool, so glad we suited up above), a few minutes walk to R1. The single bolt DCL at R1 was missing, only the screw was sticking out from the boulder (pic). So we had to improvise an alternative anchor ideas, and settled with body anchoring the first 3 people, and do a bottom belay for the last person. It doesn't sound bad, but we had to wrap the rope all around the round boulder DCL that barely had one questionable horn that was barely holding the rope from slipping up, so, it was dicy. The short rappel made it possible to do what we did. The creek walk with monster logjams after R1 and before R5 seem fluid every season, and the beta felt somewhat confusing because we didn't find the Diving Board definitively, and we found several The Deck Project type logjams. The creek walk before we got to R5 was solid 2+ hours with very proficient proceeding over all the logjams. Water was not super cold for the most part, but a lot of swimming and wading for sure. R5 bolt was there, but the screw in the boulder was moving very slightly, so we thought it probably needs to be re-bolted (or come up with a different anchor). We had a great time running this creek, but I thought it's not the best creek if one is nursing a knee or shoulder joint(s) because of the length of the creek walk and the intensity of the logjams (appx 25-30' high x 4 or 5 jams).

title: Conditions:Lena Creek-20260708224335
comment: We did 4 rappels and 0 jumps sadly. Flow is still a bit high which was cool for the big rappels but a tad intimidating. We started off as a party of 4 but two turned around as we had a beginner with us. The canyon section felt very short and probably took is 30 mins. While the boulder hopping was a hour and a half. We found one rabbit hole through some boulders that looks cool, otherwise very underwhelming.

title: Conditions:Little Goose Creek-20170822154620
comment: Creek was running surprisingly high for mid-late August. Number of hydraulics were present, so we did most rappels out of the flow. Nice waterfalls and interesting rock formations -- but it's a long dull slog of rock-hopping between the upper falls and the bottom narrows. Had a somewhat low opinion of the creek until reaching the bottom section which is worth the price of admission. All drops in the narrows are less than vertical. Bushwhacked directly up the steep, yet mostly open forest slopes just downstream DCR of the confluence. Found a cave on the way out; there were bear footprints on the floor.

title: Conditions:Little Goose Creek-20210811222512
comment: 1 thing noteworthy was at the 12 foot ramp at the entrance to the narrows: A large rock (larger than a football) came off from below a tree on canyon wall DCR and crashed directly into and thru the line of the next rappel. We had already down climbed DCR of the ramp. The rock came from much higher above after our down climb. Maybe we loosened something just enough to cause it? But to our inspection it didn't seem that we had. When making that down climb take extra care AND when attempting to rappel out of the flow on that ledge DCR take extra extra care. That columnar basalt, especially in this canyon is wanting to come down.

title: Conditions:Little Goose Creek-20230807055215
comment: Great run! Definitely could be rated much higher with the scenery in this canyon. Lush greens, bright red rocks, interesting rock formations, 1 beautiful rap and a very nice narrows section! Totally worth the trip, not sure why this place gets no love. It was relatively easy but its beauty is the draw here, not the challenge of how technical it is. I posted a vid for anyone interested is seeing what it looks like before making the trip out. 🤙 there is a large boulder above the R2, to rap in the flow, a set of linked bolts would be really nice!?

title: Conditions:Lookingglass Creek-20200902193040
comment: Could be a good creek on a day with better conditions. Approach begins on an easy trail but disappears into burn area regrowth, lots of dead trees to climb over and hordes of thorny brush to thrash through. The falls themselves are impressive but Mt Adams has been pumping out heavily silted flow all season, you def want to avoid getting that sandy water in your eyes. Water level marked extreme for the final drop, the flow was not close to runnable and we popped over a ridge DCR to rap down a much friendlier leg.

title: Conditions:Looky here-20250324204102
comment: Stopped and did the creek for fun. No sign of travel! The creek wasn't brushy and we had a good time. Certainly a seasonal drainage and not worth the time if its main canyon season

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20220926001508
comment: No anchors were found, so this was likely a first descent of Lower Fulton. We had somewhat low expectations dropping in -- and were pleasantly surprised when LF kicked off the day with a cool corkscrew rappel down underneath a giant chockstone on the first rappel. The canyon is committing, but has some fun obstacles, great pools, swimming, jumps, and water features. One of the best is a giant 35ft+ slide into a deep punchbowl. Need to find a better exit, but we'll be back next year to improve on the anchoring situation. Used the nearby Hamma Hamma Cabin as our basecamp for the weekend - awesome! :-)

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20230903042252
comment:
Likely the first complete descent of Lower Fulton. It turns out we cut out a bit early last time and there's still some nice canyon to see below, including one more quality rappel into a short slot. We were rather surprised on exiting to find a very nice trail leading off downstream on the north side of the creek - clearly built and maintained by locals. It would be really interesting to know where this comes out. According to Jefferson County parcel maps, it's all private land ownership downstream with nary an easement to be seen. We explored the trail a little bit, but ultimately turned around and thrashed back out, staying on timber company land. (This exit is only mildly better than the original way we went out during the first descent in 2022.) Lower Fulton is a fantastic and fun creek up there with the other "greats" of the Olympic Peninsula, only brought low by a horrid exit. If the trail route out was viable for canyoneers (and a shuttle could be set up), that would be amazing.
Other info: Fulton clearly sees very high flow in the early season; some of the logs from last year were tossed around like matchsticks. The 30ft slide is in... although going down the first 10ft or so on a rope might be good to get past a rather bumpy section. The exit from the Dragon's Den was completely blocked with woody debris; we sent a scout down to check to see if we could get out... and had to knock out a woody block and remove a bunch of sticks so that we could squeeze through.

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20230924193631
comment: The canyon is the most beautiful I've done on the Olympic Peninsula so far with some amazing sections of narrows. Had to build a few anchors. Didn't read the beta before R5 and built a bad anchor on a small chockstone that led to some troubles dislodging our rope. Should have used the big tree far back from the rappel to avoid issues. We got off track on both approach and exit with lots of bushwhacking involved. Even with the tough bushwhacking would recommend it to anyone with good skills. Already looking forward to my next run-through of the canyon. Side note the forest road to the start of the canyon is in very rough condition we were lucky enough to have a friend drop us off and pick us up at the bottom of the FS road only adding about 20 minutes or so to the day. Would be tricky without car support to coordinate if your vehicle can't make it up the road

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20240823225958
comment: Moderate-low flow, mainly since near the end on the last rap it’s a little pushy. Would be epic if better access is acquired. Bolts and anchors were is good conditions (wasn’t looking for a bolt on R7 so didn’t check and missed if it was their or not). Some of the boulder problems were a little tricky. The Dragons den you can still fit thru, looks like you can’t from the top on DCR but if you look from DCL you can see the hole is large enough. Hint for the exit is it’s easier to crawl in a lot of places, wear pants

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20240828044025
comment: We all agreed that Lower Fulton is a very underrated canyon. It was our favorite in the Olympics and such a fantastic day! The narrows are beautiful - green and sculpted - and the rappels and jumps are really fun. The mega-slide into the punchbowl is in (and so fun!) and we also jumped R2 (25'), R5 (15') and R7 from the mid-pothole (20'). A lot of logs have shifted around since last season and all of the webbing in the watercourse was trashed. We cut, carried it out, and ghosted everything else except R1 where we installed a single bolt on the chockstone. The boulder garden is a fun scramble, and we found a rabbit hole in the middle that allowed us to avoid a nuisance rappel. The approach is easy and the exit was not so bad. We found game trails through the steepest sections and were able to take advantage of some overgrown logging roads in the upper section. We were back at the road in 49 minutes!

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20240903191446
comment: Full Fulton was fun and totally worth running. We went a day early to scout the lower exit and work on the trail. We found a game trail and did a lot of cutting and cleaning up. The exit is steep and way less bushwacking than the original beta. I'll update the kml map. I wore a shorty wetsuit with a rain jacket and was fine with temp. Our time was 1.5 hours for upper Fulton and 4.5 hours middle, lower, and exit. Total was around 6 hours.

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20250708044547
comment: First time back since the FD. Did the direct approach and ran just Lower Fulton. Jumped a lot more than my previous visit, and had a great time. Such a good value half day. The slide is in, as is everything else documented as a jump. R6 (Dragon's Den) has changed a fair bit. Rather than climbing up DCL and rapping from a tree as we did before - the roof of the cave is now open so you can rap directly in. However the narrow gap to exit it is blocked, so you have to climb up and over the top instead - not hard, just very much changed. The highlight was the jump at R7. With a small but committing jump out to the enormous root ball from which a ~30(?)ft step-off can be made. The area has a bunch of jump options. We tried to follow the updated exit, but frankly it felt very similar to before, albeit moderately easier due to more traffic through the thickest bushes. The first part of the dirt road is in bad condition and requires high clearance.

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20250720231948
comment: First time through this canyon for me, and wow! Beautiful carved rock, lots of jumps and maybe the best slide in Washington? All the anchors looked to be in good shape, but we only rapped the three mandatory ones and jumped or slid everything else. Hike out wasn’t as heinous as I expected except one small section of bulldozing through spider infested holly, but there does seem to be trails forming that appeared and disappeared throughout the hike out.

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20251013204628
comment: Flow similar to prior descent in late June. Exit path requires some attention as there are many game trails throughout. Requires careful navigation to avoid crossing into private land.

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20260526023250
comment: Beta updated to reflect changes in the Dragons Den. Fun day out in the canyon, interesting to see how this canyon changes year to year. A knife was lost, if found let me know!

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20260701045244
comment: One of the best slides in the PNW! Anchors are solid, rocks are slippery however the water in not very cold. Great run!

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20260707004956
comment: I cut out most webbing anchors on obvious jumps. Just meat anchors people down drops who don't want to jump, or use cem/toggle. One 10' drop is technical that we rappelled off the log. There is still an anchor on the last drop which is a technical-ish jump. Theres a bolt, hanger, quicklink, teblyon ring anchor dcr that goes as a slide. (It does look like a slide) Exit is wonderfully great to follow. Will hopefully update the exit track. There are ground wasps on approach and exit. For the approach to stay to the right of the track a bit once you start descending. On the exit, avoid the second steep lose section of the exit trail. I got stung once on the entrance and a teammate for 4 stings on the exit.

title: Conditions:Madison Falls-20230814192053
comment: Very low flow, easily could be done in much much higher flow. Probable FD. Fun little creek/canyon

title: Conditions:Madison Falls-20231228075455
comment: Fun low commitment winter canyon. Will update some of the beta soon.

title: Conditions:Madison Falls-20260118050416
comment: Some nice moderate flow today, also not as cold as anticipated.

title: Conditions:Malachite Canyon-20180806015357
comment: The first descent took us 10.5 hours. Likely a 3.5 rating canyon - more stars if the approach wasn't so difficult. The approach took us 4 hours but we realized we should have stayed on the crest of the mountain instead of navigating the very difficult terrain closer to the canyon walls. The canyon is very unique to what I've done. Almost all the rappels have curiously slanted and all have little "caves" behind then. Lots of rappels and lots of opportunities for natural anchors.

title: Conditions:Marten Creek (Alpine Lakes)-20181020234239
comment: Biked up to the intersection between Marten and Taylor trails. Rainy and wet, so we moved kind of slow. Canyon was excellent, with a long bit of boulder hopping in the middle. A little slick in that boulder hopping part, but very sticky rock elsewhere. Pools were a bit shallow to jump or slide much at all, and we fiddlesticked an awful lot.

title: Conditions:Middle Fork Big Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20201005024936
comment: Randomly happened across this creek while looking for a campsite in the area. Came back the next day and ran the creek. Surprisingly fun with some beautiful rock formations and great flow. Somewhat akin to a mini-version of Fulton. Natural anchors are a bit sparse at several drops, so it may be wise to bring a bolt kit. Bolting would also let you run the flow on several drops, including R1 which is tight chute into a deep pool. Access is super easy -- a nice addition to creeks of the Wynoochee Valley area on the south side of the Olympics.

title: Conditions:Middle Fork Big Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20230724151614
comment: This canyon is a great short day. Five rapid fire rappels and jumps through a beautiful canyon. It's a shame it's so short. Placed bolts at R1 and R2. Will probably go back and develop a little further.

title: Conditions:Mosquito Creek (South Cascades)-20170822163544
comment: Possible first descent? No anchors were encountered. Some very nice bedrock. Lower Langfield is more impressive than upper Langfield Falls -- sort of a smaller sibling of Little Niagara. Nice little jaunt.

title: Conditions:Mosquito Creek (South Cascades)-20180808171440
comment: Fun little canyon. I (regrettably) opted to skip the first main falls since there were tourists present, but the lower falls still made it worth a visit. I think I exited the water course a bit too early on the scramble out and never found the elk trail that is described in the write up.

title: Conditions:Mosquito Creek (South Cascades)-20230904031836
comment: Nice short little canyon. My partner and I rapped R1 several times getting practice in and out of the flow. As we are both beginners with just under ten canyons each this is a great place to build our skills and confidence on rappelling. There were several visitors at the falls and we continually asked if people wanted us to remove our rope so they could take unspoiled pictures of the waterfall but everyone we spoke with was more entertained watching us. Watch out for rockfall on R1 as the face is sedimentary rock with lots of exposed round rocks that work free very easily. The rest of the canyon was short and sweet. We rigged all rappels with retrievable anchors except the 50-foot rappel at the end. My arborists friction saver kept hanging up on pre-rig test pulls so I added a new webbing anchor on a large root DCR. The logs at the bottom were not a problem as long as rope length is correct, too much will definitely get tangled up at the bottom. There was a logging operation in the area of the original beta exit route, so we climbed straight (arduous climb) up as soon as possible DCR and then followed the canyon rim back to the top of R1.

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20200705050409
comment: Great flow. Skipped R1&R2 because of crowds. Used the bypass rap on R3, however the hydraulics looked much friendlier from the bottom than they did at the top... would probably rap in the flow if I did this again. We replaced a few anchors, and they are in good shape for most raps. The confluence with the Duckabush was a very pleasant lunch spot. The hike out was steep and overgrown.

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20200914043623
comment: Ran the entire canyon and floated the Duckabush back to the bridge. All anchors looked good. Replaced R1 and another one down canyon.

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20200922033050
comment: R5: rap off the sling on the bouncy steel cable 😈. Rapped the side of the final falls to get a look, ascended back up and exited from there.

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20200922185341
comment: Follow the trail to the upper viewpoint with a bench. Follow use trails up behind the bench to gain the ridge. Follow the ridge up until it's relatively easy to traverse back over to the creek. If it looks steep you're in the wrong place. Large crowd of boy scouts cheered us on during the descent of the big falls. Below, creek very slippery in places. No hydraulics or dangerous water movements for us, but could be much more severe in high water. Found a much better exit route along an overgrown logging grade. Two minutes of bushwhacking, followed by an easy 5-10min walk out on the grade, then back up the road to the trailhead. Easy. Beta has been updated.

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20210713160843
comment: New cairn anchor had to be made. Some webbing and links replaced. New and loose wood appears to be everywhere. The exit is very bushwacky and GPS should be used as there is absolutely no trail most the way out. Flow was perfect. Check the anchor on cable at the last rap as it tears through webbing. Super fun.

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20230619034928
comment: Fun flow today. Hydraulics and siphons currently don’t need to be bypassed. Replaced about half of the webbing found in canyon.

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20230831141206
comment: Flow was low, no significant dangers or challenges posed by flow. We replaced webbing/quicklink at R4 and took out the cairn anchor that had been built nearby. Also found R6 had been moved to a tree DCL. The road grade hike out is overgrown, full of blow down and washed out in places. Would recommend the float out on the Duckabush option.

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20240708014032
comment: Lovely canyon. R1 and R5 are spectacular. We replaced webbing in several.places, and change the anchor in a couple of spots. Lots of logjam debris to navigate in the canyon. The strainer, siphon and hydraulics aren't of major concern in current conditions.

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20250527235254
comment: Fun day in Murhut. The water harzards aren't of major concern at the moment. Explored the upper section and found a few fun drops, beta has been added for those who are interested. A picture of UM_R2

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20250720221917
comment: Great day on Murhut, did the upper section as well and floated the duckabush as much as we could. Some strainers along the way to watch out for. Took a car all the way to the upper drop in point making it just a couple hundred feet down and you get started. Road needs a vehicle with decent clearance to get to the upper section and it's a bit grown over so make sure it's not a new car!

title: Conditions:Murhut Creek-20260519012648
comment: Fun chill day in Murhut. Upper and lower, with car shuttle

title: Conditions:Nash Creek-20200322175615
comment: First descent on a dreary winter day where other options were limited. A few of us were testing out new drysuits (not so ideal with the all the devil's club), and we spent the day battling logjams and brush. Nevertheless, good times were had. At the bottom, we realized the keys for the lower shuttle vehicle were left in the upper shuttle vehicle. We started the 4 mile hike up the road to retrieve them, and fortunately hitched a ride in the back of a pickup truck with some locals. A day saved.

title: Conditions:Noochee Creek-20231030003451
comment: Halloween freeze-fest! No anchors were found, so likely a first descent. Another nice short destination in the Wynoochee Valley with some beautiful pools and neat geology. Water levels were a bit high (and cold! air temp 34-degrees at the bridge) -- we suspect it was running in flood stage, so ended out rappelling along the edge or out of the flow on most of the drops. A couple of the drops can probably be jumped. Some strong current present in places and a few careful downclimbs. Anchors are a bit thin on the final drop... we'll be back to improve the anchor situation.

title: Conditions:Panther Creek-20250324184005
comment: Just wanted to say that this is probably the coolest waterfall I have ever seen. Even though it isn't a technical canyon (well it is, just don't do it). I cannot stress how awesome this place it. It is a must do in the area!

title: Conditions:Paradise Creek-20190911062434
comment: Getting in and out is a bear, but a beautiful section of narrows. Nice to see, but not sure how often I'll be back.

title: Conditions:Paradise Creek-20220413171757
comment: Great creek. Creek walked up to the features on a very easy to walk creek bed. Quite possibly a rock hounds paradise. Large landslide up creek right just beyond the confluence of Proverbial. Scoped out the wide drops on the lower end of Proverbial. Neat but nothing worth running. Local have hand lines set up due to the rocks quality. Once around the landslide we continued up and around to the drop in. Very tight and fun creek. The big drop could be fun with some more water.

title: Conditions:Paradise Creek-20220605053538
comment: Took the creek route as it is mostly mellow and by the time the other tour is available via the gate opening, the creek wouldn’t be at a fun flow. This place is truly special.

title: Conditions:Paradise Creek-20250610192508
comment: Walked up creek after running Koda. Climbed up canyon left just before the base of R7 and then traversed to drop in above R1. The steep section is pretty open, the drop in is pretty brushy. Some of us did R1 as a slide, as always check the pool first. Rebuilt R3 cairn anchor that was gone. R4 single bolt is in a really bad spot for the pull. Rope slides across sharp edges on the wall and flicking the rope to release damages the rope. Climbed up and pulled from halfway, then downclimbed and shallow jumped into the pool. Recommend last person does the same or try adding another QL to the anchor which might help. Lower section with high walls is really pretty. R6 anchor high on tree DCL should probably be relocated and I wouldn't use it again. I recommend using a tree a little further back DCL which puts the rappeler in the flow, avoids damaging the bank, and improves the pull. Did the creek walk exit down to Wind River Road, it's long with lots of logs to crawl over/under. Was about a 10 hour day car to car combined with Koda. Watch your step, we came a cross a Pacific Giant Salamander, and a couple dozen frogs. The landslide spot is actually really cool, pretty easy going DCL. A couple hundred feet before the road there is a trail that goes off DCL, take it to a campsite and an easy road walk the rest of the way to Wind River Road...could park here instead of the turn out by Paradise Creek Campground.

title: Conditions:Pothole Surprise-20230623040805
comment: Very short canyon with some interesting geology. Water was very low, little to no flow with deep pools. We didn’t set any rappels and downclimbed all obstacles with some partner assisted downclimbing.

title: Conditions:Pothole Surprise-20230903042844
comment: A short diversion outside of Port Angeles; was passing through and stopped by to check it out. Would probably be more fun with more water, and the new (?) homeless camp (lots of kitchen knives lying around; totally bizarre) atop the falls takes away from the allure. Use caution as the central pothole is deep and may need a partner assist to escape. Brought a rope but ended out downclimbing everything.

title: Conditions:Prairie Creek-20200817022750
comment: Descended forested slopes a short distance to drop in via a convenient draw. Progression downstream surprisingly easy. Flow was low, but some quality rappels. Re-rigged the last two rappels to run the flow and rappel into the Dragon's Chamber which was super fun. Think we went past the recommended exit point and ended up having to do a bit more thrashing to get out of the creek.

title: Conditions:Prairie Creek-20240808233931
comment: Fun creek! A couple of the rappels could use bolts to run the flow. Probably best run in June-July timeframe for more water. Could be a good weekend combo with Tule nearby.

title: Conditions:Prospector Creek-20230812021330
comment: This amazing little creek is a perfect place to chill and enjoy the views with out the hard work or getting in or out...1 rappel can be done but in good conditions can be jumped from multiple spots ranging from 17ft up to 40ft if you climb up DCL. To jump there is a gap to cover a few feet out but pretty easy to accomplish. Once in the pool the 2nd jump is only about 3-4 ft into another beautiful deep pool with awesome underwater rock features and fish! Water levels were perfect for practicing rappels or working on water entry from different heights. Exploring past this area up creek to the Washougal falls is def worth the time, there are a few other deep blue/green pools to get in and the views are really nice.

title: Conditions:Prospector Creek-20230828085639
comment: Great little swimming/jumping area. There were 5 other people there swimming plus our 3. Water is cold, we wished we brought more than shorty wetsuits.

title: Conditions:Rachor Creek-20180815035235
comment: Its clear no one has been here in years. Found two pieces of very old webbing. We only did the upper canyon - to the base of Old Spur Falls. We did it in 4 rappels and a few downclimbs. Above the technical section, there is a LOT of wood. Once you get into the technical section, the canyon is quite beautiful. Water is incredibly low throughout the state right now, and in low conditions, the canyon is a3 - definitely not a4. Benign with relatively short rappels... but also with some good exposure to get to some of the rappels. All in all a fun day!

title: Conditions:Rachor Creek-20220708064334
comment: Really fun at this flow! All but R5 anchors were in good shape. We did not replace R5 - just downclimbed dry canyon left

title: Conditions:Rachor Creek-20220719014259
comment: We ran upper and lower. Lower is a much different beast - very deep canyon walls with much higher flow. The flow seemed to be about double what it was in upper. The 250ft waterfall is one of the tallest vertical falls in WA and features a severe roostertail that blasts horizontally out about 2/3 down. The 2nd tier of the big fall rappel has a huge curtain to duck under and a behind the veil rappel. The downclimbing from the last rappel to the hike out is some of the best in WA. I would give the upper 3-star and the lower 5-star.

title: Conditions:Rachor Creek-20250630073554
comment: A day of extremes. Overcast skies, very slippery canyon, and many blow-downs detracted from the Upper section. But my god! The lower section is amazing. A huge multistage waterfall. High flow made it look incredible, with double rooster trails and general aquatic mayhem. Despite the chaos it was straight-forward to rappel. We did it in two pitches (skipping R12). We also doubled-up the R13 single bolt to make a great exposed stance. The down-climbing below the big falls was terrible - hideously slimy and very slow progress. Then we exited DCR by scrambling up to the ridge line and returning back to the vehicles at the mid-point exit. This too was unpleasant, with loose terrain followed by steep clear-cut bushwhacking. Despite both these enormous detractors the multistage still made it worth it. I'm still convinced there's a better way to exit by climbing to the rim and then dropping to the power-station and walking out the gated road. I think I'd prefer 2 miles of flat road to climbing clear-cut, but hard to say by going off maps alone. (This is different from the original exit idea of crossing the river).

title: Conditions:Rachor Creek-20260630002633
comment: Overcast skies and temps in low 60s. Canyon was very slippery and the big log in R3 still remains unfortunately. New bolts were added at R5 to replace the pinch point webbing anchor that requires annual replacement. All other anchors were in good condition The lower section is super scenic with some rebelays and great exposure. New anchors were added at the hanging pool below the big drop so the canyon can be run with 60m ropes. Exiting the big falls was not fun but totally worth running the lower section. It was EXTREMELY slippery and a lot of down logs to manage that are new. We used the same scramble up exit as last year to the shuttle mid-point exit Great day with a solid crew!

title: Conditions:Razzle Dazzle Creek-20200216210907
comment: A fun day out with friends not taking no for an answer from the rain and snow. An easy day and a surprisingly pretty canyon. Early start and early conclusion meant more time for beers at the end of the day. Good times.

title: Conditions:Razzle Dazzle Creek-20220912193025
comment: We descended this on a cold and rainy spring day with a winter storm advisory in the mountains above. This creek exceeded our expectations (which were low) as it's quite pretty with lovely rappels. Easy access, easy exit, and straightforward rappels would make it a great descent for beginners or those just getting into canyoning (ideally on a warmer day!)

title: Conditions:Razzle Dazzle Creek-20230604214227
comment: Fun early season shortie creek. Good for beginners or when other creeks are running high. Would have been more fun with a bit more water. Found a big pile of old webbing someone left at R1. :(

title: Conditions:Razzle Dazzle Creek-20250505170002
comment: All anchors in good shape, flow was low but this is a very chill scenic run. The last two raps are really fun and make the canyon worthwhile! Would love to go back and redo in higher flow!

title: Conditions:Razzle Dazzle Creek-20250517174410
comment: Rainy day in canyon, the bushwhack in is surprisingly mellow and short lived, the canyon is a short but nice one, rappels are scenic and easy, although the pull on R2 almost stuck our rope and took some work. First two quick links are very rusted, webbing on the last rappel is only half inch. Canyon could use with some updates to the anchors.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Alternate, South Cascades)-20211130012905
comment: No other signs of anchors were found, so this was likely a first descent of the Alt-Rock tributary of Rock Creek. Ski Jump Falls must be super impressive in higher water. This tributary drops right in above the main technical section of Rock Creek and is an easier approach than the original one described for Rock Creek.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Alternate, South Cascades)-20240520033845
comment: Descended Alt-Rock and dropped into the main technical section. Flow low in Alt-Rock, moderate in main Rock. All anchors replaced - would be lovely to find a way to stay in the flow on pitch 2 of Ski Jump Falls. Water in main Rock is much higher, but easy to find lines out of the flow. Don't let the 2mi hike out scare you, much of the hiking is on gravel bars and bedrock areas. Takes about 2hrs to reach the PCT from the bottom of the last rappel.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Alternate, South Cascades)-20250527030258
comment: Descended Alt-Rock to main Rock. The massive ramp of Ski Jump Falls is pretty unique and Rock itself would get run all the time if access was easier. The 2mi creek-walk out is relatively easy with numerous gravel bars and areas of bedrock, plus a few obstacles to keep things interesting. Follow the path of least resistance to the PCT footbridge. Ran into a PCT trail crew at the lower trailhead who fed us snacks. One tick found.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Cowlitz County)-20210601021404
comment: Canyoneers on ice! This is one of the most slippery creeks I've visited in the Pacific NW. Even canyon-specific footwear comes up short here. Dropped into the creek above the bridge and headed down through the most interesting section. Probably one of the best slides near Portland on the terraces. The big drop was kicking off some serious flow. Would be fun to set an anchor to run the flow although it should be unobtrusive as this area is clearly visited by locals. Creek-walking down to the lake was a bit tedious; we had to stop and dunk in the pools occasionally to cool off. Found a much better exit along an old track that climbs back up to the powerline. Short stint of bushwhacking, but really not that bad. Fun half-day adventure.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Indie, South Cascades)-20211130012306
comment: First descent of Indie-Rock. Would be more fun with more water - probably would be more enjoyable earlier in the season - although Rock Creek will also be higher for the hike out. Interesting rock features near the fin at the bottom.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20200823030546
comment: A great day in this gem of a canyon hidden in plain sight. It's unlikely anyone had been down this canyon in quite some time. Short, sweet, great bang for your buck. The truck overturned in the upper creek appeared to be fairly new. Called and reported it to the Mason County Sheriff's office.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20200922031615
comment: If you find the green scooter for a good jump photo, plz rebuild the rock stack for future use 😀

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20210621151540
comment: Great conditions and an unexpectedly beautiful creek! (almost mod-high conditions... couldn't decide) Plenty of non-technical jumps (but a depth-checker is a must because if you jump in the wrong spot, it would be bad), a couple of short bumpy slides, and a stunning hanging pothole. Rock creek on a sunny day - highly recommend! The exit is a calf-burner and a bit dirty, but well worth it.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20210625040207
comment: If you like jumps and friendly whitewater this is a great one. All but the final rap can be jumped, into deep pools ranging from 10’, 15’, 25’ and 30’. A couple friendly champagne rooms to play in. Three short bumpy slides are better to avoid tearing the wetsuit. If the final drop is bolted DCL to go down the massive 80’ rooster tail, this would be one of the best rappels in the region.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20210720200536
comment: Excellent day. Lovely jumps with impressive but non-challenging water. The R3 pool looked a lot more hydraulicy that it was, no problems exiting. Surprised to find new DCL bolts on R5 that provided a lot more options, and we stayed in the flow. Superb finish. The hike out made me almost deduct a star, but it's actually shorter than I'd imagined and quickly forgotten, so overall the route gets a solid 4*.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20210802172004
comment: Water has certainly dropped, but I was impressed at the flow that remained. The jumps all still went, though much lower and they'll need to be shallow-jumped. The twin jets at the beginning were still a fun feature, and the final sequence still offered pushy flow in the middle for those that wanted it. This is such a high quality canyon IMO, despite being short. This is my third time in it this season, and though conditions have changed, it's great to see that it's got at least a solid 5-6 week season, even without rain.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20220810205113
comment: Always a great half-day canyon! Flow was lower than expected… the first little jump goes, the canyon left jump to the big pothole goes, the shorter jump at the end of the 3-tier sequence is filled in and too shallow. Be sure to set the rope long enough to get down. One of the big logs in the pool at the bottom of the big falls is gone. Amazing. Where could it be?? The ancient looking log is still there, though it has started to tip in a different direction. The enormous biting bugs on the exit were very unpleasant.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20220811010525
comment: Great day in the canyon- the water is clear and refreshing. The 110’ rap looks saucy from the top, but it can be negotiated on the right, or take the pothole route. All anchors were in decent condition. Taking time to set rope length is still a good idea as the pools are still fairly deep. The flipped car is still there. Bring a trash bag to help keep the turnout at the parking area clean. We dispersed an improvised fire ring that presented a hazard to people trying to use the turnout.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20220815005821
comment: Terrific half-day canyon in the Skykomish River Valley. Spent some time playing and jumping into the pools. Single bolt at R3 is not well placed for ease of getting on rappel; would be nice to improve the anchor situation on this one. Rappelled down the wet side of R4 to the rebelay for an exquisite final rappel. Hats off to the installation team. Route out is not entirely obvious; I should have brought some marking tape. Would be good to standardize an exit route to combat erosion.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20220828001302
comment: Great day at Rock creek. Anchors were in good condition. Water was pretty cold, so take enough neoprene. All jumps went. The exit was pretty bushwhacky, thankfully short lived.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20230620021057
comment: Found the canyon flowing moderate and had a great time. All the jumps looked to be clear of debris/obstacles, all the anchors were in good condition, although we did not check the webbing on the DCR option for the last rappel into the mosquito pool or the anchors for the semi-hanging rebelay since we opted to make the DCL rappel in a single shot. Pull was very clean on this and didn’t have any trouble with the rope. The bolt on R3 is solid, but the hanger is a little loose and someone might want to snug it up with a wrench next time through. There was quite a bit of trash in the canyon from people treating it like a dump and might be a good idea to put together a team/group to do a clean up some time. Water is very cold.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20230706032315
comment: A great day out with a group of 7 for the 4th of July weekend! Water running at moderate flow. Added a single bolt DCL after R3 to avoid having to make the shallow jump, use human anchors in the limited space after R3, or having to do R3 in two stages (which leads to an entanglement hazard in the pool). The "Mosquito Pool" pothole is becoming stagnant and the water level is dropping to keeper levels. Recommend taking the multipitch route DCL into the final pool.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20230710015357
comment: Bit depressing to find the parking area full of trash left by previous slobs with no respect for the environment. We picked up a little, but the next team should please bring a garbage bag; hopefully we can all do a little to help keep the area clean. (Our team elected not to experiment by ingesting random drugs found on the ground in the parking area.) Canyon itself was in fine form & fun as always. Couple bolts could use tightening, but all anchors in good shape. Very slippery!

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20230712050733
comment: Scenic and fun canyon! Waterflow was a good level for beginners. Jumps were rather shallow as pools had filled with more gravel compared to prior years (according to those who had been there before). Exit is a bit of a thrash (Grade 1, BW2 on the Cascade Brush and Bushwhack Rating System), but it's not too long. Although you suit up at the parking, some might prefer to pack a thin long-sleeve shirt for the exit (instead of wearing the wetsuit top).

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20230718101653
comment: Took mosquito pool route, pothole escape is easy-moderate difficulty using climbing. Messed around with some pack toss for a few minutes, poor geometry and or not enough weight (only used one bag). Exit is punchy

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20240428235757
comment: Great early season day out. Wasn't sure if the route was going to be low enough to be runnable but it was! A lot of features look scary but they were easy to avoid or go through.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20240707204418
comment: A great day in Rock Creek on a hot day. Moderate water flow. Not much has changed from 2023. Some pools still on the shallower side with some gravel fill-in. Be aware the lip at the edge of the pool on R3. A teammate hit their knee coming off rope into this pool and required a few stitches but was ok.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20240721024627
comment: Great little canyon. Super fun with some unique features. We didn’t do any jumps. Jumping seems doable in certain spots, but there are quite a few shallow areas at the current water levels. My only complaint about the canyon is that I wish it was longer. We didn’t see the sign in the beta and ended up walking a little too far on the exit, but were still able to find a relatively straightforward path out. You could easily run this twice or combine with another nearby canyon. Anchors and webbing were all in good shape.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20240830192132
comment: There’s more trash in this canyon than any other one I’ve seen in Washington. It seems folks just dump their litter into the creek from the roadside. 😭 Next time I’ll bring an extra dry bag/trash bag. All anchors are in good condition. Exit trail is coming in quite nicely, we could follow the social trail all the way from the creek to the road.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20240903205715
comment: I wish I would have read the condition report about the trash. We should have brought trash bags. Fun creek, protect your rope on the edge of the bridge. The water was surprising strong on the chute of R5 till it hits the end where it makes the rooster tail. We rigged it and ran it all in one shot. Fun rap!

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20240930200114
comment: Good shoulder-season day in Rock Creek. Canyon features themselves were really fun and water was chilly for those wearing 4/3, but comfy for 5/4. Unfortunate amount of trash along DCR past the overturned car where someone is dumping trash over the side of the road. It makes it down to the water which is gross. Had I known the in-canyon trash situation I would have taken a big pack and three trash bags to triple bag the gross-ness out of the canyon so people don’t have to see it- it detracts from the experience in a big way. If some canyon angel does clean up, bring nitrile gloves and work gloves for your protection. The exit had a lot of blown down trees- someone has been cutting the blowdown which is great, but there were three cans of half-used motor oil (prob for the saw) laying sideways next to the stream and associated trash. C’mon man. I don’t know if it was part of our community or not- we removed it. If you’re going to the effort to hike in a saw, hike out the accessories too.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20250419153409
comment: Rock creek was in fun mode, although a new log obstacle has appeared at R3 (R2 if you jump/downclimb the optional R1 which I’d recommend at this flow). I was able to push aside the one log primarily impeding the rappel, but the other one still presents a strainer hazard. The DCL option for the final rappel is in extra spicy mode, opted for the mosquito pool rappel option which is lovely this time of year.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20250708064359
comment: The webbing around the log at R2 is now replaced by a beautiful pair of unlinked bolts. Jumped all the canyon except R5.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20250715135733
comment: Beautiful sunny day in canyon. Anchors in great condition. Jumps are in.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20260329013705
comment: Rock Creek was ripping today! One team member who has run Rock early season over multiple years said this was the highest they have seen it. For those who had only run the canyon at normal flows, the feedback was that it felt like an entirely different canyon. It is COLD and even with a 5mm and a splash jacket you could feel it. No issues descending, precautions were taken (throw ropes, staging teammates, etc) to ensure that swiftwater hazards were managed. This type of management is required to run the canyon at this cfs. Some jumps but minimal compared to what one could do in lower flow. This was because 1) fun wet lines and 2) a combination of slippery conditions and high flow/swiftwater that frequently made rappelling the better option. J1 seems to get more shallow every year, so definitely depth check and spot for people. R3 was a delightful sequence that involved rappelling a few feet, setting your rappel device friction to low, kicking off the back wall and quickly dropping on rope to pass the violent boil. Team went the mosquito pool route for the last rap sequence as the alternative would have been hazardous to our health. It was truly a wonderful Lagoon! With that being said: Bolts/anchors generally in ok condition!

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20260518150636
comment: Water has already down to moderate flow. Conditions are perfect right now for this canyon. No issues. Appreciate the new bolts!

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20260702052558
comment: Great lil run, cool jumps and really fun last rappel with sweet rooster tail! Rapped in from the bridge to start. Lots of garbage thrown in from the road above. There is even a ford truck with lots of tread left on the tires. Downside is very slippery rocks and the exit isn’t the greatest.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Post, South Cascades)-20250615030751
comment: No other anchors were found, so possibly a first descent. Nice to finally visit after hiking by this minor trib for years and being curious about it. Lots of loose rock and signs of tree damage in the lower creek; something major clearly came down last winter. While Post-Rock will never be one of the "greats", it made for an entertaining outing.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (South Cascades)-20170511022034
comment: Beautiful creek -- feels more like a California creek than the Pacific NW.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (South Cascades)-20170724161301
comment: Dropped in via the "Alt-Rock tributary". Will have to add beta as the approach is different, but encountered a previously unknown (to us) 250ft high multi-tier giant skateboard ramp falls. Alt-Rock enters the main fork of Rock Creek between R3 and R4, so we hit the best part of the main Rock Creek. Flow on main Rock is great currently -- fun to rappel all the falls. Needed to be a little careful in places where the flow chutes up, but not that bad. This would be a Grade-A creek were it not for the long approach and, seemingly, even longer exit. Recommend being prepared for a long day out. Our time is likely a little longer than the norm per exploratory approach (first descent?) on Alt-Rock. We found a giant seven-point elk antler at the bottom of Kala-Kala Falls.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (South Cascades)-20190622044559
comment: First descent of Indie-Rock. Won't be one of the all-time greats, but an entertaining day out with a surprising amount of bedrock ramps that go on and on. Indie-Falls and adjacent spire was pretty cool. Anchored high up on the right to run the flow. Beginner-friendly. Long walk out on Rock Creek.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (South Cascades)-20190705142731
comment: What can I say, the falls and geology of this canyon are amazing. Such a diverse range of rock - it really is beautiful. The approach and exit take away from it significantly. Some REALLY steep stretches of the ATV trail on the hike in and then the miles of river walking is less than awesome. Doubt I will run this again, but it was a good experience.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (South Cascades)-20190729055037
comment: Descended Alt-Rock. Very low water on Alt-Rock, moderate-low on main stem.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (South Cascades)-20240520223031
comment: Great day descending Rock Creek from Alt Rock. That means we skipped the first three rappels. Water was moderate-high so we stayed adjacent/on the edge of the flow. This led to fun rappels with some impressive close interactions with the main flow (like tucking under the horsetail on R7). The exit is a dream for a 2 mile creek walk with lots of bed rock and gravel bars, plus a few fun deep spots to cool off in.

title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Tsolo Scramble)-20170724154513
comment: Exploratory outing into this part of Rock Creek. Wished we'd brought wetsuits to rappel the falls. Plunge-pools below Tsolo look like a fun place to play. Great cataracts, side waterfalls, beautiful sculpted bedrock. Total gem of a (non-technical) creek.

canyon: Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)
title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)-20170531195854
comment: Explored the middle tributary and found +3 falls. Water in the tributary was fine, but tripled on entering the main fork. Still lots of snow in the upper drainage.

canyon: Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)
title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)-20170605014022
comment: Explored the east fork. Unfortunately only +1 waterfall. Looks like the west tributary is the best bet for running NFRC... or drop directly in from the PCT. The falls on the main line are worth it, but the cost to play may be a little high for some.

canyon: Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)
title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)-20170830051737
comment: Very low water, just a trickle at most of the falls. Deepest pool was about waist deep, but most were far shallower. Walked through a bee hive on the east side of the creek on the stretch between r3 and r4 and got stung on my legs, arms, and neck. :(

canyon: Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)
title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)-20210124041406
comment: Flow was surprisingly low for mid-winter, but much fun was had. N.F. Rock seems to be a good one in the early season with a nice south facing drainage. Temperature was 38 degrees F on returning to the car. Practiced a few rigging techniques just for the heck of it.

canyon: Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)
title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)-20260715001436
comment: I ran the lower section below the PCT. It's been almost a decade since I last did this and I forgot how much bushwhacking was involved. In my opinion it is not worth the effort. I only rapped R5 and R7 and was able to down climb or bypass everything else. All raps were ghosted and I removed some old webbing along the way.

title: Conditions:SMC Creek-20250607063215
comment: First descent of SMC and lower falls. 5 hours car to car. We did a sneak approach on the logging road (47.54994, -121.70274) that brings you to SMC falls (47.55396, -121.70104), making for an easy bike then 30 min hike approach. Fresh downfall in canyon otherwise it was a great day in moderate flow. Plenty of anchor options at each rap and likely an escape from canyon at each rap, and an escape at the bridge, so this would be a great canyon for an early season and/or rainy day close to Seattle.

title: Conditions:Satsop River Gorge (West Fork)-20201005031434
comment: Gorgeous river canyon. Flow was pushy in places, but not that bad. Most of the rapids can be bypassed on one side or another. Lower canyon was sublime. The Olympic's answer to Oneonta Gorge in Oregon, except with zero tourists. Ran Middle Fork Big Creek and the Big Creek Narrows earlier the same day.

title: Conditions:Satsop River Gorge (West Fork)-20230727142039
comment: A nice chill canyon in the Olympics, although a bit of clambering around tree debris.

title: Conditions:Skookum Falls-20240512063705
comment: put in 4 anchors to safely descend the canyon. There is 2 dry rappels that we did to access the canyon. First one is cord off a tree down about 105 ft. Second is webbing off a tree, 120ft. This takes you to a tree above the first big waterfall. ~200ft. after that, cord off a tree DCL ~220ft. On the last rappel you will probably be unable to hear any whistles from your rappeler.

title: Conditions:Skookum Falls-20250111114445
comment: Low elevation, easy access, short day and great seasonal flow makes Skookum a lovely winter objective. The tall waterfalls are so fun and the flow is dispersed. At moderate flow, you could pick a line straight through the flow or a line to keep your head dry if you wanted to. As the creek is so open, it felt warmer than other canyons in similar conditions. 35F at the trailhead and we weren’t cold in the canyon. FR-7315 is gated from December 15 to May 1, so we parked at Sun Top Sno Park which added 1.75mi and 900’ of elevation gain to the approach. 3.5 hours car-to-car.

title: Conditions:Skookum Falls-20250420232932
comment: Fun day! Flow was great but the water was extremely cold. Most of us tried to stay dry on R3, but R4 is great fun in the dispersed flow. I managed to accidentally leave a closed system at the anchor, so one teammate got to ascend the final pitch. That is a cold, wet ascent, 0% of which is dry. Not recommended 😂 The 4h car to car includes the ascending shenanigans.

title: Conditions:Skookum Falls-20250509031530
comment: Awesome pay-to-play ratio for this one. Flow was fun but not challenging. Very cold water (I wore fleece underlayers, 6/3 mm of neoprene with a hood, a rain jacket and neoprene gloves, and I was chilly after the last rap but generally a good temperature) Beautiful last rap!

title: Conditions:Skookum Falls-20250509051452
comment: If you find my VT prussick by R2 please let me know. Just a few feet above anchor on flat spot. 814-592-4211 thanks! Great canyon with drive up, 4 back to back raps, and quick exit. Recommend goretex hood over helmet for r4. Got brainfreezed

title: Conditions:Skookum Falls-20260503010948
comment: FR7315 is still closed, so we parked at the gate and hiked 40min up to the usual upper parking spot. Down-climbed the rock cliff (described as R1, but couldn't find existing webbing). Then used the existing webbing DCL for R2 (adding a missing quicklink). Anchors still good in R3 & R4. The log has gone from the river crossing, instead we had to do a ferry swim, with some rope work to get the packs across. Such a lovely route, even with the extra hike in. General thoughts: * check out a potentially better approach of crossing the stream high up, and following the ridge down to above R3. * bolting the DCL wall at R3 to allow for a more-wet line, keeping the route interesting in lower flow.

title: Conditions:Snag Creek-20210517002327
comment: No signs of anchors or other presence were found, so this was probably a first descent. Pretty thrashy in sections on the way in and out, but the creek itself was excellent. Snag Creek Falls turned out to be pretty straightforward with a tree really nicely situated for the final drop. Suspect flow is on the low side for this time of year per a really dry spring. Not sure I'll be back, but nice to click one off the list I'd been curious about. Saw two wood rats on the hike out.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20150609044744
comment: Great trip! The weather was spectacular - sunny and almost 80F. The water flow was just enough to make it fun, but we did have to do a fair amount of stream walking rather than floating. The log jam mentioned in the canyoneeringnorthwest.com beta (somewhere between Fall Into The Wall and Franklin Falls) was no longer present. It was National Trails Day, and Franklin Falls was packed with spectators. We continued down canyon and exited at a popular swimming hole after Denny Camp Falls.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20150708162254
comment: This is a great canyon for those looking to get into canyoneering. This canyon is more like a waterpark than a canyon and the lower section is my favorite. Always check the depth of a pool before jumping. Water flow was moderate-low and should not be a problem for beginners.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20170809213744
comment: Fun day in the sun! We did not alter any anchors, as all were as (now) described in the Descent beta. The drop previously described as a slide looks like a very dangerous slide, even at low flow, and anchor options abound. The beta has been altered to reflect this. I can't even remember how many times we jumped, but all the jumps were incredible. The water was cold enough to where our hands were getting somewhat numb after swimming a lot. Gloves would have been nice. We took my Dad down his first canyon, and he had a great time. No difficult raps, but a few tricky downclimbs.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20170907011311
comment: Agree with Tiffanie's note below: it's more of a waterpark than a canyon. Lots of short swims and jumpable pools. Nothing technical. Much is more of a creek-walk than a canyon, so if you're looking for a deep dark slot this isn't a good choice. Great place to take canyoneers who haven't tried Class C conditions before.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20190701024320
comment: Did just the top section - Fall into the Wall to the base of Franklin Falls. Most pools had a fair bit more whitewater than lower water. The hydraulics are no big deal, but the foam made it impossible to see into pools. As a team of 2, we didn't find it made sense to check the depth in enough detail to do most of the jumps. Anyway, most of the places we usually jump from had enough flow to make them pretty sketch to get out to. Some of the downclimbs were more challenging than usual, but still not that bad. We used the upper bolts to more safely access the lower bolts at the lip of Franklin Falls, but water was not flowing through the pool near the lip. Shortly after the pool, water was flowing about 1-4" deep (pulsing) over the ledge leading to the lower bolts. It was slippery and the flow was pushy when it pulsed, so we felt good using the upper bolts. Franklin was easily the best part of the canyon at this flow, whereas the jumps are usually the highlight at lower flows. We bailed after the falls.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20190922024653
comment: Great beginner canyon with Franklin falls always making me worry about beginners as they go over the edge. NOTE. The road is closed from the upper side due to construction till mid october. From the bottom, drive up and through the road closure signs at the franklin falls lower, lower parking lot. Parked car at upper lower franklin falls turn out and shuttled up to turn out above the franklin falls past all the switchbacks. Parked and walked uphill half mile to jump in spot. Not bad, just different.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20200724184735
comment: Garcia gauge on SF Snoqualmie was reading 80cfs. This is a great and fun level for most canyoneers. We descended at 6pm and finished by 8pm by hiking to the car after franklin falls. Until right before the falls, everything is either a jump or slide. Full wetsuit is recommended as you spend a lot of time up to your neck or chest in the cold south fork water. We could have finished it faster, but made a stop to re-do the Franklin Falls anchors. Note that the handline from the pool above to Franklin Falls anchor is gone, you must use the one bolt anchor to rappel yourself to the anchor. While it may seem to be an couple of easy steps to get to the anchor point, a slip would certainly mean falling down the falls.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20200805191423
comment: Ran the lower half. Perfect water level. All of the anchors are in good shape.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20200811225917
comment: Downstream gauge (12143400) was reporting ~60cfps. Great fun! At the second feature I did the 20ft jump (didn't touch the bottom [6ft, 210lb]), other two in the group did the 18' slide. As noted before, no hand rail above the falls. Returned to road from Franklin Falls.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20200912225213
comment: This canyon is best at super low flow - none of the raps get you all that wet anyway. The pools are all clear, the jumps are super fun (and sessionable) and even with the smoke, it was as pretty as ever. Walked the wagon road trail and the road back to my car. FYI, parking on the left side of the road next to the exit is closed right now, and the road is one way (uphill only) - makes the drive in slower if you're used to getting off at the Alpental exit.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20210719011300
comment: Ideal flow. All the jump ledges are traversable, and the spray from Franklin is primo. Get after it! Removed some tat from the anchor at the top of Franklin. Otherwise, anchors are all good.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20210727063452
comment: Post work evening trip. 77cfs on the Garcia gauge. Good conditions for the jumps. Rigged line between R2 & R3. Somehow core-shot the rope on Franklin however!

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20210802015513
comment: Great water levels. Could use a bolt installed on the initial traverse to Franklin, we meat anchored our way across. We picked up some trash in the river. Good time, great group

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20210812083318
comment: Fantastic trip through South Fork Snoqualmie on a hot afternoon. Flow was 52 cfs on the Garcia gauge. The big slide is VERY fun at this flow. All the usual jumps are in.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20210815094811
comment: A very fun thing to do on a very hot day. Very chill, and a relatively swimmy canyon. Some beta says bail after Franklin Falls but I think you miss the prettiest part of the canyon if you do this.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20210815175017
comment: 48cfs. Ran whole length, usual jumps & slides all in on upper & lower section. Orange glow from the smoke just added to the already beautiful route. Lower section really does add to the overall trip.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20210924021702
comment: Full canyon. 45-50 CFS. Great canyon! A very adventurous one - no doubt! Not much webbing in place, so come prepared with quick links and webbing. Lots of slides and jumps. Water was a bit chilly at times with 3mm gloves. Truly amazing views and scenery in the canyon! Didn’t expect that amount of creek walking though. Several miles of Creek walking. Some more technical rappels, Some slightly exposed or risky climbs to get to the rappels. Took about 6–7 hours to do the full canyon.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20220816160928
comment: Water is lower but the pools are still deep and cold. R1 anchor was removed, we opted to just walk around rather than set-up a new one there. Franklin Falls anchor has redundant large rappel rings, two highly rusted and does create a significant drag for the rope if you run it through them for the pull down. Test before use, or remove and replace them completely.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20220921204546
comment: Ran full length. 78F air temp, but the water was particularly cold. Low flow (25cfs), however all significant jumps still went fine. Found black fig8 in one of the early pools. Cut rusted quicklinks off Franklin bolts and replaced with fresh stainless steel ones. Canyon was looking very beautiful. A lot more trees in the canyon this year, with a dam formed in the lower section where the large jump is (you might want to now rap down the damn if not jumping).

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20240718050041
comment: Such a fun, playful canyon! All the usual jumps and slides are in. Flow was 74 cfs and there were no special challenges. We recommend the traverse lines to approach Franklin Falls as the spray was making the rocks slippery at this flow. All anchors in good condition. 3.5 hours car-to-car from Fall in the Wall to the Franklin Falls Trailhead.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20240812192100
comment: Really fun, easy canyon to run in a short time. Only ran it up to top of Franklin Falls and then exited back to cars.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20250222052752
comment: An unseasonably cold and dry period meant South Fork was a winter wonderland full of snow and ice. 😍 Flow was 90 cfs and had been fairly low for the previous couple weeks. We ran the upper section and then hiked back up to our cars. There was knee- to thigh-deep postholing involved!

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20250625061451
comment: Fun day is SF. We bypassed most of the creek walk as in high flow + slippery it was annoying. We did a lot of the jumps at the start but did not do the slide. It should be depth checked.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20250701165507
comment: Late afternoon cool down. Alice Creek gauge was at 104cfs. Slides/jumps etc all go. Little pushy in some sections of rapids, but nice current to float in reduces the creek walking. Careful footwork needed on some of the middle section jumps. Franklin anchors are good. Didn't run lower.

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20250807002555
comment: Even on a rainy weekday you may have an audience at Franklin

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20250815044513
comment: Low water made for a lot of slow creek walking. Many of the slides and jumos were very shallow and had to be done carefully or skipped. The last rappel was still worth the price of admission!

title: Conditions:Snoqualmie River (South Fork)-20250825224015
comment: This creek is a very pretty creek walk with a dry last rappel into an amphitheater with 50 people watching. There is so much better stuff to do than this one.

title: Conditions:Star Creek-20260510043652
comment: While the flow wasn't impressive, Star Creek gets extra points for amazing scenery and gorgeous snow-clad mountain views. Definitely takes home the prize as the most scenic creek near Portland. Even the approach hike is highly photogenic. Star Creek caught us a little off guard with some bigger rappels than we were expecting... and we were not expecting an audience on the 180ft rappel near the end. If you demand beauty in your canyon adventures, this one's for you.

title: Conditions:Steel Creek-20230710023649
comment: No anchors or signs of previous passage were found, so this may have been a first descent. A couple of nice falls/sequences, but have to knock off a bunch of points for all the fallen trees (some of them ancient) in Steel Creek. Just about every drop had some evidence of vegetative destruction; would bump up to a three star if it ever blows out. There are much better canyon destinations in the Skykomish River Valley. Excess wood in some of the drops makes the canyon less safe than it should be.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20141014043557
comment: Very nice canyon. Did it right before the rain started, then went up to Mount Rainier for some idyllic stroll in the rain/snow.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20150720210316
comment: We descended the middle section. It's a scenic/great little section that can be done in a few hours and will be good for Class C beginners when the flow is low/moderate low.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20170726053625
comment: Gorgeous canyon!Water was great. We only descended the middle section. Flow appeared higher than we thought so we elected to rappel out of the water on Thunder Falls. Never did see the exit trail and bushwacked straight up a very steep hill to the road.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20180806035441
comment: Ran the middle section. Replced the webbing on the two bolted raps. Flow was low enough that you could rap in the water course if you wanted.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20200824190632
comment: Ran the middle section. All the anchors are in good shape. Sad to see the campsite has been decommissioned.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20200907013913
comment: Combined nearby Panther Creek in MRNP with the middle section of Summit for an all-around fun day. We dropped in just above the bridge on the south side. Small shallow jump just below the bridge. Below this, there's a short creek walk before action begins. Some great geology and huge fractured blocks with deep pools between. Found a dumped computer that was full of rocks. Finale is rappelling down Thunder Falls which is spectacular and photogenic. Just below the final downclimb, look left for a mossy gully and climb up the user trail (steeep!) to the canyon rim.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20200921225301
comment: Hangers at R2 and R3 have been almost completely flattened by seasonal flooding. I have not seen anything like it. Neither wiggled and we rappelled with no issues. We cut and packed out the old webbing for the bolt and knot chock at R3 DCR as both were in ragged condition and the quicklink was badly rusted. Next group that wants to do the DCR rap will need to bring materials to rebuild the anchor. The DCL rappel was fun and very photogenic.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20210913183909
comment: We set a ghost anchor and dropped in just upstream of the Middle Section, R2. We skipped R1 because it's a downclimb and wanted to eliminate the creek walking. We jumped R2 and rappelled R3. Thunder Falls is one of my favorite waterfalls. Love it!! This is a great place to camp when running creeks as well.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20220830185010
comment: Gorgeous route with great pay:play ratio. The group did two laps of the middle section from the campground. R2 bolts are still flattened, but seemingly sound (we replaced the webbing). If someone is passing nearby with a bolt kit, replacing R2 bolts would be a great (and not very time consuming) idea.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20220906201443
comment: Did the middle section. It's lovely. R2 is a jump, I jumped from DCR. Anchors and webbing all in good condition. The thunder falls rap is spectacular

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20230529182632
comment: Checked out the flow after camping nearby. Very high still. Photos attached.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20230724193321
comment: This is an excellent waterfall to rappel. My favorite way to run this creek is to walk down the road from the campground, exit the road on a trail just after the road bends to the left. Find a good tree and rappel line near the top of the elevation gain. I rappel into the canyon here. Swim around the corner and you will arrive at the 15 foot jump/rap. Descend that and you are at the top of Thunder Falls. The single strand of anchor webbing is bouncy, watch out it doesn't damage your rope. I'll replace it with a long quad strand when I return to reduce that bounce.

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20250709212642
comment: All anchors are still good

title: Conditions:Summit Creek Canyon-20250817141730
comment: Fun, easy day to start the Rondy. Just did middle section. Used DCL option on last rappel, bolt on R2 is pretty smashed, but feels solid. Might be good to explore a new bolt placement. Jump was in.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20180623023959
comment: First descent took us 8 hours car to car. Those running the canyon will likely complete it in a shorter amount of time. It would be nice it someone set a 2nd bolt for the stations.
