We dropped in around 2400' feet and the upper portion of the canyon were still covered in snow. The first part of the canyon is quite similar to Deadhorse Creek but with much lower flow.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20181210204805
comment: Icy conditions - the first rappel was completed covered in ice. Ice fall and slippery footage adds additional risk/hazards. Surprisingly, the ledge for the 2nd rappel was NOT iced over when we went. An anchor has been installed from the beginning of ledge to get to the main anchor for added protections to either setup another line or handline. The canyon walls are lined with icicles which makes for some beautiful scenery. Unfortunately my car was broken into at the trailhead. The window was smashed and the theives stole my purse. Be sure to hide all valuable (which were hidden) and know that there's a risk of break-in.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20190714010306
comment: What a stellar canyon! Excellent find, and thanks so much to those who bolted it. It's very well bolted. We did it with a 300ft rap line and a 200ft tag line. Probably could get away with just a 200ft rap line and 200 ft tag line, even on the first rap, especially if you just rapped off the first of the two bolts at the end of that rap. After that first rap, no need for anything over 200ft. Water was borderline warm in most parts. Felt fine even in the flow with just pants and a short sleeved shirt. On the way out, we just crossed the Taylor and bushwhacked a short ways to the Taylor River Trail. Highly recommend doing that at these low flows as opposed to bushwhacking on the left side of the Taylor from the bridge.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20200623075221
comment: A great day out. Flows were perfect. June is prime time for Tesseract. Snow at the drop-in point, but none in the canyon itself. Updated many anchors and backed up single point anchors. The traverse line at the bottom of R1 is now redundant and makes the transition between R1 and R2 much more efficient. Long day, took our time and had a few issues, resulting in a late exit in the dark, but got out safe and sound.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20200707075937
comment: A spectacular day on an amazing canyon. All of the anchors were in decent shape, 3 small logjams to climb over.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20200928162043
comment: A spectacular canyon! The first three rappels are just incredible and feels like you are dropping into the bowels of the earth. 4" of rainfall over the past 72 hours (after an extended dry period) brought Tesseract into perfect condition for a fall run. All anchors are in great condition.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20210524204319
comment: Entered below R3 due to big snow field in the box section. Will probably be a while before that melts out. ~6 hours car to car included some anchor work. Brief ice cold pools but most of the run is out of the water.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20210531040605
comment: Hike up could use some flagging once you get away from the road to better define a trail. Drop into R4 involves two dry canyon Repels to get down. Very Pretty but froze with no sunlight due to North facing canyon. Anchors were well place and no issues with pulls. Water was higher than the trip the week before, used a couple handlines with our 2 newbies.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20210801005159
comment: Really dig this canyon - just beautiful. All bolts are in good shape. Someone linked a bunch of the bolt stations - don't do that, folks! We unlinked the ones we used that shouldn't have been linked. Lots of loose rock - we knocked a ton off the first rap, then some came in from the canyon rim lower down (maybe goats?). Watch that loose rock! There are still some ice spires after R3 - so cool! They were sublimating and putting off cold fog, which was trippy.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20210925030823
comment: A great day in an awe inspiring canyon, even in very low flow. So low the creek entirely disappeared underground in multiple points leaving the canyon dry at the junction with the Taylor River. The loose rock on R1/R2 is a serious issue, people should not wait around at the immediate bottom of the rap, instead keep moving down to R3. We had some near misses as the ropes were pulled. I think some of the bolts *are* meant to be linked - e.g. R6 (if I remember correctly) which is on old moss covered rock, vertically aligned, with a smaller quicklink on the upper hanger, and a fullsized quicklink on the lower. See discussion regarding this layout in Mineral Creek[1] [1] https://www.facebook.com/groups/pnwcanyoning/posts/10159518885585789/?comment_id=10159534099730789&reply_comment_id=10159535970035789 We exited by crossing the Taylor River and picking up the trail on the other side - much quicker than our approach along the river south bank. Will definitely be back when there's more water around!

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20220704165755
comment: Clear of snow but there is more avy debris than last year. Anchors are intact. Great rainy day canyon.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20230930232844
comment: Sunny day after a week+ of rain. Canyon was flowing, but surprisingly not very high still. R3 was the highlight. All anchors are good (one of the two bolts at the R2 traverse line has taken a knock, but still functioning). Cold in the water & upper section, warmed up down below.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20240514061121
comment: Fun day! Lower than we had expected given the snow pack. It was moderate-low for tessy… we were hoping for sporty. The canyon is slimier than usual, but it’s still a beautiful canyon! And the slot is just as great as always.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20240620211312
comment: Time was significantly impacted by extra time taken to cross the river. This is my first time through the canyon and I don't have a reference point for staying on river right and avoiding the crossing, but we spent an extra hour finding a spot to cross both in/out of the canyon. We started late and ended late, but there were no significant obstacles or difficulties in the canyon. Last rappel was by headlamp. It was quite warm in neoprene on the first two raps and felt very good to get into the water. All in all, it was a fun day in the canyon.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20240622221819
comment: Crossing the river on approach was a little pushy, made a tension diagonal to assist some of the team. Otherwise a straightforward day in low flow.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20250526164557
comment: A great day out in Tessy. No avalanche snow in the narrows this season, all anchors appear in good condition minus one traverse line bolt at R2 that had previous damage. This bolt while bent, still seems solid in the rock. Flow was low but pleasant and the sunny warm weather made for a great day out.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20250602021145
comment: All anchors in great shape. Lower flow than we hoped for, but still fun. More greenery than we'd ever experienced. Made for a gorgeous day, but also downclimbs were more slippery/spicy than usual.

title: Conditions:Tesseract Canyon-20260301021949
comment: A nice relaxing day in Tessy - in February! The approach/canyon were entirely clear of snow. Flows were very low, but it was nice to be out in some water with good people, and the canyon is quite beautiful! We doubled up all of the anchors and replaced most quicklinks with ring hangers. We also replaced the previously smashed-up traverse line anchor. Abrasion mitigation is a requirement in many sections. The middle/lower sections of the canyon are ludicrously slippery currently. Important Note: Tesseract is a terrain trap, so if you are doing an early season run, you should be tracking avalanche conditions and snow levels. Today there was little/no snow above us, and canyon was clear of snow.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20190908042843
comment: Had visited the base of the falls by hiking several years previous, but this was likely a first descent. A nice pleasant little canyon outing.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20191215222628
comment: Good creek for high water. Easy to pick anchors to get as much flow as you want.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20201220054639
comment: Fun run. Mostly avoided flow as it was bonkers.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20211011020601
comment: This is a great creek. Short and sweet. Great pay to play if you have a shuttle. I'm trying to figure out how to update the beta to add the shuttle.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20220627034832
comment: Be aware: there's a log down across the road near its end, so you have to park and walk in an extra 0.5mi if you're taking a shuttle. Hike down the road, drop onto the ridge, follow it to its end, and head straight downhill to reach R1. Several core shots have been reported on the main slot -- so this is a good candidate for future bolting - and we may add a redirect further down the slot to keep the rope away from a rope-eating notch (hidden under the water?). Delightful day out. Rigged a guided rappel on R3 just for fun.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20220710034737
comment: Great day with awesome raps. Flow was fun, not too pushy but active. Rapped R2 2x by following goat trail back to top. Log has been removed from the road.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20230416153035
comment: Road up is covered by a snowdrift at the bridge before the turnoff for the lower parking. Additionally, there is a large tree down on the road up to the lower parking. Hiked in through lots of drifts but no snow in the creek. Fun day as always.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20240420235823
comment: Looks like the first trip this year. Three trees across the road about 2 miles from the drop in point. A chain saw would fix those pretty quickly. The road walk was easy. Anchors were in good shape except for the last drop. We replaced the webbing on the dry rappel there. Overall a great day at 3 corner!

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20240422145634
comment: We cut the trees and opened the road. R1 we dropped the main waterfall in the flow. There were many many loose rocks we cleaned off and kicked down along the way. Very crumbly basalt. R2 we went DCL at the bottom of the rappel in the rock formation. Right looked a little spicy as most of the flow was dumping in there. R3 we installed webbing on the boulder. Great day in 3CC!

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20240519001745
comment: Nice day out in 3 corners. R1 and R2 anchors good. R3 wet anchor from boulder in creek was already compromised despite new webbing being put in a few weeks ago - the little loop in the frost knot had a sizeable hole worn into it. Rather than waste another 10 feet of webbing that wont last until the next group we moved the quicklink to the main loop in the webbing and moved the knot to the backside of the boulder. Next group in should inspect this anchor carefully if using.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20240701030843
comment: Water is getting pretty low but it's still a fun creek. More opportunities to play around in all the grooves on R2. Replaced the anchor on the low flow side of R1 to stay in the flow. Also replaced the anchor around the boulder in the center of the creek on R3.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20250420214116
comment: Nice day out. Road to the top is clear and in great shape (there was still snow on the road mid April last year). Fun flow level, all rappels were quite manageable in the flow. Replaced the R3 webbing anchor at creek center, but I think this anchor would be better served with bolts on top of the boulder as the webbing just gets trashed in the flow. Looks like a few new large hunks of basalt came off the R3 cliff face this winter DCL but nothing in the rappel zone. R3 dry anchor looked to be in good shape tho we didn’t use it.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20250504041331
comment: Great trip. Bolted up R1 and R3. I'll update the beta.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20250616181637
comment: Great day in a beautiful creek. We ran laps on R2. New bolts on R3 are great!

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20251101225616
comment: Big water! Three Corner was raging in flood stage after a huge precip dump on the PNW. The creek is a different animal at this level with simple tasks like crossing the creek needing care & caution to avoid getting swept. Rappel lines are good from R1-alt, R2, and R3 (DCR). The creek center anchor on R3 was virtually underwater. Anchor R2 and R3 were damaged and needed replacement. At the bottom of R3 we crossed DCL going under the falls (awesome) and took a user trail along the DCL bank. Took the alt exit directly up the hill from the confluence which was a lot faster than staying in the creek. Bit of a user trail all the way out. Nice! Note for future teams: there are a bunch of old anchors around R1 that should probably be removed. 1) Looked like two anchors on trees DCL of the main drop on R1. 2) Anchor on old snag 50ft back on R1-alt.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20251229030746
comment: There is snow on the road. Lots of water has been in the creek. Log jams have moved around.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20251229041005
comment: Snow lvls currently above the creek. No snow in parking areas. Waterflow is really nice, enjoyable and not extreme. Water temps are cool but not cold, air temps in the early AM are chilly but by mid day is was really nice in creek. Anchors are solid and I flow raps are very manageable but still fun!

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20260627043938
comment: Beautiful canyon with three great rappels. Anchors were all solid. Love the smooth, rounded hangers. I considered putting some new webbing on R2, but it’s still solid.

title: Conditions:Three Corner Creek-20260628004055
comment: Fantastic day in 3C, despite the overcast skies. Intermittent rain and sun - but the creek and the raps are great. All anchors in great shape. Awesome ending to the 2026 Mazamas Canyon class.

title: Conditions:Tule Canyon (South Cascades)-20210528171622
comment: First descent took 6 hours so likely the canyon can be run in a shorter amount of time. Flow was pushy only if the rappel line was directly in a channeled section, otherwise, flow is probably considered moderate. Great canyon for early season and lots of rappels to make the trip worth while.

title: Conditions:Tule Canyon (South Cascades)-20210606065030
comment: Fun addition to the Dark Divide. The creek is pretty action-packed from start to finish. Lots of trees down in the drainage. Creek must see extremely high water from time to time based on the amount of vegetative destruction at the bottom of the canyon.

title: Conditions:Tule Canyon (South Cascades)-20220718154839
comment: Tule is a great easy little creek. R6 - We added a high anchor DCL. This splits the drop into R6a and R6b to avoid the sharp edge.

title: Conditions:Tule Canyon (South Cascades)-20240528171852
comment: Brrhhhh.... very cold! Nice flow levels however. Group of 9 going at a relaxed pace took 6hrs car-to-car. Blow-down 0.4 miles from parking added a short bit of road walking. Added a single bolt somewhere around the middle of the canyon to protect mid-sized rampy rappel. We re-rigged R6 to go from a tree DCL (photo in beta), as the previously added overhead R6b anchor was now quite marginal. The new anchor location allows both stages to be descended at once without significant edge problems. A spotter can be to be placed on the lip to help facilitate communications down the whole pitch. We cleared the debris we could from the jump pool. One log remains submerged running the length of the pool. It can still be jumped using accurate flat-jumping technique.

title: Conditions:Tule Canyon (South Cascades)-20240610223917
comment: Fun trip down a beautiful canyon. Impressive pile of logs and debris after the canyon narrows just before the exit. We removed the log from the J1 pool - watch for rock on jumper's left. Also recommend using upper anchor for first tier of R6 then transitioning to rope shown in picture to avoid a hard start and a collapsing bank.

title: Conditions:Tule Canyon (South Cascades)-20250525123159
comment: A scenic canyon with chill vibes at moderate-low flow. At least four raps can be bypassed with down climbing. J1 pool was too low to jump, but did present an opportunity for controlled slides.

title: Conditions:Tule Canyon (South Cascades)-20250827003309
comment: Decent flow at all falls but but nothing extreme. Really fun canyon and not super committing, just right for us as beginners!

title: Conditions:Twin Falls-20221007224001
comment: 2nd time running the route. The flow is not any lower than it was in mid-Aug. Donated a long quickdraw to the R2 redirect. Selecting "special challenges" due to the overall nature of the route (e.g. risky start to R2, every pool has a current which will happily snatch ropes & bags). However a fun evening trip in a beautiful canyon. Departed parking lot 3.30pm, back at 6.15pm.

title: Conditions:Twin Falls-20230823180610
comment: Fun sunny afternoon/evening run. Lots of jumping, and some ascending to lap them. The currents were very manageable but still present. Did the slide on the main falls - that's quite something! The start of R2 is still the crux move (I'm not sure the flow here ever gets below the Mod-High reference photos). Improved the R2 redirect with a proper stainless quicklink & extended webbing, and removed the corroded quick draw.

title: Conditions:Twin Falls-20240917121448
comment: The R2 redirect was still in place from last season although a little worn. Bringing a brush to clean the rock face near the redirect may prove to be helpful with the footing for all except the first rappeller. Our team opted for the dry line on R3. The waterflow link showed double the cfs today compared to 8/22/23 on all gauges except the SF SNOQUALMIE RIVER AB ALICE CREEK NR GARCIA, WA meter which was about the same as then - mid 20's cfs. Visually, it only looked a little higher today. Pay special attention to the final rappel rigging. The rope pull on this can be difficult due to the spacing between the bolts relative to the rock (see photo). This anchor should be adjusted possibly by simply adding another quicklink to just one of the bolts. Overall, this was a fun mid-afternoon canyon.

title: Conditions:Twin Falls-20240919052814
comment: The Alice Creek gauge was reading 25cfs. Time in canyon was 2 hours. None in our group could make it to the slide on R4, too slippery on the very mossy rock. We did 6 jumps from 5 to 20 feet. Would like to come in lower flow in order to do R3 in the flow and to have a better chance of getting over to the slide. Also would like to do mid-day when sun is hitting the canyon. Excellent anchor placement throughout, Kudos to the development team.

title: Conditions:Twin Falls-20240922235909
comment: Awesome day in Twin Falls! Time includes running the whole route twice! We skipped the redirect on R2 for the second lap (and we short rope + jumped R3 each time). Getting to R4 was a relatively trivial swim exit. This route is amazing! We would highly recommend running it twice for maximum fun. We were also unable to traverse to the slide despite a lot of effort... Very, very slippery.

title: Conditions:Twin Falls-20250725054113
comment: Fun afternoon jaunt through Twin Falls. Team found water features/hazards (ex. Pourovers) very manageable/the water was not very pushy, but the team consisted of generally strong swimmers so future teams should reflect on the own comfortability. Interestingly, the water was surprisingly warm. The webbing at R1 has some minor fraying so future teams could consider replacing depending on comfortability. The quick links at R2 were pretty rusted so again, opportunity for replacement. Overall, a very laid back and pleasant trip with deep pools, a few raps, a few jumps, and some swimming. None of us used the redirect on R2.

title: Conditions:Twin Falls-20250825202019
comment: First time through this canyon and it’s a stunner. A lot of water moving through this canyon, but very manageable with a little knowledge as long as you don’t put yourself in a bad situation. Thought we could avoid the crowds by going early on a Monday, but we had a surprisingly large audience from the bridge and viewpoint. Good reminder to keep a low profile, use whistles sparingly and try not to attract attention that would encourage casual attempts to access the top of the falls. The spinner bolt at R3 pulled out of the rock with only a little force and we found it was only secured in an unstable flake…yikes. Anchors could use some work.

title: Conditions:Twin Falls-20250924001323
comment: Thanks to whomever put the new rings / anchors in. It made the pulls buttery smooth. Pulled R4 from the bottom of R4 - as opposed to from across the water.

title: Conditions:Twin Falls-20260707210940
comment: Amazing day in Twin Falls! Bolts are quite bad at R2. Hangers are flat to the wall, one bolt is jiggling, the other is 1 cm exposed on one side. Both hangers are spinning (sleeved bolts, we did not take photos unfortunately). We did not bring equipment to fix it on this trip but it would be hard/impossible to fix without new holes and bolts. The redirect carabiner has been ripped out of the webbing. We re-tied the webbing shorter and did not leave hardware. The next group should bring webbing and a biner for the redirect. Bolts for R3 are good, webbing is maybe a bit bleached. Gauge was 58 CFS. Conditions were substantially higher than Moderate-High photos. Overall a great day!

title: Conditions:Wallanding Creek-20200919230925
comment: Dropped in around 2200 feet elevation. This put us just above the big drop. Most raps before the committing section are a nuisance but the canyon is delightful and now that the road is fixed, truck or suv recommended. All bolts are good and still are singles so the next team should plan to add more. Flow was fun.

title: Conditions:Wallanding Creek-20230605163720
comment: This is a great canyon! A 4WD with higher clearance is needed to clear one portion of the road otherwise the dirt road is in good condition. The total is not indicative of a "regular" canyoning trip as the team took extra time to find an approach route and set bolts. The water is surprisingly cold even with warmer air temperatures so a dry suit is recommended. There are some rappels where you will rappel directly in the full force of the water. With the current water level, the crux move requires a little stemming and dropping directly in the flow (with an overhang/minimal visibility) so canyoners should be comfortable with that move.

title: Conditions:Wallanding Creek-20230626021150
comment: Got to work for it a bit, but a great little canyon! Tip of the hat to the bolting teams -- very nice. Water levels seem to have diminished over the last several weeks. Get a good power-wash to the face on some of the rappels, but otherwise pretty friendly. Was all psyched up for a terrible thrash out and was pleasantly surprised at how nice it was. Jumped on the old logging grade to get back up to the trucks.

title: Conditions:Wallanding Creek-20230807212745
comment: Lovely pretty canyon with beautiful waterfalls, some fun sporty flow, a couple of short jumps. Got to see a salamander at the very end. It is not done very often, but it definitely deserves more love. Anchors were in great condition. We got to do some fun problem solving in the lower section, which have some short drops but no fixed anchor material. The approach sucks a bit, beware of hornets. Exit went pretty smoothly

title: Conditions:Wallanding Creek-20230914052123
comment: We did an "after" season trip on our way to Gifford-Pinchot. Low water and late day made for extremely underwhelming conditions. Although I still argue it's an overrated canyon. Rap lengths seem to be overstated by a bit, but we were hurrying and didn't measure. I wasn't overly looking to jump, but jumps didn't seem as plentiful and/or as obvious as they are made to sound. Jumper beware. Descent is a bit unpleasant, but fortunately short.

title: Conditions:Wallanding Creek-20230929000314
comment: Canyon flow was very low, but still R1 2 and 3 were fun. Water levels this low would make those 2 technical jumps even more precise... Pretty sure the exit is no longer marked as mentions in a previous trip report. The trek down to the creek is no too bad but the exit I chose was quite steep and littered with downed trees. The creek walking is abundant but def has some great little areas to cool off in or just appreciate. YT vid link shared on the main page.

title: Conditions:Wallanding Creek-20240611053528
comment: Canyon was fun, although flowing higher than expected. Made dry line rappels on the first and crux rappels, and had to protect for the downclimb. Flow seemed to be diverted more heavily DCR around chockstone rappel. Log jam may have built up more on DCL side. Also created a hydraulic in the pool that pulled back toward falls on DCR side in pool at the bottom.

title: Conditions:Wallanding Creek-20240707193924
comment: Nice day out before flow is too low to be fun. We noticed several small siphons; in higher flow special care should be taken as the siphons were located directly below where the anchors are placed. From the top of R3, the rappel looked scarier than it turned out to be. However, special care should still be taken and canyoneers should be confident rappelling in flow at moderate-high or higher levels (avoiding the water was not possible at the moderate-high flow).

title: Conditions:Washout Waterslide-20260405075556
comment: Opening this canyon was fun, a very easy run with some quick fire fun rappels. Should be combined with Galena next door for a full day of cold water splash zone action. The last 3 drops had to be done on one rap due to the lack of natural anchors present at the final 70ft waterfall known as washout falls. This canyon would be much more attractive to do if bolts were installed for the last rap and then this could be run with a 70m rope. Def best to run this in winter or spring when flow is mod high or high, it can be run quite safely.

title: Conditions:Whitehorse Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20210719035231
comment: Hiked the Putvin Trail and dropped into Whitehorse at the toe of the avalanche field. Our time may be a bit longer as every drop had to be assessed (we found all of two anchors from previous parties). Toward the bottom we were getting a bit tired, but it would be fun to set more anchors to run the flow on the lower drops, especially the final section below Lower Whitehorse Falls. Next time need to look at the feasibility of dropping into the creek from the decommissioned road. This would shorten up the descent and allow more time to enjoy the best falls towards the bottom of the creek. Flow was strong/pushy in the narrow sections. Most drops, however, had the option to rappel down the edge of the flow so you could avoid getting blasted. We're probably a little early for Whitehorse; it'll probably be more fun in a couple weeks as water levels continue to drop. There are a of couple deep pools, but no real swimmers on the creek. No serious water hazards were encountered. Bonus note: Just east of the bridge where the Hamma Hamma Rd crosses Whitehorse is a campsite with an enormous old cedar stump. It's ideally placed for canyon teams exiting Whitehorse after a long day.

title: Conditions:Whitehorse Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20220815015539
comment: Returning to Whitehorse this year, we scouted out the lower entrance just off the old logging road. This is a much better approach (it drops you in just above R9) and improves the creek's pay-to-play ratio significantly. Last time we were pretty tired by the time we got to the big rappels. We spent some time establishing anchors and installed two bolt stations on R15 and R16 (both outside the wilderness boundary) allowing you to run the flow. Other groups will probably be faster. This canyon has everything you could want: great access, good water, and fun rappels. If camping in the area, try to grab Big Stump Camp (a dispersed campsite marked on the map). This allows you to exit the creek and have a leisurely stroll down the road and right into your campsite. Outstanding!

title: Conditions:Whitehorse Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20250428231454
comment: Ran the main section. Flow was low but still a great time with a couple beginners. Replaced webbing at several anchors and ended up linking R11 and R12.

title: Conditions:Whitehorse Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20250428233541
comment: Ran the main. Flow was low but still great day with a couple beginners. Replaced webbing at several anchors and ended up linking R11 and R12.

title: Conditions:Whitehorse Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20250721215719
comment: Whitehorse full run is an amazing, action-packed creek with almost all rappels coming back-to-back in quick succession. Expect a high-energy descent with very little downtime. The current beta was significantly off—many rappel lengths were understated, which led to some logistical challenges. We took notes but didn’t get everything dialed, so expect discrepancies. Because we based our gear off the existing beta, we didn’t bring many long ropes and ended up short-roped several times, waiting at stations for the long rope to catch up. Most anchors needed to be rebuilt. We used approximately 100 feet of webbing and were starting to worry about running out. Where possible, we ghosted anchors to conserve material. Water wasn’t particularly cold. Air temps reached around 70°F. I ran it comfortably in a 4/3 Farmer John with a rain jacket. While most nearby creeks are running on the moderate-low side, Whitehorse was definitely moderate-high—stronger flow than expected given the conditions in the area. Bring extra webbing, I updated the beta closer to what we found the raps to be. Killer creek with great flow, but be prepared.

title: Conditions:Whitehorse Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20250803020308
comment: Ran the full canyon as outlined in rope wiki. Flow has tailed off now as the snow has mostly melted from up high on the mountains. Still a great run and a fun time in the water!

title: Conditions:Whitehorse Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20260708034321
comment: Fun canyon. Anchors are ok but some needs thicker webbing. Canyon could do with an extra bolt or webbing anchor to avoid a dry rappel dcr. We just did the lower section.

title: Conditions:Whitehorse Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20260708034426
comment: Enjoyable canyon. Not quite as good as Fulton. Starts out a little boring, gets better towards the end. Webbing is in decent condition at all of the anchors. There is a paper wasp nest DCL at the downclimb before the R15 anchor, so treat that with respect.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20200223022118
comment: Followed the road up, then took a brushy track south across the lesser trib and dropped into Wieberg. Rappelled another 20ft drop before reaching the rappels in the beta. Nice early season creek. Second rappel needs another bolt to complete the station. The last rappel was super fun. Bit surprising a falls of this height doesn't have a trail leading to it.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20210412052229
comment: Sunny day, 25F at the start. Cut out an anchor station on last rap due to it being tied to a dead looking alder.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20220227024352
comment: Great day at Weiberg. Ice was a bit much and the temps were higher than forecasted so we needed to kick out a few large pieces to make it safe. Rigged R3 from a tree more DCR than the current one for the big drop. Ice was most significant on the last drop and the height at which it could fall led to the new anchor. Old anchor was left alone.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20220410231858
comment: Good flow. Reset anchor DCL on R3 (due to a partial cut or hungry animal). All other anchors are in good shape.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20220606204115
comment: Super fun creek, flow was perfect!

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20230604030551
comment: Fun Creek with great rappels. Bushwacked in to R2. Great day! All anchors in good shape.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20240415021328
comment: The creek was great. Sunny day, warm weather, no better place to be. We didn't damage any ropes. There is a sharp spot at the top of R4 kinda where you go over the edge. We creeped and didn't have any problems.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20250331180850
comment: Super fun day! Flow was great! The rappel lines are setup perfectly to either be in or out of the flow. We lowered the rope after each person to prevent rope damage. We removed the webbing from the bolts and would like to keep them unlinked. Pull R3 from across the creek where you have a line of sight on the anchor.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20250408204251
comment: the flow dropped a lot in the last 5 days. Still no rope damage. I think the core shots in the beta were from bouncing on rappel.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20251215123728
comment: Great day in the creek.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20251224030500
comment: All anchors are solid, sharp rock and tough rope pulls make this one interesting. Current water lvls are impressive with snow in the area melting. The final rappel is stunning and eye opening! In lower flow this would not be worth the effort in my opinion! Massive core shot occurred on R3 that happend during the rope retrieval.

title: Conditions:Wieberg Creek-20260308002905
comment: Nice leisurely day in Wieberg with good flow and a fun team. We did all four raps and replaced the webbing on R1 and placed it higher on the tree. No core shots to report, though we did creep the rope at R4. Unfortunately there is a lot of marking tape along the lower creek that seems to indicate there may be logging in the near future?

title: Conditions:Wolf Creek (Olympic Peninsula)
comment: No evidence of previous descent in canyon, nor can I find any info of the canyon online. So probable first descent. Ghosted canyon due to being unfamilar with ONP policy on leaving webbing.
